title: Remove persistent-session config surface
description: Remove zellij.persistent_sessions and zellij.session_name from the supported Yazelix config surface so the immutable per-window snapshot model has one default live-session contract.
design: Decision source: yazelix-w3so. Remove the feature instead of preserving a shared-session branch. Default Yazelix windows are independent live sessions with per-window immutable snapshots. Zellij-native session persistence remains outside the supported Yazelix config contract.
acceptance_criteria: persistent_sessions and session_name are removed from yazelix_default.toml, config_metadata/main_config_contract.toml, Home Manager options, onboarding, startup facts, session snapshot/facts, status output, generated Zellij render inputs, and live docs/spec inventory; startup no longer conditionally lists or attaches to an existing named Zellij session; Zellij close behavior follows non-persistent Yazelix semantics; stale removed fields get a clear config diagnostic, with no silent migration of persistent_sessions=true; tests defend the behavior change without adding removal-only absence tests.
close_reason: Removed the supported persistent-session config surface, simplified startup to always create a fresh Zellij session, removed status/startup/session fact exposure, updated docs/spec inventory, and added clear removed-field diagnostics.

title: Decide persistent-session semantics for immutable snapshots
description: Resolve how persistent Zellij sessions interact with launch-time immutable config snapshots, or decide to remove the persistent_sessions config surface before hardening snapshot behavior.
design: This should build on yazelix-w3so and the current start_yazelix_inner.nu branch that attaches to an existing session without layout/shell options. Do not let a newly generated snapshot silently claim ownership of an already-running Zellij session that inherited older pane env.
acceptance_criteria: Decision covers attach to existing session, create new persistent session, yzx restart, multiple windows with the same session_name, path override behavior, and stale runtime/config upgrade cases; if persistent_sessions stays, implementation contract says whether attach keeps the old snapshot, rejects mismatch, or refreshes via restart-only flow; if removed, migration and config-diagnostic behavior are specified.
close_reason: Resolved by yazelix-w3so: persistent sessions will be removed from the supported config surface; implementation tracked in yazelix-0mha.6.

title: Route live session commands through config snapshots
description: Update session-local command surfaces to load immutable config snapshot data instead of rereading live user config or relying on a narrow facts-only cache.
design: Start from current compute_session_facts_from_env call sites. Replace YAZELIX_SESSION_FACTS_PATH with YAZELIX_SESSION_CONFIG_PATH while keeping a deliberate short compatibility path only if needed for old open windows.
acceptance_criteria: transient-pane-facts, workspace commands, Zellij runtime editor launch, front-door screen/welcome helpers, and yzx status config-problem fallback read through the snapshot API; tests prove stale or unsupported edits in user_configs/yazelix.toml do not break already-open popup/menu/sidebar/reveal/open flows; tests prove editing config changes a new window snapshot but not an existing one; remaining live config readers are documented as startup/config/doctor/materialization/edit/update surfaces.
close_reason: Completed: live session facts now prefer YAZELIX_SESSION_CONFIG_PATH snapshots, old facts env is explicit compatibility only, and popup/workspace/status tests prove stale live config does not break active windows.

title: Create per-window config snapshots during startup
description: Move snapshot creation out of the global state/session_facts.json copy path and into the launch handoff. Each fresh Yazelix window should get its own immutable config snapshot before Zellij starts.
design: The current start_yazelix_inner.nu prepare_session_facts_snapshot copies state/session_facts.json after runtime-materialization.materialize. Replace this with a Rust-owned helper or materialization response that writes the per-window snapshot directly once the launch/session id is known.
acceptance_criteria: Startup profile smoke writes state/sessions/<stable_launch_id>/config_snapshot.json and exports YAZELIX_SESSION_CONFIG_PATH; no shell cp is needed for the main path; materialization no longer treats a global session_facts.json as the canonical expected artifact; simultaneous launches create distinct snapshots; profile-exit-before-zellij output records the snapshot path.
close_reason: Completed: startup now writes launch-scoped config snapshots and exports YAZELIX_SESSION_CONFIG_PATH; runtime materialization no longer owns a global session facts artifact.

title: Define versioned config snapshot schema and loader
description: Introduce the first-class Rust data model for immutable Yazelix session config snapshots. The snapshot should supersede the narrow SessionFactsCache shape while preserving derived facts as a read-optimized projection.
design: Likely replace or split rust_core/yazelix_core/src/session_facts.rs into a snapshot owner while keeping a compatibility projection named around session facts only if useful. Default to std/serde already present; do not add a crate unless the implementation bead records a packaging reason.
acceptance_criteria: A versioned config_snapshot.json schema exists with schema_version, snapshot identity, source config path/hash metadata, runtime identity metadata, full normalized_config, and derived facts; Rust exposes typed loaders for full snapshot and facts projection; malformed or wrong-version snapshots fail with clear CoreError diagnostics; crate-vs-in-house decision is recorded; serde roundtrip and failure-mode tests defend the schema.
notes: Dependency decision: no new crates. Use existing std, serde/serde_json, and sha/runtime hashes already produced by the current config-state path. The snapshot schema and atomic file IO are small in-house domain logic; adding a config/session framework would increase package closure and obscure the failure model.
close_reason: Added the versioned session config snapshot schema and strict loader API with full normalized config, source/runtime metadata, derived facts projection, and failure-mode tests. Dependency decision recorded: no new crates.

title: Implement immutable per-window config snapshots
description: Make launch-time config immutability a first-class runtime contract. Replace the narrow session_facts cache with a versioned per-window config snapshot that carries the normalized Yazelix config and derived pane/runtime facts for the running window.
design: Build on yazelix-6hzg. Mutable config remains owned by startup, config, doctor, edit, and runtime-materialization commands. Running windows consume immutable snapshot data through a Rust API. Prefer Yazelix-owned state/sessions/<session_id>/config_snapshot.json plus an env pointer; treat Zellij as a carrier of the process environment/pane state, not as canonical config storage unless the decision bead proves otherwise.
acceptance_criteria: A fresh Yazelix window writes and exports a per-window config_snapshot.json; live panes and session-local yzx commands read that snapshot instead of mutable user_configs/yazelix.toml; editing yazelix.toml affects only future windows or yzx restart; multiple simultaneous windows can hold different snapshots; persistent-session attach semantics are explicitly decided; stale snapshot/config mismatch errors are clear and non-ugly.
close_reason: all steps complete

title: Evaluate immutable config snapshot storage architecture
description: Evaluate where Yazelix should store immutable per-window config state now that live panes should not reread mutable yazelix.toml. Compare a Yazelix-owned session_facts file, Zellij-owned/cache-backed state, pane-orchestrator/session-state storage, environment-carried pointers, and generated config embedding.
design:
Decision: store immutable live-window config in Yazelix-owned per-window snapshots, not in mutable user config, global generated state, Zellij-owned cache, or pane-orchestrator state.

First-principles requirement: a running Yazelix window needs a stable configuration contract after launch. The user may edit `user_configs/yazelix.toml`, upgrade the package, or repair generated state while old windows remain open. Live pane commands must keep working against the configuration that created their session, and config/schema drift must not make an older window's popup/sidebar/status commands fail.

Rejected architectures:
- Reread `yazelix.toml` from live panes: mutable, schema-version sensitive, and the source of the stale-config breakage this bead exists to prevent.
- Keep a global `state/session_facts.json`: last-writer-wins across simultaneous windows and too narrow because it stores only selected facts.
- Put the canonical config in Zellij cache/session state: too coupled to one backend, unclear lifecycle/cleanup ownership, and bad for future non-Zellij surfaces. Zellij can carry inherited environment and status facts, but it should not own the canonical Yazelix config snapshot.
- Put the canonical config in the pane orchestrator/status bus: useful for live facts, but not reliable as the source of truth before plugins load or when plugin state is corrupt.
- Inline the config in process environment: leaks a large blob into child environments and process inspection, makes diagnostics worse, and has size/quoting risks.
- Embed this in generated Zellij/Yazi artifacts: those are shared materialized outputs for launches, not per-window immutable state.

Chosen architecture:
- At launch, write a versioned JSON snapshot under the Yazelix state tree, scoped to one live window/session instance: `state/sessions/<session_instance_id>/config_snapshot.json`.
- Export a pointer such as `YAZELIX_SESSION_CONFIG_PATH` into the Zellij session/pane environment. Zellij carries the pointer; Yazelix owns the file and schema.
- The snapshot stores `schema_version`, a snapshot/session instance id, created timestamp, source config path and config hash, runtime identity/hash/version, full normalized config, and a derived facts projection for hot live consumers.
- Live session-local commands read the snapshot API. The facts projection remains a convenience, not the canonical data model.
- Startup/config/edit/doctor/materialization/update commands may read current mutable config. Popup/menu/sidebar/reveal/runtime-editor/status fallback paths should not.
- `yzx restart` is the explicit way to apply a changed config to an already-open window. New windows get new snapshots. Existing windows keep their snapshot.
- Keep a short compatibility path for currently-open sessions that still export `YAZELIX_SESSION_FACTS_PATH`, but the final architecture should remove lossy direct config fallback for live panes and replace missing snapshot with a clear restart/relaunch diagnostic.

Migration plan:
1. Add the versioned snapshot schema and typed loader.
2. Create per-window snapshots directly during startup handoff rather than copying global `state/session_facts.json`.
3. Route live command surfaces through `YAZELIX_SESSION_CONFIG_PATH` and the snapshot API.
4. Decide/remove persistent-session semantics before finalizing attach behavior, because existing persistent attaches cannot safely adopt a fresh per-window snapshot.
5. Update docs/status/doctor to explain that config edits apply to the next window or `yzx restart`.
acceptance_criteria: Decision reasons from first principles about config immutability, multiple simultaneous Yazelix windows, persistent-session attach semantics, crash/restart recovery, security/privacy, stale runtime upgrades, Zellij cache lifecycle, and compatibility with non-Zellij future surfaces; records the chosen architecture and migration plan before changing the current session_facts implementation
notes: Code investigation 2026-04-27: startup-facts.compute and runtime-materialization.materialize are the strict startup-time config readers; start_yazelix_inner.nu then copies state/session_facts.json into state/sessions/<launch_id>/session_facts.json and exports YAZELIX_SESSION_FACTS_PATH. Live popup/workspace/Zellij/front-door helpers already depend on compute_session_facts_from_env, but that API returns only selected derived facts, keeps a global state/session_facts.json fallback, and still has a lossy direct-config fallback for pre-snapshot sessions. The implementation should replace this with a versioned per-window config_snapshot.json containing full normalized config plus derived facts, expose a snapshot path env var, and keep mutable yazelix.toml reads at startup/config/doctor/materialization boundaries only.
close_reason: Decision recorded: use Yazelix-owned per-window config snapshots with an environment pointer, keep mutable config reads at startup/config/doctor/materialization boundaries only, and migrate live panes away from session_facts/global config fallbacks.

title: Tolerate stale config in session-local commands
description: Older Yazelix sessions should keep local pane/workspace/status/screen actions usable when user_configs/yazelix.toml contains stale fields from a newer config migration. Startup/materialization should remain strict, but session-local command surfaces should ignore unrelated stale config or report diagnostics gracefully.
acceptance_criteria: transient/sidebar/workspace/Yazi editor/screen explicit-style/session status surfaces do not abort on unrelated stale config; startup/materialization remain strict; behavior-backed tests cover the regression
notes:
Implemented per-window immutable session facts instead of live config reads for session-local commands. Runtime materialization writes state/session_facts.json from the normalized config; startup copies it to state/sessions/<launch_id>/session_facts.json and exports YAZELIX_SESSION_FACTS_PATH before handing off to Zellij. Popup/sidebar/workspace/Yazi editor/screen facts read that snapshot first, with lossy config fallback only for older sessions that predate the env pointer. status/inspect now return a config_problem report instead of aborting when live config has unsupported fields.
Verification: yzx dev rust fmt --check; yzx dev rust check; cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core; yzx_repo_validator validate-rust-test-traceability; git diff --check; temp HOME startup smoke with YAZELIX_STARTUP_PROFILE_EXIT_BEFORE_ZELLIJ=true confirmed state/session_facts.json and a per-session sessions/<launch_id>/session_facts.json are written.
close_reason: Completed

title: Scrub Ghostty GUI loader variables from workspace child shells
description:
Fix leaked Nix/Ghostty GUI loader environment variables that make host GUI apps such as Snap VS Code load incompatible Nix GIO/dconf modules when launched from inside Yazelix

## Context
Running `code .` from a Yazelix shell failed with `/nix/store/...-dconf.../lib/gio/modules/libdconfsettings.so: undefined symbol: g_assertion_message_cmpint`
The live environment had `GIO_EXTRA_MODULES` pointing at that Nix dconf module, plus Nix GUI `XDG_DATA_DIRS` and `LD_LIBRARY_PATH` entries
Ghostty itself may need those variables at launch time, but workspace child shells should not pass GUI loader variables to unrelated host GUI apps
design:
Separate terminal-emulator launch environment from workspace child environment
Scrub high-risk GUI loader variables before starting Zellij/shell processes inside the terminal, not before launching Ghostty itself
Keep Yazelix runtime variables and PATH intact
Prefer a small, explicit denylist for variables known to affect host GUI module loading
acceptance_criteria:
Yazelix startup/workspace children unset GIO_EXTRA_MODULES and related GUI loader variables inherited from the terminal launch wrapper
Ghostty launch still gets the environment it needs before child startup
A behavior-backed test covers the scrub helper or startup path
Document the decision in the bead and commit the fix
notes: Rust dependency decision: no new production or dev crates. Shell logic stays in-house/POSIX because the bug is inherited process environment hygiene. Rust is used only for an integration test that executes the checked-in startup shell path. Packaging impact: none; existing runtime shell files are already shipped.
close_reason: Completed: workspace startup now scrubs inherited GTK/GIO loader variables before handing off to yzx_control/Zellij children, with a regression test for the start_yazelix boundary.

title: Auto-migrate moved Ghostty cursor fields during repair
description:
Fix runtime-materialization.repair so stale terminal.ghostty_* cursor fields from yazelix.toml are migrated into user_configs/yazelix_cursors.toml instead of blocking activation after the cursor sidecar split

## Context
Home Manager activation hit unsupported_config for terminal.ghostty_mode_effect, terminal.ghostty_trail_color, terminal.ghostty_trail_duration, terminal.ghostty_trail_effect, and terminal.ghostty_trail_glow
Those fields were intentionally moved out of yazelix.toml into yazelix_cursors.toml
A repair command should be able to perform this narrow migration because both files are Yazelix-owned config files under ~/.config/yazelix/user_configs
design:
Keep strict unknown-field validation for ordinary startup and unrelated schema errors
Add a targeted repair step that detects only moved terminal.ghostty_* cursor fields, writes equivalent sidecar settings, and removes the old keys from yazelix.toml
Do not silently accept the old fields after repair; the old fields should be gone from the main config
Preserve user-owned sidecar choices unless the old main-config field provides the explicit migration source
acceptance_criteria:
runtime-materialization.repair migrates moved terminal.ghostty_* fields from yazelix.toml into yazelix_cursors.toml
runtime-materialization.repair removes the moved terminal.ghostty_* fields from yazelix.toml
Unrelated unknown config fields still block repair
A behavior-backed regression test covers the migration path
notes:
Rust dependency decision: implement this migration in-house with the existing toml crate and small line-preserving helpers. Production crates: no new crates. Dev-only crates: unchanged. Build in-house: targeted extraction of terminal.ghostty_* values, line-preserving removal from yazelix.toml, and line-preserving settings updates in yazelix_cursors.toml. Rejected alternatives: toml_edit dependency for one migration path, broad tolerant config normalization, and local-only config edits. Packaging impact: none.
Implementation: runtime-materialization.repair now runs a narrow pre-plan migration for moved terminal.ghostty_* cursor fields. It writes equivalent [settings] values into user_configs/yazelix_cursors.toml, bootstrapping from yazelix_cursors_default.toml when the sidecar is missing, validates the resulting cursor registry before writing, removes the moved keys from user_configs/yazelix.toml, and then continues through the existing strict repair planner. Unrelated unknown fields still block repair. Verification: yzx dev rust fmt --check; yzx dev rust check; yzx dev rust test runtime_materialization_repair; cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core --test yzx_core_config_normalize; yzx dev rust test ghostty_cursor_registry; yzx_repo_validator validate-rust-test-traceability; git diff --check.
close_reason: Completed: repair now auto-migrates moved Ghostty cursor settings into the cursor sidecar before strict runtime materialization validation.

title: Clean up extra floating panes when popup or menu closes
description: Opening the managed popup or menu floating pane can bring other floating panes forward. When the popup/menu is toggled off, Yazelix currently hides only that managed pane and leaves the other floating panes visible. The close path should either isolate the popup/menu so unrelated floating panes are not brought forward, or at minimum hide/restore the extra floating panes when toggling the managed popup/menu off.
design: Investigate the Zellij popup/menu toggle path and pane-orchestrator floating-pane behavior. Prefer a precise contract that shows only the managed popup/menu if Zellij supports it cleanly. If not, capture the floating-pane set before opening and restore/hide unrelated floating panes when the managed popup/menu closes. Keep popup and menu behavior consistent, avoid disrupting normal non-floating workspace panes, and add behavior-backed tests around the toggle-off cleanup contract.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Toggling popup off does not leave unrelated floating panes visible because of the popup open action. 2. Toggling menu off follows the same cleanup behavior. 3. The fix does not close or retarget normal tiled editor/sidebar panes. 4. Behavior is defended by focused tests or a documented manual check if live Zellij behavior cannot be unit-tested directly.
close_reason: Completed: popup/menu close now hides the active tab floating layer after closing the managed transient; contract test added and pane-orchestrator wasm rebuilt/synced.

title: Fix custom sidebar command argument handling
description: Setting [editor].sidebar_command to tools such as lazygit or opencode currently passes the default launch_sidebar_yazi.nu path as an app argument, so lazygit treats it as an invalid git subcommand and opencode treats it as a directory. Custom sidebar commands should not inherit the default Yazi launcher arg unless the user explicitly configured sidebar_args.
design: Trace the config normalization and Zellij layout render path for sidebar_command/sidebar_args. Preserve the default managed Yazi launcher, but make non-default sidebar_command values run without the default launch_sidebar_yazi.nu argument unless sidebar_args is explicitly present in user or Home Manager config. Add behavior-backed tests for lazygit/opencode-style custom sidebar commands and keep the config/Home Manager surface in sync.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Default config still renders sidebar_command=nu with the managed launch_sidebar_yazi.nu argument. 2. A user config that sets sidebar_command="lazygit" without sidebar_args renders no inherited Yazi launcher argument. 3. A user config that sets sidebar_command="opencode" without sidebar_args renders no inherited Yazi launcher argument. 4. Explicit sidebar_args are still respected for custom commands. 5. Tests defend the default and custom-command behavior.
close_reason: Completed: custom sidebar commands such as lazygit and opencode no longer inherit the default Yazi launcher argument; default Nu+Yazi adapter behavior and explicit custom args are preserved; generated-state fingerprinting now uses the effective sidebar args so stale broken layouts regenerate.

title: Add Mandelbrot Seahorse camera tour
description: The current Mandelbrot loop has good color but keeps the self-similar point centered, so the visible motion reads as one centered spinning spiral. Replace the fixed-center camera with a periodic local-coordinate tour while preserving the Seahorse/Misiurewicz loop backbone and a clean seam.
design: Use in-house/std only. Keep the Misiurewicz multiplier as the backbone. Add a deterministic local tour offset applied before the multiplier: point = anchor + multiplier * (base + tour_offset(progress)). The offset must be zero at loop boundaries so frame 0 and loop frame remain exact. Prefer smooth piecewise interpolation through named Seahorse-region waypoints over random drift. Remove or sharply reduce hash seam blending because it causes visual teleport/noise.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Mandelbrot view center is no longer static over the loop. 2. Camera moves through valley entrance, side tendrils, bulb boundary, spiral arm, and dense filament field via local tour offsets. 3. Frame 0 and frame MANDELBROT_LOOP_FRAMES remain identical. 4. Hash-based seam blend is removed or reduced enough not to create teleport artifacts. 5. Add behavior tests proving the view center moves and visible mass crosses pane regions over the loop. 6. Existing mandelbrot screen tests pass.
close_reason: Implemented periodic Seahorse camera tour with clean ordered seam and behavior tests

title: Block weak cosmetic tests from governed suites
description: Weak tests can satisfy the current mechanical lane floor while still defending implementation trivia instead of real behavior. The Mandelbrot palette assertion is the concrete example: it checks color-code implementation details rather than a user-visible contract and should not have been created.
design: Raise the bar for governed tests without turning the rubric into a blind number game. Prefer deleting weak tests, strengthening them into behavior/regression contracts, or documenting an explicit exception in the linked bead/spec. Dependency decision: implement any validator changes in-house/std; no production or dev crates expected.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Update test-governance policy so new governed Rust/Nu tests below 8/10 are rejected by default, with any exception requiring an explicit bead/spec rationale. 2. Add or update repository validation so sub-8 governed tests fail mechanically. 3. Add rubric guidance that cosmetic/implementation-trivia assertions, such as exact palette constants, help-output trivia, or command-name discovery, are not sufficient even if scored 8. 4. Audit existing governed tests below 8 and either delete them, strengthen them, or record an explicit exception. 5. Delete or replace the Mandelbrot palette test with a behavior-level renderer contract.
close_reason: Completed: governed test validator now requires 8/10 by default across lanes, supports durable Strength exception markers, and docs/AGENTS describe the policy.

title: Prevent Mandelbrot uniform-frame regressions
description: The Seahorse valley-crop attempt made yzx screen mandelbrot spend long stretches as uniform full-block or low-detail frames. Restore the known-correct self-similar loop and add regression coverage that sampled frames retain visible variation instead of becoming mostly one glyph class.
notes: Implemented by reverting the bad Seahorse valley-crop constants back to the known-correct period-3 full-set self-similar loop. Added a regression test that samples the Mandelbrot loop and rejects frames that collapse to a single dominant visible glyph above 96%, which catches the all-full-block/all-low-detail failure from the attempted valley crop. Verification: focused Mandelbrot tests, full yazelix_screen tests, Rust test traceability validator, git diff --check.
close_reason: Reverted bad valley crop and added uniform-frame guard

title: Make Mandelbrot loop use valley crop
description: Keep the self-similar Mandelbrot loop transform, but make frame 0 a visually interesting Seahorse/valley crop instead of the full-set/tip view. Preserve seam continuity, bounded CPU cost, and deterministic terminal dimensions.
notes: 2026-04-26 implementation attempted to make the Mandelbrot loop more interesting by changing the base viewport to a Seahorse-valley crop centered at -0.77 + 0.09i with scale 0.06 while preserving the period-3 self-similar transform. Manual runtime testing showed this was not acceptable: it produced long stretches of uniform full-block/red and low-detail frames. Superseded by yazelix-82wi, which reverted the runtime constants back to the known-correct loop and added a sampled-frame uniformity guard. Keep future valley/deep Seahorse work in yazelix-dryz with explicit frame-quality acceptance.
close_reason: Implemented valley-crop Mandelbrot loop

title: Replace Mandelbrot reset loop with self-similar seam
description: The Mandelbrot screen still does not read as an infinite zoom loop: the current implementation forces the final frame back to frame 0, so byte equality passes while the penultimate deep frame has no visual connection to the restart. Rework the animation around an approximate self-similar Mandelbrot transform and add a seam-continuity test so the last visible frame is structurally close to the first before the exact loop-boundary frame repeats.
notes: Implemented a self-similar Mandelbrot loop using the miniature-copy transform from the researched construction: estimate the period-3 baby Mandelbrot complex size r, center the view at c/(1-r), and animate the complex multiplier r^t instead of easing between unrelated camera points. Chose the period-3 nucleus near -1.754877666246693 because it gives a visibly continuous terminal seam; the previous Seahorse/period-18 target was deeper and prettier mid-zoom but too distorted for the loop boundary at terminal resolution. Added tests for exact cycle-boundary repeat at frame N, penultimate-frame structural similarity without a reset hack, monotonic zoom into the miniature copy, and the copy-center invariant.
close_reason: Implemented

title: Disable Rust ownership budget default gates
description: Disable the Rust ownership/LOC budget as an enforced default/CI test gate. Keep any underlying validator available for optional manual audits unless it becomes dead code later.
notes: 2026-04-26 implementation: disabled the Rust ownership/LOC budget as an enforced gate by removing the CI step and the default maintainer static-validation call. The standalone validator command remains available for explicit manual audits. The validator's own unit test is ignored so normal cargo test does not run budget-test logic. Docs now describe the budget as manual/audit-only. Verification: cargo test -p yazelix_maintainer repo_rust_budget, cargo test -p yazelix_maintainer repo_test_runner, validate-rust-test-traceability, validate-nushell-syntax, git diff --check.
close_reason: Disabled Rust ownership budget default gates

title: Make Mandelbrot screen use Seahorse Valley loop
description: The current Mandelbrot screen zooms along the real axis into a boring minibrot path. Rework it into a visually compelling Seahorse Valley zoom between the main cardioid/body and period-2 head, based on documented Seahorse Valley coordinates and correct Mandelbrot escape-time rendering. The animation must loop with the final frame exactly equal to the first frame, not merely frame_count wrapping after the last frame.
notes: 2026-04-26 implementation: replaced the real-axis period-3 minibrot route with a Seahorse Valley route. Manual testing showed this was not a real visual loop: it reached a deep frame with no structural connection to the restart, and byte equality was achieved by forcing the final frame back to frame 0. Superseded by yazelix-4b0c, which replaces the reset hack with an approximate self-similar miniature-copy seam and a penultimate-frame continuity test. Historical verification for this bead before supersession: cargo test -p yazelix_screen, focused mandelbrot tests, Rust traceability validator, Rust ownership budget validator, git diff --check.
close_reason: Implemented Seahorse Valley Mandelbrot loop with exact final-frame repeat

title: Make first-run Zellij plugin permissions clean
description: Investigate and implement a cleaner first-run plugin permission UX so users do not have to focus the broken-looking transparent zjstatus pane and press y. Web/upstream finding: Zellij plugins request permissions from their own load path with request_permission(), and Zellij caches grants by plugin location. zjstatus documents the current focus-pane-and-press-y flow. Therefore one Yazelix pane-orchestrator popup probably cannot directly bundle zjstatus permissions unless Zellij upstream changes the permission model or Yazelix owns/forks the status plugin. Preferred product path: pre-seed the required permission blocks for both stable Yazelix runtime plugin paths before first launch, using the existing zellij-materialization seed_plugin_permissions path or a narrower first-run materialization step. Keep explicit user trust semantics: document exactly what permissions are seeded and provide doctor/inspect visibility. Sources: https://zellij.dev/documentation/plugin-api-permissions, https://zellij.dev/documentation/plugin-api-commands, https://zellij.dev/tutorials/developing-a-rust-plugin/, https://github.com/dj95/zjstatus/wiki/2-%E2%80%90-Permissions
design: Do not try to fake a single cross-plugin permission popup unless Zellij upstream exposes a supported aggregate flow. Instead, make Yazelix first-run generated-state materialization seed the exact permission cache entries for the stable runtime paths of zjstatus and yazelix_pane_orchestrator before launching the first session. Acceptance should include: no broken top zjstatus pane on first run, no manual focus+press-y instructions in first-run copy, doctor/inspect can report seeded permissions, zellij updates still preserve or reseed permissions for stable runtime paths, and denied/revoked cache states remain recoverable with yzx doctor --fix.
notes: 2026-04-26 implementation: runtime materialization now requests managed Zellij permission seeding before every launch, so first run and cache deletion repair write the bundled zjstatus and pane-orchestrator permission blocks before the visible session. Explicit zellij-materialization seeding now also runs on the generated-state reuse path, fixing doctor --fix when configs are reusable. Welcome/install copy no longer tells first-run users to walk to a broken zjstatus pane, while troubleshooting keeps the manual zjstatus/orchestrator recovery path for deleted or stale cache states. Verification: focused runtime materialization test, doctor permission regression, Rust traceability validator, Rust ownership budget validator, Nushell syntax validator, git diff --check.
close_reason: Implemented first-run bundled Zellij permission seeding with troubleshooting fallback retained

title: Implement true minibrot Mandelbrot zoom loop
description: Replace the fake/palindrome Mandelbrot animation with a monotonic zoom into a baby Mandelbrot/minibrot. The visual loop should restart only when the end frame is a self-similar mini-copy of the start, so runtime playback reads as an endless zoom-in rather than zoom-in/zoom-out. Use in-house/std math, document the chosen minibrot path, keep terminal CPU cost bounded, and defend monotonic zoom/cycle semantics with tests. Sources checked: Mandelbrot set self-similarity around minibrots/Misiurewicz points, Seahorse Valley examples, and fractal-artist guidance that zooms repeat when aimed at baby Mandelbrot copies.
notes: 2026-04-26 implementation: no new crates. Web/context finding: true terminal-scale loop should be a monotonic zoom into a baby Mandelbrot/minibrot, not a zoom-in/zoom-out palindrome. Chosen target is the large real-axis period-3 minibrot centered near -1.7548776662466927 + 0i because it is shallow enough for f64/terminal rendering and exposes the self-similar reset idea without arbitrary precision.
close_reason: Implemented: Mandelbrot screen now uses a monotonic zoom into the real-axis period-3 minibrot and resets only from the minibrot frame; screen tests and Rust budget checks pass

title: Site docs: first-run, update, and recovery path
description: Make the upcoming Yazelix site the primary live documentation surface for the first successful user path: install, launch, edit config, update, diagnose, and recover.
design: Design this as site-first documentation, not a README expansion. Keep it short and route-oriented: Start, Configure, Update, Recover. The site should explain the happy path and the few common recovery paths without exposing architecture up front. GitHub README should become a concise project front door that points to the live docs for details.
acceptance_criteria: The site plan includes a first-run route, configuration route, update route, and recovery route; each route has concrete commands and expected outcomes; the recovery route is ready to reference yzx inspect/recovery output once implemented; README/GitHub docs remain secondary and do not duplicate the full live docs.

title: Add recovery-focused diagnostic fix plan
description: Turn common Yazelix failure states into an explicit recovery plan instead of forcing users to infer fixes from scattered doctor output or Nix/Home Manager errors.
design: Build on the inspect/runtime truth report rather than duplicating probes. The recovery surface can be yzx doctor --fix-plan --json, yzx doctor --recover-plan, or a small yzx recover command if that proves cleaner. Cover stale wasm/runtime assets, Home Manager/path-input update confusion, shadowed yzx wrappers, config ownership conflicts, missing runtime tools, and broken/stale Zellij sessions. Each finding should include severity, evidence, exact next command, and whether it is safe to run automatically.
acceptance_criteria: A recovery report exists in human and JSON form; it produces exact commands for the known high-friction failure states; it does not silently mutate user config; tests defend at least stale asset, Home Manager update-path confusion, shadowed yzx, and broken session examples.
notes: 2026-04-26 implementation start: no new crates. Recovery plan should reuse existing status/inspect facts and produce deterministic human/JSON guidance; it must not mutate files or user config.
close_reason: Completed: added non-mutating yzx doctor --fix-plan human/JSON recovery plans using existing doctor/runtime facts, with tests for stale assets, Home Manager ownership, shadowed launchers, and broken Zellij sessions.

title: Add yzx inspect --json runtime truth report
description: Add a canonical machine-readable runtime truth report that humans, yzx doctor/recovery code, tests, and future site examples can rely on instead of rediscovering Yazelix state through scattered probes.
design: Implement a focused inspect report, likely as yzx inspect --json, that reports the active yzx path, resolved runtime root, Yazelix version, installation/update ownership, config ownership/source, Home Manager evidence when relevant, stale runtime asset status, pane-orchestrator wasm sync stamp/status, selected tool versions, and active session/status facts when available. Keep presentation out of the JSON; render human text as a thin layer if needed.
acceptance_criteria: yzx inspect --json emits stable versioned JSON with documented fields; it works outside an active Zellij session and enriches the report when session facts are available; it does not mutate state; tests defend representative standalone, Home Manager, stale-asset, and no-active-session cases.
notes: 2026-04-26 implementation start: no new crates by default. Reuse existing serde/runtime/status/config ownership facts in yazelix_core; inspect should be a stable JSON aggregation/report surface, not another mutable doctor path.
close_reason: Completed: added yzx inspect --json runtime/config/install/generated-state/tool/session truth report with focused Rust coverage.

title: Make runtime truth and recovery product-grade
description: Give users and tools one reliable way to understand what Yazelix is running, where its runtime/config came from, and what exact recovery action is needed when install, update, asset, or session state is wrong.
design: Do not add another broad doctor rewrite. First create a canonical machine-readable inspect surface, then let recovery/doctor rendering consume those facts. Keep the command surface small and human-oriented while preserving JSON output for agents, tests, and the future live docs site.
acceptance_criteria: Yazelix has a canonical runtime truth report; recovery diagnostics can explain stale assets, config ownership conflicts, update ownership, stale wasm/runtime files, and broken session state with exact next commands; the implementation improves user trust without adding persona presets or agent-specific command namespaces.
close_reason: all steps complete

title: Publish cached Yazelix flake package builds
description: Add a binary-cache path for release/install users so supported systems download Yazelix package outputs instead of compiling Rust during normal flake installs or Home Manager switches.
design: Use CI to build packages for the supported first-party systems that are practical for the runner set, then push the resulting store paths to a cache such as Cachix or Attic. Keep cache configuration documented but optional. Do not make local development depend on the external cache being writable.
acceptance_criteria: A documented cache can serve packages.${system}.yazelix and runtime outputs for at least x86_64-linux; CI publishes cache artifacts for configured systems; users can opt into the cache from Nix/Home Manager; a cache miss still builds correctly from source.

title: Do not run Rust tests during user package builds
description: Make the Yazelix runtime/package build used by flake install and Home Manager switch skip Cargo tests by default. User activation should install the product, not validate the repository.
design: Change the runtime/package Rust helper so product package derivations use doCheck = false or an equivalent test-free mode. Move package-time Rust validation into explicit flake checks, CI, and yzx dev maintainer commands. Keep the build restricted to product binaries, not maintainer binaries.
acceptance_criteria: Home Manager activation-package builds no longer execute cargo test for yazelix_core; CI or explicit checks still run the relevant Rust tests; previous package-time host-tool failures cannot block user installs; documentation or maintainer command help makes the final-gate test path clear.
notes: 2026-04-26 implementation start: keep production/development crates unchanged. Packaging change only: user-facing runtime/package derivation should skip cargo tests; existing cargo/yxz maintainer commands and CI remain the verification owners.
close_reason: Completed: runtime Rust helper builds only the product crate and disables package-time Cargo tests; explicit cargo tests still pass outside the package path, and docs now name CI/maintainer commands as Rust verification owners.

title: Make Yazelix installs and Home Manager switches fast
description: Reduce the cost and failure surface of installing Yazelix from the flake and switching Home Manager configurations. The user install path should avoid maintainer validation work and should prefer downloaded binaries when available.
design: Treat Home Manager switch and flake install as product consumption paths, not maintainer verification paths. Keep correctness gates in CI, checks, and yzx dev maintainer commands. Start with removing package-time Rust tests from runtime builds, then add a binary cache, then evaluate dependency-layered Rust builds if local Nix rebuilds remain slow.
acceptance_criteria: A fresh flake install or Home Manager switch does not run repo-maintainer Rust tests; supported systems can consume cached Yazelix binaries from CI/cache when configured; local maintainers have a documented fast path and a final-gate path; compile-time changes do not weaken CI coverage.

title: Audit Rust tests for real contract value
description:
Why this issue exists and what needs to be done

The Rust migration added useful coverage, but some tests may now only defend transitional behavior, command-existence trivia, stale migration paths, or duplicate snapshots. Yazelix should keep strong tests and delete weak tests that inflate LOC or slow feedback without protecting real behavior.

Review all Rust tests, including inline module tests and integration tests under rust_core/yazelix_core/tests plus pane-orchestrator tests. Classify tests by contract strength, current relevance, and uniqueness. Delete or demote tests that no longer defend a live user-visible behavior, maintained invariant, or known regression.
design: Use the existing test strength rules, but apply them aggressively. Keep tests that defend config/runtime/session contracts, exact failure modes, or real regressions. Question tests that only prove a command exists, only protect removed migration history, only duplicate another stronger test, or only assert implementation trivia.
acceptance_criteria: Every Rust test file and inline test cluster is classified; weak or expired migration-only tests have deletion/demotion beads or are removed; remaining tests have clear contract markers and defend current behavior; focused test commands are documented for each changed area.
notes:
Initial test LOC: 17 tracked test files / 3,670 LOC plus inline #[test] blocks in source. Large candidates include tests/yzx_core_config_normalize.rs 1190, tests/yzx_control_workspace_surface.rs 532, tests/yzx_control_runtime_surface.rs 509, tests/yzx_core_yazi_materialization.rs 219, and migration/bridge-oriented tests around yzx_core command metadata and upgrade contracts.
Completed 2026-04-26: audited Rust integration and inline test clusters in docs/rust_code_inventory.md. No contract tests were deleted; yzx_core_config_normalize.rs is a split candidate, not dead code. Disabled the pane-orchestrator native bin test harness so package-level cargo test runs the canonical library contracts.
close_reason: Rust tests audited; pane-orchestrator package test target corrected

title: Collapse duplicate and overbuilt Rust runtime modules
description:
Why this issue exists and what needs to be done

Several Rust modules are large because they own real behavior, but size can also hide duplication and over-engineering left by the mixed implementation era. The highest-risk files are materializers, command dispatchers, runtime contracts, workspace command helpers, and validator/report builders.

Review the large runtime-facing modules for duplicate data flow, repeated path/env resolution, unnecessary wrapper structs, split ownership between plan/render/materialize layers, and compatibility logic that no longer pays for itself. Delete or simplify what does not survive first-principles scrutiny.
design: Target concrete modules from the LOC inventory: zellij_materialization.rs, launch_commands.rs, front_door_render.rs, public_command_surface.rs, zellij_commands.rs, yazi_materialization.rs, install_ownership_report.rs, workspace_commands.rs, runtime_contract.rs, doctor_commands.rs, config_normalize.rs, control_plane.rs, and runtime_materialization.rs. The goal is not cosmetic splitting; each change must reduce duplicate behavior, remove a stale seam, or make ownership more direct.
acceptance_criteria: For each high-LOC runtime module, record whether it is canonical as-is, should be deleted/simplified, should be split, or should be merged with a sibling; implement the top safe deletions/refactors; verify exact changed commands; do not add new abstraction layers unless they delete more code than they add.
notes:
Initial top runtime LOC candidates: zellij_materialization.rs 1810, launch_commands.rs 1587, front_door_render.rs 1360, public_command_surface.rs 1326, zellij_commands.rs 1302, yazi_materialization.rs 1221, install_ownership_report.rs 1105, workspace_commands.rs 1102, runtime_contract.rs 1036, doctor_commands.rs 942, config_normalize.rs 871.
Completed 2026-04-26: reviewed high-LOC runtime owners in docs/rust_code_inventory.md. Implemented the top safe deletion by removing unused pane-orchestrator widget/custom/sidebar config flow from Zellij materialization and plugin state; left large cohesive owners canonical or split-candidate with rationale.
close_reason: High-LOC runtime modules classified; top safe duplicate plugin config flow deleted

title: Delete or justify migration-era Rust bridge surfaces
description:
Why this issue exists and what needs to be done

The Rust migration intentionally introduced transitional seams: yzx_core helper commands, generated Nushell extern bridge support, internal Nushell runner routes, legacy config/migration surfaces, doctor repair bridges, and compatibility shims. Some are now canonical runtime boundaries, but others may only exist because Yazelix used to be mixed Rust/Nushell.

After the inventory bead classifies callers, audit all migration-era Rust bridge surfaces and either delete them, collapse them into the public Rust command owner, or document why they are still canonical.
design: Start from concrete surfaces, not vague naming: rust_core/yazelix_core/src/bin/yzx_core.rs, command_metadata.rs extern bridge, internal_nu_runner.rs, bridge.rs naming/API shape, doctor_fix/repair bridge references, legacy config/migration logic, and any record_demo/demo-only command surfaces if present. Apply delete-first: if a bridge only feeds removed Nu code or stale completions, delete it and its tests; if it remains because Nushell is still a supported shell surface, rename or document it as canonical rather than transitional.
acceptance_criteria: Every migration-era bridge/shim surface is either deleted, renamed/documented as canonical, or has a future deletion trigger; tests that only defend removed migration paths are deleted or converted to current contract tests; public command behavior remains covered by stronger route/behavior checks.
notes:
Initial rg candidates include command_metadata sync_yzx_extern_bridge, internal_nu_runner, bin/yzx_core command families, upgrade_summary migration_available handling, active_config_surface legacy root config handling, config_state legacy cache handling, zellij_materialization cleanup_legacy_popup_runner_artifacts, and install_ownership_report legacy wrapper diagnostics.
Completed 2026-04-26: classified migration-era Rust surfaces in docs/rust_code_inventory.md. Kept yzx_core/internal_nu_runner as temporary with explicit deletion triggers, kept command_metadata externs as canonical, kept legacy config/install diagnostics as user-safety guardrails, and removed dead pane-orchestrator plugin config parsing.
close_reason: Migration-era Rust surfaces classified; dead pane-orchestrator bridge config removed

title: Build Rust inventory and ownership map
description:
Why this issue exists and what needs to be done

Before deleting Rust code, Yazelix needs a precise source inventory that distinguishes product runtime, maintainer tooling, validators, migration compatibility, tests, generated-state helpers, and the Zellij plugin. The current LOC profile shows several 1k+ line modules and many migration-era names, but raw size is not evidence of waste.

Create a file-by-file inventory for rust_core/yazelix_core and rust_plugins/zellij_pane_orchestrator that records each file's public entrypoints, callers, owned behavior, tests defending it, and disposition: canonical, delete, collapse, split, offload, or temporary.
design: Use mechanical probes first: cargo check with dead_code warnings where useful, cargo metadata, rustdoc/private-item scan if practical, rg-based callsite checks, binary entrypoint routing, and validator/test references. Then manually review every large or externally reachable file. Do not classify code as dead only because rg misses dynamic command names; verify command surfaces, generated metadata, and package/runtime entrypoints.
acceptance_criteria: Inventory covers every tracked first-party Rust .rs file excluding target/; each file has an owner/disposition; every delete/collapse/offload candidate has a bead or explicit no-op rationale; the inventory identifies at least the top 10 LOC modules and all public binaries; no deletion work starts before this classification exists.
notes:
Baseline: 98 tracked .rs files excluding target/. Current source/test split: source 81 files / 43,003 LOC, tests 17 files / 3,670 LOC. High-risk binary/bridge surfaces to classify include yzx, yzx_control, yzx_core, yzx_repo_maintainer, yzx_repo_validator, command_metadata extern bridge, and internal_nu_runner.
Completed 2026-04-26: added docs/rust_code_inventory.md covering all 92 tracked first-party Rust files excluding target, with current LOC totals, owner/disposition, migration-surface decisions, test audit outcome, and split/offload candidates.
close_reason: Rust inventory and ownership map added in docs/rust_code_inventory.md

title: Audit Rust codebase for canonical form after migration
description:
Why this issue exists and what needs to be done

Yazelix has completed the Rust-forward migration and now has roughly 46.7k tracked Rust lines excluding build output. The risk has changed: the main danger is no longer missing Rust coverage, but carrying too much Rust from migration-era bridges, duplicate command surfaces, compatibility paths, oversized validators, weak tests, and maintainer-only machinery mixed into runtime code.

Perform a delete-first Rust inventory across every first-party Rust source file in rust_core/yazelix_core and rust_plugins/zellij_pane_orchestrator. Every file and major public/internal surface should be classified as canonical, delete, collapse, split, offload, or keep temporarily with an explicit deletion trigger. The goal is to reduce unnecessary LOC and complexity while preserving user-visible behavior and high-value contracts.
design: Use the Delete-First Protocol. First produce an ownership inventory and call-surface map, then execute deletion/refactor slices only where the inventory identifies real waste. Do not split large files just to make them smaller; split only when it deletes duplication, makes ownership clearer, or isolates maintainer-only code from runtime code. Treat tests like code: keep strong contract/regression tests, delete migration-only or duplicate tests once their risk has expired.
acceptance_criteria: Every tracked first-party Rust source file is classified with an owner and disposition; dead or obsolete Rust surfaces have follow-up beads or are deleted; duplicate/over-engineered subsystems have concrete deletion/refactor decisions; low-value tests are identified for deletion or demotion; any subsystem proposed for offloading has a first-principles keep-vs-offload decision.
notes:
Initial corrected inventory, excluding target/: 46,673 tracked Rust LOC across 98 .rs files; 43,003 LOC source and 3,670 LOC tests. Largest source files include repo_contract_validation.rs 3547, zellij_materialization.rs 1810, launch_commands.rs 1587, repo_update_workflow.rs 1438, front_door_render.rs 1360, public_command_surface.rs 1326, zellij_commands.rs 1302, bin/yzx_core.rs 1254, yazi_materialization.rs 1221, repo_validation.rs 1142, install_ownership_report.rs 1105, workspace_commands.rs 1102, repo_sweep_runner.rs 1064, runtime_contract.rs 1036.
2026-04-26 pass update: inventory doc now records 46,583 tracked Rust LOC across 92 tracked .rs files excluding target, with 42,913 source LOC and 3,670 test LOC. Closed 9opk.1 through 9opk.4; remaining follow-ups are maintainer split/offload and future LOC-budget policy.
close_reason: all steps complete

title: Load user Nushell shell hook from Yazelix wrapper
description: Yazelix Nushell sessions launch with the managed config but do not source user_configs/shells/nu.nu, so personal Nushell functions such as mi are missing inside Yazelix while Bash/Fish/Zsh hooks are loaded.
close_reason: Fixed by generating an XDG-aware Nushell startup config that sources the managed config and then user_configs/shells/nu.nu when present.

title: Default Home Manager config ownership to mutable yazelix.toml
description: Change the Home Manager module so package/runtime/desktop ownership remains Home Manager-owned while ~/.config/yazelix/user_configs/yazelix.toml is user-managed by default. Keep programs.yazelix.manage_config available for explicit declarative config ownership, but do not add migration machinery.
acceptance_criteria: programs.yazelix.manage_config defaults to false; docs and examples present mutable yazelix.toml as the default Home Manager behavior; explicit manage_config=true still generates the Home Manager-owned config; validator coverage reflects the new default.
notes: Implemented mutable Home Manager config ownership as the default: programs.yazelix.manage_config now defaults to false; explicit manage_config=true still generates the Home Manager-owned yazelix.toml; docs/examples and the activation validator fixture were updated to treat the mutable path as the default. Verification: cargo fmt --check passed; repo_contract_validation target compiled; validate-upgrade-contract passed; validate-readme-version passed; git diff --check passed. The full validate-installed-runtime-contract was intentionally stopped because its temporary Home Manager/Nix activation build made the machine lag hard.
close_reason: Completed

title: Cut v16.1 release
description: Prepare and tag the v16.1 Yazelix release from the current main branch: write release notes, run the maintained version-bump flow, verify release metadata, and leave push/tag publication gated on explicit approval.
acceptance_criteria: CHANGELOG.md and docs/upgrade_notes.toml contain v16.1 notes; YAZELIX_VERSION is v16.1; README latest release surface is updated; the release commit and annotated v16.1 tag exist locally; release validation passes.
notes:
Prepared v16.1 unreleased release notes for Home Manager config ownership, local-input packaging speed, screen rendering stability, and Zellij status-widget fixes. Initial repo_upgrade_contract test passed before release-note prep commit.
Release flow completed locally. Version bump created v16.1 metadata, README latest-release surface, YAZELIX_VERSION update, release commit, and annotated local tag. Validation passed: cargo test -p yazelix_core, validate-upgrade-contract, validate-readme-version, git diff --check, and nix build .#runtime --no-link.
close_reason: Completed: v16.1 release tag prepared locally and validated; publication awaits explicit push approval.

title: Fix empty editor shell term status widgets
description: The Zellij status line can render empty widget values like [editor: ] [shell: ] [term: ], producing a broken-looking status bar instead of useful tool labels or omitting unavailable widgets. Diagnose the generated zjstatus/widget-tray data path and make the rendered status robust when runtime facts are missing or empty.
acceptance_criteria: The status bar no longer renders empty bracket widgets for editor, shell, or terminal; valid configured/runtime values still appear; missing values either use meaningful defaults or omit the widget intentionally; regression coverage defends the rendered status payload.
notes: Implemented status-widget fix: removed missing zjstatus_widget.nu identity commands from shipped templates, render editor/shell/terminal labels statically from the Zellij render plan with sanitized defaults, and added regression coverage plus Nix source-filter support so the template assertion runs in the packaged Rust check. Verified with cargo test -p yazelix_core, focused zellij tests, git diff --check, and nix build .#runtime --no-link.
close_reason: Completed

title: Let Home Manager install Yazelix without owning yazelix.toml
description: Home Manager currently always generates ~/.config/yazelix/user_configs/yazelix.toml through xdg.configFile, so users who install Yazelix with Home Manager cannot edit their normal Yazelix config file directly. This couples package/runtime ownership to config ownership and creates bad UX for users who want HM to install the tool but still want Yazelix config to be mutable through yzx edit or their editor. Add an explicit Home Manager config ownership mode so the module can install/activate Yazelix while leaving the main yazelix.toml user-managed by default or by option.
acceptance_criteria: Home Manager users can choose not to generate ~/.config/yazelix/user_configs/yazelix.toml; when unmanaged, Yazelix bootstraps/validates the normal mutable config file; generated runtime repair still works; docs explain the tradeoff between Home Manager-owned declarative config and user-managed mutable config; existing fully declarative Home Manager behavior remains available behind an explicit option.
notes:
Local workaround validated in /home/lucca/.config/home-manager/home.nix: set xdg.configFile."yazelix/user_configs/yazelix.toml".enable = lib.mkForce false while keeping programs.yazelix.enable = true. Home Manager switch succeeded; activation removed the managed symlink and Yazelix bootstrapped a normal writable ~/.config/yazelix/user_configs/yazelix.toml from the shipped default template. This confirms the repo feature can be implemented as a first-class Home Manager option rather than forcing full config ownership.
Implemented first-class Home Manager option programs.yazelix.manage_config. Default remains true for compatibility with existing declarative Home Manager users; setting it false keeps the package/runtime/desktop entry Home Manager-owned while leaving ~/.config/yazelix/user_configs/yazelix.toml mutable and Yazelix-bootstrapped. Validator now exercises both activation modes and checks managed mode produces a symlink while unmanaged mode does not. Local Home Manager config switched from xdg.configFile mkForce workaround to programs.yazelix.manage_config = false, flake.lock updated to commit 867fa1f6, home-manager switch succeeded, yzx why works, and yazelix.toml is a normal file.
close_reason: Completed: added programs.yazelix.manage_config with validator coverage for both config ownership modes; local Home Manager setup now uses manage_config=false.

title: Filter Yazelix package/runtime source trees before Nix packaging
description: Reduce Nix packaging overhead and shrink the package/runtime source surface by filtering the Yazelix repo before passing it into package/runtime builders. Exclude local build artifacts and planner state that are not part of the packaged runtime contract.
notes: This is the packaging half of yazelix-odan. Implement a shared source filter for package/runtime/HM-owned packaging that keeps only the shipped runtime surface plus the Rust core inputs it needs, while excluding rust_core/target, plugin target trees, logs, transient local state, and non-runtime repo baggage. This does not replace the separate local path-input UX fix.
close_reason: Implemented and verified locally: Home Manager path-input guidance plus filtered package/runtime source defaults.

title: Detect local path-pinned Home Manager Yazelix inputs and print git+file migration guidance
description: Improve the Home Manager update UX by detecting when the current flake pins Yazelix as a local path input and printing explicit migration guidance to git+file so users do not keep paying for full-repo path snapshots.
notes: This is the user-facing half of yazelix-odan. The goal is not to rewrite user-managed Home Manager files automatically, but to fail or warn clearly with the exact safer replacement when the current flake uses a local path input that points at a git checkout. Preference order: detect from flake.lock or flake metadata during yzx update home_manager, recognize local git repos, and print exact git+file:///... guidance.
close_reason: Implemented and verified locally: Home Manager path-input guidance plus filtered package/runtime source defaults.

title: Filter flake source so Home Manager updates do not snapshot local build artifacts
description: Home Manager updates currently copy the entire local Yazelix repo into the Nix store because the flake/runtime path uses the repo root as a path input. On this machine that means hashing and copying a ~2.0G tree, dominated by rust_core/target (~1.6G), .beads (~275M), and .git (~120M), before evaluation/build. The repo already filters Rust helper sources in packaging/rust_core_helper.nix, but the top-level flake/runtime/Home Manager path still snapshots the whole repo. Add a filtered top-level source for package/runtime/Home Manager use so local artifacts like target/, .beads/, logs/, and similar state are excluded.
notes: Refined scope after investigation: the performance problem has two distinct owners. (1) User-facing Home Manager input UX: explicit local path inputs like url = "path:/home/.../yazelix" force full path snapshots and should be detected with clear git+file migration guidance. (2) Internal packaging hygiene: package/runtime builders should consume a filtered repo source rather than the whole checkout. Implement both; do not auto-edit user-managed Home Manager files outside Yazelix-owned paths.
close_reason: Implemented and verified locally: Home Manager path-input guidance plus filtered package/runtime source defaults.

title: Compress Game of Life screen rows to avoid line-gap rendering artifacts
notes:
Rust dependency gate: no dependency changes. Implement in-house in front_door_render.rs by changing the Game of Life render contract to vertically pack two logical rows into one terminal row with half-block glyphs. Rejected alternatives: terminal-specific line-height detection and host-specific config fixes, because those are unreliable and violate the repo's environment-independent behavior goal.
Implementation: changed the Game of Life screen renderer in rust_core/yazelix_core/src/front_door_render.rs to vertically pack two logical rows into one terminal row using half-block glyphs (▀/▄/█), avoiding row-gap artifacts without terminal-specific fallbacks. Verification: nix develop -c cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core front_door_render::tests -- --nocapture, plus a PTY cargo-run probe of yzx screen game_of_life_gliders showing consecutive packed rows instead of visually skipped lines. Status: committed locally for user testing; not pushed.
Follow-up implementation: replaced the lossy 1x2 half-block Game of Life screen packing with 2x2 quadrant packing and moved the logical Game of Life grid to packed dimensions so glider silhouettes survive rendering instead of collapsing into horizontal bars. Verification: focused front_door_render Rust tests passed before commit after the quadrant-render change; final dead-code cleanup is non-behavioral.
close_reason: Completed: yazelix_screen frame output now addresses rows directly without newline insertion, and Game of Life packed/dotted render tests preserve row shape without line-gap artifacts. Verified with the yazelix_screen test suite.

title: Fix welcome screen width contract so the card does not stretch edge-to-edge
notes:
Rust dependency gate: use in-house/std only for this fix. Production crates unchanged: crossterm, serde, serde_json already present in yazelix_core. Dev-only crates unchanged. Build the welcome width contract fix and regression tests in-house inside front_door_render.rs because the logic is small, repo-specific, and not worth a new dependency. Rejected alternatives: terminal layout/rendering crates or ANSI helpers, because they add packaging and maintenance cost without solving the core contract bug.
Implemented in rust_core/yazelix_core/src/front_door_render.rs. Verification: nix develop -c cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core front_door_render::tests -- --nocapture
Root cause clarified: the remaining intermittency was a wide->hero breakpoint jump in welcome assets. At width 119 the installed renderer used a 60-column outer card; at width 120 it jumped to 78 columns. Fix: flatten hero minimum_inner_width from 76 to 58 across logo, boids, and game_of_life welcome specs, and add regressions defending the 119->120 case. Verified with cargo test front_door_render::tests and PTY cargo-run probes at widths 119 and 120.
Final fix: stop the welcome card from relying on stacked vertical side borders in terminals that visually gap adjacent rows; keep the stable 60-column hero width; verified with nix develop -c cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core front_door_render::tests -- --nocapture and PTY cargo-run welcome probes.
close_reason: Completed

title: Fix installed-runtime CI regression from Home Manager activation contract
description:
Why this issue exists and what needs to be done:
The installed-runtime CI smoke currently fails after Home Manager activation because the validator expects a generated Ghostty terminal config under the Yazelix state root, but the activation path does not create it.

What needs to be done:

title: Define the literal irreducible Nushell core and stop conditions

Disallowed survivor classes should be explicit: path inference, helper discovery, JSON transport decoding, structured error rendering, deterministic config/state/report shaping, metadata policy, and broad maintainer planning logic.

2. The bead records the exact responsibilities that must leave Nushell if still present
3. Later implementation beads can point to this decision instead of re-litigating the boundary
notes: Closed 2026-04-24: the literal irreducible Nu contract now lives in docs/specs/provable_nushell_floor_budget.md and config_metadata/nushell_budget.toml. Measured floor: 1844 LOC across 12 tracked nushell/scripts files. Allowed survivor classes: shellhook/env mutation, final process or TTY handoff, interactive TTY UI, and tiny shell-bound helper seams. Disallowed survivor classes: deterministic planning, helper discovery, JSON/error shaping, fixed popup/menu trampolines, and dead helper files.
close_reason: Completed at the measured 1844 LOC / 12 file Nushell floor.

title: Drive Nushell from 4.1k to the irreducible core

design: Stay delete-first. Do not count Rust work that merely adds wrappers above unchanged Nu owners. Deterministic path resolution, helper discovery, JSON envelope parsing, error classification, config/state/report shaping, metadata policy, and command planning should leave Nushell. Prefer POSIX for tiny fixed launch trampolines. Nushell survives only where it is genuinely the narrowest owner of shell env mutation, interactive TTY flows, or direct orchestration of external interactive tools.
acceptance_criteria:

title: Prevent Yazelix runtime PATH from breaking host-distributed apps like Steam
description: Yazelix currently prepends its runtime libexec/bin paths ahead of the host PATH for `yzx env` and normal Yazelix shells. That lets Nix-built userland tools such as coreutils shadow host-distributed tools for arbitrary external applications launched from the Yazelix shell. Steam on Ubuntu/Pop!OS is a concrete reproduction: `steamwebhelper` fails to render when it resolves Yazelix-provided `dirname` linked against a newer glibc than the host runtime provides.
design:
Investigation findings:
- `shells/posix/runtime_env.sh` prepends `$runtime_dir/libexec:$runtime_dir/bin` ahead of the inherited PATH for POSIX launchers
- `nushell/scripts/utils/runtime_env.nu` does the same for `yzx env`, startup, popup, and other managed runtime flows
- `packaging/mk_runtime_tree.nix` populates `libexec/` by symlinking every binary from every package in `packaging/runtime_deps.nix`
- `packaging/runtime_deps.nix` currently includes both real user-facing tools and incidental helper/userland packages such as `coreutils`, `findutils`, `gnugrep`, `gnused`, `util-linux`, `procps`, and `nix`
- This means a Yazelix shell currently exports far more than the intended interactive tool surface. Any host-distributed app launched from that shell can resolve Nix-built helper binaries before `/usr/bin`, which is unsafe on non-NixOS Linux systems where those binaries may require a newer glibc than the host runtime provides
- Steam is only the clearest reproduction. The boundary bug is broader: any host app that shells out through PATH and expects host-compatible core userland tools is in scope

Preferred fix direction:
1. Delete-first: stop treating the full runtime dependency closure as the interactive PATH surface
2. Split the environment contract into a curated user PATH and an internal helper resolution path
3. Keep only first-class interactive tools on the exported PATH; resolve helper tools needed by Yazelix internals via absolute runtime paths or a helper-only PATH used inside managed wrappers/scripts
4. Audit all PATH-exporting seams together: `runtime_env.sh`, `runtime_env.nu`, `yzx env`, startup, popup/editor wrappers, and any launcher that re-exports the runtime environment
5. Add regression coverage for the boundary itself, not just Steam specifically: a host command launched from a Yazelix shell should not resolve incidental runtime-owned helper binaries such as `dirname` ahead of the system tool on Linux

Relevant tension to keep in mind:
- current docs market `yzx env` as a way to load Yazelix tools into ordinary shells and editor terminals
- current package/spec prose also talks about a fixed runtime toolset, but that does not require exporting the entire dependency closure as first-class interactive commands
acceptance_criteria: A Yazelix shell or `yzx env` session must not break host-distributed applications because runtime-owned helper binaries shadow system tools through PATH ordering. The exported interactive PATH should contain only the curated user-facing tool surface Yazelix intentionally owns, while incidental helper binaries needed by Yazelix internals are resolved through a separate internal path or absolute runtime paths. Steam on Linux should launch from a Yazelix shell without the `dirname` / glibc mismatch, and docs should describe the resulting PATH boundary honestly.
notes:
Reproduction from user report: with Yazelix active, PATH begins with the Yazelix runtime and coreutils store paths, Steam logs `dirname: ... GLIBC_2.42 not found`, `steamwebhelper` becomes zombie/defunct, and the UI never appears. Launching Steam with a clean system PATH fixes the issue immediately. This likely affects more than Steam: any host-distributed app that shells out through PATH and expects host-glibc-compatible userland tools is in scope.
Additional investigation: the packaging reshuffle that moved runtime tools into `libexec/` reduced profile/Home Manager collisions, but it also made the whole dependency closure easy to prepend as a single PATH segment. The real follow-up is to narrow what counts as the exported interactive tool surface, not to special-case Steam.
close_reason: Completed: exported interactive PATH now comes from curated runtime toolbin while libexec stays runtime-private, docs/specs describe the boundary, and runtime/package regressions cover toolbin-vs-libexec behavior.

title: Pass nixgl through the Home Manager Yazelix package build
description: The Home Manager module imports yazelix_package.nix with only pkgs, while the flake package output passes nixgl too. On Linux this can produce a Home Manager-installed Yazelix runtime that lacks the packaged nixGL wrapper path used by Ghostty desktop launches, so Home Manager users get a weaker runtime than .#yazelix.
design: Keep one package contract. The Home Manager module should consume the same nixgl-aware package path as the flake output instead of rebuilding a variant that silently drops nixgl. Do not solve this in user configs. After the repo fix, refresh the local Home Manager lock and verify the installed runtime contains the expected libexec nixGL wrapper and updated doctor/runtime checks.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Home Manager builds Yazelix with the same nixgl-aware package contract as the flake package output. 2. A Home Manager build from the local flake produces an installed Yazelix runtime with the packaged Linux graphics wrapper when applicable. 3. The fix is verified by rebuilding the local Home Manager config and checking the resulting runtime/doctor surface.
close_reason: Completed

title: Make `yzx doctor` detect stale host-shell shadowing without invocation-context redirects
description: The current doctor logic can warn about stale host-shell `yzx` shadowing when a modern wrapper redirect has already happened, but it can still report a false healthy state when the user invokes doctor through an older store-pinned shell function or when doctor is launched from the correct profile wrapper while stale shell blocks still exist on disk.
design: Teach doctor to inspect known shell startup files directly for Yazelix-owned stale shell blocks and store-pinned `yzx` functions or aliases. Do not rely solely on `YAZELIX_REDIRECTED_FROM_STALE_YZX_PATH` or wrapper-resolution context. The output should name the file and explain the cleanup path clearly.
acceptance_criteria: 1. `yzx doctor --verbose` warns when a stale Yazelix-managed shell block shadows or can shadow the profile-owned `yzx` command, even if doctor was launched from the profile wrapper. 2. The warning identifies the relevant startup file or managed block location. 3. The guidance points users at the supported cleanup or takeover flow instead of leaving a false healthy summary.
close_reason: Completed

title: Archive stale Yazelix shell blocks during Home Manager takeover
description: `yzx home_manager prepare` currently handles manual config, desktop artifacts, and the legacy ~/.local/bin/yzx wrapper, but it does not detect old Yazelix-managed shell blocks that still define `yzx()` or source store-pinned runtime hooks from files like ~/.bashrc. Those blocks can keep shells pinned to an obsolete runtime after Home Manager takes ownership.
design: Extend takeover artifact discovery to include known Yazelix-managed shell blocks in supported shell startup files. Keep the scope narrow: only archive blocks Yazelix can identify as its own, and do not rewrite unrelated user shell config. Reuse the delete-first migration philosophy: remove stale ownership instead of adding more runtime indirection.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Preview mode lists stale Yazelix-managed shell blocks as Home Manager takeover artifacts. 2. `yzx home_manager prepare --apply` archives only Yazelix-owned shell blocks and leaves unrelated user shell config untouched. 3. After takeover cleanup, a fresh shell resolves `yzx` through the profile-owned command instead of a stale store path.
close_reason: Completed

title: Fix split-brain Yazelix ownership after Home Manager takeover
description: After a Home Manager takeover, stale Yazelix-managed shell blocks can keep interactive shells pinned to an older store runtime while the desktop entry and profile wrapper use the Home Manager-owned runtime. That creates split-brain behavior: shell launches can appear healthy, desktop launch can fail, and users can receive conflicting signals about which Yazelix install is active.
design: Do not change the desktop launch path to depend on host shell state. The desktop entry should keep targeting the profile-owned yzx wrapper. Fix the migration and diagnostics path instead: Home Manager takeover helpers should detect/archive old Yazelix-managed shell blocks, and doctor should report stale host-shell shadowing even when invoked from the profile-owned wrapper.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Home Manager takeover no longer leaves a stale Yazelix-managed shell block silently shadowing the profile-owned yzx command. 2. Desktop launch and fresh interactive-shell launch resolve to the same owned runtime after cleanup. 3. Users get clear diagnostics and recovery guidance instead of a false healthy state.

title: Runtime bootstrap: resolve bundled tools through private runtime PATH
