close_reason: Completed: Mandelbrot now uses a deterministic fractal zoom/tour with visible structural motion, bounded iteration detail, and loop continuity. Verified with the yazelix_screen and front-door render tests after the stricter Mandelbrot follow-up landed.

title: Fix Mandelbrot screen continuous fractal loop
description: The Mandelbrot screen producer should continuously feel like an infinite fractal zoom/loop. The current implementation does not visibly keep fractalling as expected.
design: Keep the implementation in-house/std inside yazelix_screen. Adjust the view path and tests so frames evolve through a seamless zoom loop rather than settling into a static or shallow animation. Preserve bounded CPU cost and deterministic output.
acceptance_criteria: yzx screen mandelbrot continuously evolves through a seamless fractal loop; frame output changes across the loop while exact loop-boundary frames match; CPU iteration budget remains bounded; tests defend progression and loop continuity.
notes: 2026-04-26 dependency gate: production crates unchanged; dev-only crates unchanged. Mandelbrot zoom/path logic is deterministic numeric animation and should remain in-house/std inside yazelix_screen. Rejected adding rendering/fractal crates because the bug is the view loop contract, not missing infrastructure or performance.
close_reason: Completed: Mandelbrot now uses a deep deterministic zoom loop into a boundary target, returns seamlessly at the loop boundary, keeps iteration cost bounded, and has regression tests for structural frame progression and exact loop repeatability.

title: Evaluate dependency-layered Rust Nix builds
description: Measure and, if justified, move the Rust package build to a dependency-layered Nix builder so local source edits do not rebuild third-party crates during Nix/package iteration.
design: Compare the current buildRustPackage path with a crane-style or equivalent cargoArtifacts split. Adopt it only if it materially improves repeated local Nix builds without making packaging harder to understand. Keep the product package test-free by default and keep full tests in checks/CI.
acceptance_criteria: A before/after timing note exists for clean and incremental package builds; if adopted, dependency artifacts are cached separately from Yazelix source; package output remains identical from the user perspective; if rejected, the bead records the measured reason.
notes: Evaluation recorded in docs/specs/rust_nix_dependency_layering_evaluation.md. Current buildRustPackage path already builds only yazelix_core with package tests disabled. Measured current invalid-output package build after source changes at 0:20.88 and warm no-op build at 0:00.39. Rejected crane/dependency-layered migration for now; revisit only if repeated Home Manager/package timings show source-only Rust rebuilds above 60s or cargo-vendor-dir is a material wall-time share.
close_reason: Completed: measured current Nix package path, recorded rejection of a crane-style migration in docs/specs/rust_nix_dependency_layering_evaluation.md, and set a reopen threshold.

title: Add in-house Mandelbrot frame producer to yazelix_screen
description: Implement Mandelbrot as a CPU-generated yazelix_screen frame producer rather than pulling in a game engine or terminal UI framework.
design: Use the existing ScreenFrameProducer/ScreenAnimationContext seam. Start with deterministic CPU frames or cheap precomputed zoom/pan parameters, mapped to terminal-safe glyph/color output. Keep startup and runtime cost bounded. Do not add Bevy, Fyrox, Ratatui, image crates, or async runtimes for the first pass.
acceptance_criteria: Mandelbrot can render through yazelix_screen with deterministic frame output; frame dimensions and narrow-layout behavior are defended; runtime stays dependency-light and terminal-safe; if performance is poor, the bead records whether to reduce detail, precompute frames, or add a tiny in-house cache.
notes: Rust dependency gate: no new production or dev-only crates. Mandelbrot rendering is small deterministic CPU math and terminal frame mapping, so implement in-house/std inside yazelix_screen. Rejected alternatives: Bevy/Fyrox/Ratatui/image/async runtimes; they add packaging weight and do not improve the non-interactive terminal producer path.
close_reason: Completed: added dependency-free deterministic Mandelbrot frame producer in yazelix_screen, exposed it as yzx screen mandelbrot, and defended dimensions, math, and bounded iteration cost.

title: Decide yazelix_screen dependency boundary for animation engines
description: Record the dependency boundary for future yzx screen animation work: keep shipped terminal runtime animations in the in-house yazelix_screen crate instead of adopting broad game engines or a terminal UI framework prematurely.
design: Runtime yzx screen animations should stay in-house with simple data models: Vec<Boid> for boids, grids/bitsets for Game of Life, and CPU-generated frame producers for Mandelbrot. Add a tiny in-house spatial hash only if boid counts grow enough to require it. Add Ratatui only if yzx screen becomes an interactive app with controls, inspectors, panels, or widgets. Consider Bevy/Fyrox only for separate desktop visualizers, demo recorders, social-preview/gif generators, or other non-runtime tooling; do not put them in the shipped terminal runtime.
acceptance_criteria: The architecture decision is recorded in Beads; future boids, Game of Life, and Mandelbrot work can cite this bead for dependency decisions; any future Ratatui, Bevy, or Fyrox proposal must state which gate it satisfies and why the in-house renderer is insufficient.
close_reason: Decision recorded: keep shipped yzx screen animations in-house; gate Ratatui, Bevy, Fyrox, and spatial hashing behind explicit future needs.

title: Give boids stable visual identity in yzx screen
description: The new yzx screen boids renderer makes every boid look like the same 2-cell block and only pulses colors over time, so users cannot track individual units. Improve the boid renderer so individual boids have stable visual identity and direction-readable silhouettes while preserving the scaled no-row-gap terminal footprint.
design: Keep the in-house boids simulation. Fix only the visual encoding: make color identity stable per boid instead of tick-pulsed, and vary the 2-column cell pattern by boid identity or direction so units are distinguishable at terminal scale. Do not shrink to tiny braille dots or reintroduce skipped rows.
acceptance_criteria: yzx screen boids renders distinguishable boid units with stable identity over time; direction or identity is visible without relying on global pulsing; full_block and dotted modes keep the same frame dimensions and no inserted rows; focused tests defend stable per-boid rendering and frame footprint.
notes: Implemented: removed time-pulsed boid colors, expanded the stable boid palette, and made each boid render as a 2-cell directional silhouette. Full-block boids now distinguish east/west/north/south with ▐█, █▌, ▀▀, and ▄▄; dotted boids use matching scaled braille signatures. Verification: cargo fmt --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml --all -- --check; cargo check --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core -p yazelix_screen; cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_screen; cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core front_door_render.
close_reason: Completed: boid units now have stable color identity and direction-readable scaled silhouettes.

title: Popup: define standalone-capable contract and extraction gate
description: Define what would have to be true before the popup capability becomes a standalone Zellij plugin or external package.
design: Do not extract by enthusiasm. The gate should require a generic config surface, stable request schema, documented Zellij pipe or plugin command contract, tests that do not depend on the full Yazelix runtime, and clear examples for plain Zellij users. If those are not true, keep it in-repo.
acceptance_criteria: There is a written extraction gate for popup; plain-Zellij value is documented without promising an immediate repo split; the current Yazelix implementation path remains the source of truth until the gate is met.
close_reason: Completed: added POP-005 to the floating TUI panes spec as the standalone popup extraction gate, documenting plain-Zellij value without promising an immediate split and keeping the current in-repo Yazelix implementation canonical until the gate is met.

title: Popup: isolate generic transient policy from Yazelix adapters
description: Separate generic popup/transient-pane policy from Yazelix runtime wrappers, menu wrappers, runtime_dir assumptions, and sidebar-refresh hooks.
design: Keep the pane orchestrator as the implementation owner. Generic policy should cover identity, command argv, cwd, geometry, focus/toggle/close semantics, and single-instance rules. Yazelix-specific wrappers and post-close hooks should be adapters passed into that policy rather than hardcoded into it.
acceptance_criteria: TransientPaneRequest and related policy surfaces no longer require Yazelix wrapper names to explain their behavior; Yazelix popup/menu behavior remains unchanged; sidebar refresh remains supported as an adapter hook; regression tests cover open, focus, toggle, close, and single-instance behavior.
notes: Rust dependency gate: no new production or dev-only crates. The transient/popup policy is existing in-house state-machine logic over pane snapshots and argv/cwd/geometry values. Rejected alternatives: adding a command-builder crate, process framework, or TUI/popup framework before standalone extraction needs are proven.
close_reason: Completed: split generic transient matching/toggle/launch policy from Yazelix popup/menu adapters, kept sidebar refresh as an adapter hook, and expanded focused contract tests.

title: Bar: add AI activity and token-budget widget extension points
description: Add the extension points needed for future Codex, Claude, ChatGPT, and other agent status widgets, including thinking/activity spinners and token or subscription budget displays.
design: Separate live activity facts from provider budget/accounting sources. The first implementation can expose fixture-backed and config-backed provider widgets before any networked provider integration. Keep provider-specific code isolated so adding a new agent does not fork the whole bar renderer.
acceptance_criteria: The bar has a documented widget extension shape for AI activity and provider budgets; thinking/spinner state can be rendered from status-bus facts; token or subscription budget widgets have a provider adapter boundary; existing widget behavior is unchanged.
notes: Dependency decision before implementation: extend yazelix_bar in-house with existing serde-free/static rendering boundaries unless existing crate dependencies already suffice. No provider SDKs, no network/accounting crates. Token-budget providers stay adapter-shaped and fixture/config-backed only. Packaging impact: none.
close_reason: Completed: zellij.widget_tray now accepts ai_activity and token_budget extension widgets, zjstatus commands are wired, and existing default widgets are unchanged.

title: Bar: consume pane-orchestrator status bus for dynamic widgets
description: Connect yazelix_bar to the versioned pane-orchestrator status bus for dynamic session-aware widgets.
design: The bar may format facts, but it must not rediscover live Zellij state by parsing panes, KDL, logs, or generated files. Start with facts that are already reliable in the bus: focus context, active tab/workspace, sidebar state, popup/menu state, and layout state.
acceptance_criteria: At least one dynamic widget consumes the status payload through the supported transport; unsupported schema versions fail visibly; bar rendering remains deterministic in tests with fixture payloads; no pane-orchestrator presentation formatting leaks into bar code.
notes: 2026-04-26 implementation start: no new crates. Bar consumer should use the supported zellij status-bus snapshot/JSON shape and keep formatting local to bar code.
close_reason: Completed: added optional workspace bar widget that renders through the validated pane-orchestrator status-bus transport, with schema/fixture tests and config/HM/docs updates.

title: Bar: extract widget registry and zjstatus renderer boundary
description: Move bar-specific widget definitions, static/custom text rendering, command widget templates, colors, and zjstatus template decisions behind a yazelix_bar-owned boundary.
design: This should not wait for the live status bus. Preserve the current zjstatus backend and existing editor, shell, term, cpu, ram, and custom text behavior. The split should reduce generic Zellij materialization knowledge of bar presentation while keeping package/layout generation stable.
acceptance_criteria: A yazelix_bar-owned source boundary exists for widget registry and zjstatus rendering; existing bar widgets render the same as before; CPU and RAM widgets keep their current behavior; tests cover widget rendering and narrow or empty tray cases without a live Zellij session.
notes: Rust dependency gate: no new production or dev-only crates. Build the bar widget registry and zjstatus segment renderer in-house/std because the domain is small string rendering with a fixed widget set. Rejected alternatives: a KDL parser dependency for the existing template, a full TUI/status framework, or serde-backed schema work in this bead; all add packaging surface without improving the current renderer boundary.
close_reason: Completed: extracted yazelix_bar crate with zjstatus widget registry and renderer boundary; kept existing widget output and added focused renderer tests.

title: Status bus: model AI pane activity facts
description: Add a clean status-bus fact model for AI panes so the bar can show activity such as a thinking spinner for Codex, Claude, or other agent panes in the current tab.
design: Keep this as status facts, not provider UI. Detect or accept explicit pane activity signals through pane-orchestrator-owned state and avoid brittle scraping where possible. The schema should support provider name, activity state, pane association, and freshness without coupling to one CLI.
acceptance_criteria: The status payload can represent inactive, active/thinking, stale, and unknown AI pane states; tab-local association is explicit; no token or provider UI formatting leaks into the pane orchestrator; future bar widgets can consume these facts directly.
notes: Dependency decision before implementation: model AI activity facts in-house using existing pane-orchestrator/status-bus structs and serde/serde_json already present. No new production crates or dev crates. Rejected alternatives: provider scraping libraries or provider-specific SDKs; this bead is fact schema/state only. Packaging impact: none.
close_reason: Completed: status-bus extensions now model tab-local AI pane activity facts with normalized unknown, inactive, active, thinking, and stale states.

title: Status bus: expose producer and consumer transport
description: Expose a reliable way for Yazelix consumers to request or receive the pane-orchestrator status payload.
design: Prefer the smallest useful transport first: an explicit pipe command or snapshot command backed by the pane orchestrator state. Do not add a long-running second state owner. If subscription is needed later, it must still treat the pane orchestrator as the only live Zellij truth source.
acceptance_criteria: A consumer can obtain the current versioned status payload without parsing ad hoc logs, KDL, or generated files; stale or unsupported schema versions fail clearly; tests cover request handling and a representative consumer path.
notes: 2026-04-26 implementation start: no new crates. Transport should reuse existing zellij pipe mechanics and pane-orchestrator state; no second long-running state owner.
close_reason: Completed: added zellij status-bus consumer command backed by the existing pane-orchestrator active-tab snapshot pipe, with schema-version validation and focused tests.

title: Status bus: define versioned session schema
description: Define the versioned pane-orchestrator status/session payload before adding more consumers.
design: Start from active tab session state and explicitly classify fields as owned live state, derived state, or adapter state. Include schema_version, active tab, workspace root/source, managed pane IDs, focus context, layout/sidebar collapsed state, sidebar Yazi state, transient pane state, and extension slots for AI pane activity. Keep presentation and widget formatting out of the schema.
acceptance_criteria: A documented schema exists with stable field names, ownership notes, and compatibility policy; tests defend serialization and representative payloads; the schema does not make the bar rediscover Zellij truth or embed bar-specific formatting.
notes: Rust dependency gate: no new production or dev-only crates. The status bus schema should use existing serde support already present in the pane-orchestrator crate; the schema itself is plain data and should stay in-house/std. Rejected alternatives: adding a schema-validation framework, JSON schema generation crate, or event-bus framework before the producer/transport contract exists.
close_reason: Completed: expanded the active-tab status bus v1 schema with ownership/compatibility notes, transient pane facts, AI activity extension slots, and serialization/back-compat tests.

title: Screen: define future animation extension seam
description: Define the yazelix_screen extension seam for boids, Mandelbrot, fullscreen screen plugin use, and future animation families after the renderer and Game of Life contracts are stable.
design: Use a small frame-producer boundary rather than a broad TUI framework. A simulation should own deterministic state updates and emit cells into the shared framebuffer. The renderer should stay style-agnostic and terminal-safe. Avoid losing current welcome features while making new animations addable without editing terminal-output internals.
acceptance_criteria: A new animation can be added by implementing the screen frame-producer contract; boids and Mandelbrot can share framebuffer/output code; fullscreen yzx screen plugin work has a stable renderer API to target; startup cost and narrow-layout behavior remain explicit acceptance checks.
notes:
Rust dependency gate: no new production or dev-only crates. In-house logic: a small ScreenFrameProducer trait, ScreenAnimationContext, and a GameOfLifeAnimation implementation that wraps the existing Game of Life state/render/resize behavior. Rejected alternatives: a broad animation framework, async runtime, ratatui app model, or plugin-level abstraction; the next useful seam is only deterministic frame production into the existing framebuffer/output path. Packaging impact: no external dependencies and no new binaries; this extends the internal yazelix_screen API so boids and Mandelbrot can target it later.
Implemented: added ScreenAnimationContext and ScreenFrameProducer in yazelix_screen, plus GameOfLifeAnimation as the first concrete frame producer. yzx screen now drives Game of Life through render_frame, advance_frame, and resize instead of hand-rolling state calls in front_door_render. This gives boids and Mandelbrot a small concrete API to target without changing terminal output internals. Verification: cargo fmt --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml --all -- --check; cargo check --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core -p yazelix_screen; cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_screen; cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core front_door_render; cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core --test yzx_control_front_door.
close_reason: Completed: future animation frame-producer seam added and Game of Life now uses it.

title: Screen: move Game of Life styles and settings into yazelix_screen
description: Move Game of Life state, style selection, full_block rendering, dotted scale-4 braille texture behavior, and related welcome/screen adapters into yazelix_screen.
design: Preserve the current UX exactly before adding new visuals. full_block remains the default. dotted remains a supported style with the same two-terminal-columns by one-terminal-row footprint, not a tiny packed-cell variant. Keep config parsing and Home Manager parity in the existing config surface while the screen subsystem owns the rendering semantics.
acceptance_criteria: core.game_of_life_cell_style continues to support full_block and dotted; both direct config and Home Manager paths still normalize the option; glider footprint, no skipped rows, and narrow-layout fit are defended by focused tests; no old welcome_style behavior is removed.
notes:
Rust dependency gate: production crates remain crossterm only through yazelix_screen and serde only where existing asset/config parsing in yazelix_core already needs it; no new production crates. Dev-only crates: none. In-house logic: GameOfLifeCellStyle parse/glyph contract, screen state, grid sizing, seed placement, stepping, and framebuffer rendering. Rejected alternatives: moving the whole ascii_art_data catalog into yazelix_screen would mix logo/boids welcome assets into the screen simulation crate; keeping Game of Life entirely in yazelix_core would leave the next boids/Mandelbrot work without a clean simulation/rendering owner. Packaging impact: no new external dependencies; this extends the internal path crate boundary created by yazelix-subsys.1.1.
Implemented: moved GameOfLifeCellStyle, parse error type, GameOfLifeScreenState, fixed Game of Life specs, seed placement, stepping, full_block/dotted glyph rendering, and Game of Life screen-frame tests into yazelix_screen. yazelix_core now keeps only style catalog resolution, welcome composition, final logo frame composition, and CoreError mapping for invalid config values. Verification: cargo fmt --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml --all -- --check; cargo check --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core -p yazelix_screen; cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_screen; cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core front_door_render; cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core --test yzx_control_front_door.
close_reason: Completed: Game of Life styles, state, stepping, and rendering moved into yazelix_screen with footprint regressions preserved.

title: Screen: extract terminal framebuffer and output backend
description: Move ScreenFrame, ScreenCell, terminal sizing, alternate-screen output, cursor addressing, autowrap handling, and frame flushing into the internal yazelix_screen subsystem without changing rendered behavior.
design: Keep this slice mechanical. The renderer contract should make terminal rows explicit and forbid newline-driven animation output. Preserve the current crossterm backend, absolute row cursor addressing, no-autowrap screen mode, and clear failure surfaces. Do not add ratatui or a second renderer abstraction unless a later screen feature proves it needs widget-style layout.
acceptance_criteria: The front-door code delegates framebuffer construction and terminal output to yazelix_screen; yzx screen and welcome paths preserve their current frame placement; tests defend no inserted renderer rows, absolute row addressing, and stable visible footprint; no user-facing screen style or setting is removed.
notes:
Rust dependency gate: production crates for this slice are crossterm only, moved into the new internal yazelix_screen crate for terminal size/mode/output primitives. Dev-only crates: none. In-house logic: ScreenCell, ScreenFrame, visible line rendering helpers, terminal width/height env override, absolute-row frame output, and screen flush primitives. Rejected alternatives: ratatui is too broad for a terminal animation surface; adding unicode-width is unnecessary because the current visible-width contract already counts rendered scalar values and this slice preserves behavior; adding an error crate is unnecessary because yazelix_screen can return io::Result and yazelix_core keeps user-facing CoreError mapping. Packaging impact: rust_core workspace gains an in-repo yazelix_screen crate and yazelix_core depends on it by path; no external repository and no new third-party dependency.
Implemented: added internal rust_core/yazelix_screen crate for ScreenCell, ScreenFrame, terminal width/height, visible-width centering helpers, raw-mode guard, screen-mode enter/leave, absolute-row frame output, and stdout flush. yazelix_core front_door_render now delegates framebuffer construction and terminal output to yazelix_screen while keeping Game of Life simulation/style behavior for the next bead. Verification: cargo fmt --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml --all -- --check; cargo check --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core -p yazelix_screen; cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_screen; cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core front_door_render.
close_reason: Completed: terminal framebuffer and output backend extracted into internal yazelix_screen crate with focused renderer regressions passing.

title: Keep sidebar as a pane-orchestrator capability
description: Clarify that sidebar should remain a capability of the pane orchestrator, not a separate repo or separate live state owner, unless future work proves a generic sidebar API independent of Yazelix-managed Yazi/editor/layout state.
design: Ground the decision in current code: sidebar identity, focus policy, layout toggling, horizontal motion, workspace state, and sidebar Yazi state all share pane-orchestrator session truth. Continue isolating reusable sidebar contracts, but keep the loaded Zellij plugin as one state owner. Separate generic sidebar terms from the current Yazi file-tree adapter.
acceptance_criteria: Sidebar remains a pane-orchestrator capability for now; existing sidebar extraction beads are retargeted toward internal capability hardening; sidebar Yazi identity, enable_sidebar removal, terminology, and tab-local state guardrails have clear related beads; no standalone sidebar repo is planned unless future work proves generic use outside Yazelix.
notes:
Related existing beads: yazelix-l968.2, yazelix-l968.2.1, yazelix-l968.2.2, yazelix-l968.2.3, yazelix-kihb, yazelix-069b.
2026-04-26 plan-space review: decision is sidebar stays in pane-orchestrator now. The implementation follow-through lives in yazelix-l968.2 children, yazelix-kihb, yazelix-069b, and terminology/postmortem guardrail beads.
close_reason: Decision recorded: keep sidebar inside pane-orchestrator for now; implementation follow-through remains in related sidebar capability beads.

title: Harden popup as a standalone-capable pane-orchestrator capability
description: Make the popup/transient-pane capability clean enough that plain Zellij users could eventually configure it without the full Yazelix runtime, while keeping implementation inside the pane orchestrator for now.
design: Start from transient.rs, transient_pane_contract.rs, yzx popup, and the existing l968.1 popup extraction bead. Separate generic popup concepts from Yazelix-specific wrapper flow: pane title/identity, command argv, cwd, geometry, toggle/focus/close semantics, and optional post-close hooks. The first milestone is a stable capability API, not a standalone repo.
acceptance_criteria: Popup identity and open/toggle request schema are explicit; single-instance behavior remains covered; Yazelix-specific runtime wrapper, menu wrapper, and sidebar-refresh hooks are isolated from generic popup policy; standalone extraction remains gated by a documented contract rather than assumed.
notes:
This updates the old standalone-extraction direction: popup has external value, but current code still depends on Yazelix runtime wrappers and sidebar refresh behavior.
2026-04-26 plan-space review: existing popup identity/argv/geometry work is valuable but not sufficient for standalone-capable use. The missing seam is isolating Yazelix runtime adapters and writing an explicit extraction gate.
close_reason: Completed: generic transient policy is isolated from Yazelix adapters in subsys.4.1, and the standalone extraction gate is documented in subsys.4.2.

title: Make yazelix_bar a first-class in-repo subsystem
description: Promote the current zjstatus-backed bar from scattered generated template/config snippets into a first-class Yazelix subsystem. The bar should own status/tabline presentation, widget rendering, CPU/RAM widgets, custom badges, and future AI usage/activity widgets while consuming session facts from the pane-orchestrator bus.
design: Keep zjstatus as the first backend. Move bar-specific rendering decisions out of generic Zellij materialization where practical: widget registry, allowed widget names, command widget templates, colors, compact/full display decisions, and custom text formatting. Do not make the bar own pane/session discovery; consume the versioned pane-orchestrator status bus instead.
acceptance_criteria: There is a clear yazelix_bar-owned source boundary for widget definitions and zjstatus template rendering; existing editor/shell/term/cpu/ram/custom text behavior is preserved; dynamic widgets consume the pane-orchestrator status bus; AI usage and activity widgets have explicit extension points; bar tests cover widget rendering without requiring a live Zellij session.
notes:
The current bar is in configs/zellij/layouts/fragments/zjstatus_tab_template.kdl plus zellij_materialization.rs render_widget_tray_segment/render_custom_text_segment and zellij.widget_tray config.
2026-04-26 plan-space review: do not block all bar work on the status bus. Static widget registry and zjstatus rendering can be extracted first; only dynamic widgets and AI activity depend on the bus.
close_reason: Completed by closed child beads: yazelix_bar now owns widget registry/rendering, consumes pane-orchestrator status facts for dynamic widgets, and exposes AI activity/token-budget extension points with tests.

title: Define pane-orchestrator session/status bus
description: Extend the pane-orchestrator's current active-tab session snapshot into a stable versioned status bus that can feed Yazelix bar widgets and future external integrations. The bus should expose live session facts without making the bar rediscover Zellij truth on its own.
design: Start from get_active_tab_session_state and active_tab_session_state.rs. Add only facts that are already owned by the pane orchestrator or can be derived from PaneUpdate/TabUpdate: active tab, workspace root/source, managed pane IDs, focus context, layout/sidebar collapsed state, sidebar Yazi state, transient popup/menu state, and eventually AI pane activity. Keep schema_version explicit and keep renderer/presentation out of this layer.
acceptance_criteria: A versioned status/session schema and transport exist with documented fields and tests; pane-orchestrator remains the single owner of live Zellij session truth; zellij/workspace commands and bar code can consume the same payload; AI pane activity can be represented as facts; no bar-specific formatting leaks into the pane-orchestrator.
notes:
This is the key seam between yazelix_pane_orchestrator and yazelix_bar.
2026-04-26 plan-space review: split into schema, transport, and AI activity facts so the bar can consume stable facts without turning the pane orchestrator into a presentation layer.
close_reason: Completed by closed child beads: the pane-orchestrator status bus has a versioned schema, producer/consumer transport, and AI pane activity facts without bar-specific formatting in the producer.

title: Extract yzx screen into an internal yazelix_screen crate
description: Move the terminal animation/framebuffer code out of front_door_render.rs into a self-contained in-repo Rust crate or module package named yazelix_screen. The screen subsystem should own terminal-frame construction, Game of Life state, boids state, future Mandelbrot rendering, and reusable frame playback primitives.
design: Do this before adding major new screen simulations. Keep crossterm as the backend and avoid ratatui unless the screen surface gains real widget/app requirements. The first split should be mechanical: preserve current welcome/screen behavior, expose a small API consumed by yazelix_core front-door commands, and move renderer tests with the subsystem.
acceptance_criteria: front_door_render.rs stops owning the full screen engine; yazelix_screen has focused tests for terminal output, row addressing, Game of Life full_block and dotted styles, boids-ready framebuffer behavior, and narrow-layout fit; existing yzx welcome/screen behavior is unchanged; follow-on visual beads implement against the new screen extension seam.
notes:
This is the strongest internal extraction candidate because the current front_door_render.rs is already a cohesive terminal animation engine. Future external extraction is plausible after the API stabilizes.
2026-04-26 plan-space review: implementation should happen through child beads .1.1 renderer/backend, .1.2 Game of Life styles/settings, and .1.3 future animation seam. This avoids mixing mechanical extraction with new boids or Mandelbrot behavior.
Implemented through children .1.1-.1.3: created internal yazelix_screen crate, moved terminal framebuffer/output primitives, moved Game of Life state/styles/rendering/tests, and added a frame-producer seam for future animations. Existing logo/boids welcome composition remains in front_door_render until those visual features get their own implementation beads; boids/Mandelbrot should now target yazelix_screen rather than expanding front_door_render.
close_reason: Completed: internal yazelix_screen crate now owns terminal screen primitives, Game of Life rendering, and future animation frame-producer seam.

title: Subsystemize Yazelix internals before repo extraction
description: Define and execute the internal subsystem shape for Yazelix before extracting any standalone repositories. The intended shape is one repo with hard boundaries: yazelix_core, yazelix_maintainer, yazelix_screen, yazelix_pane_orchestrator, and yazelix_bar. External repos should happen only after a subsystem has a stable API, independent tests, and clear value outside Yazelix.
design: Use internal crates/packages and versioned contracts first. Keep live Zellij session truth in the pane orchestrator; let the bar consume session/status facts; make screen rendering a self-contained terminal animation crate; keep popup/sidebar as pane-orchestrator capabilities until their generic API is proven. Avoid separate repos while subsystem APIs are still changing quickly.
acceptance_criteria: There are concrete Beads for the maintainer crate split, screen renderer/style/extension seam, session/status schema and transport, bar widget/rendering subsystem, AI activity/widget extension, popup capability hardening, and sidebar capability decision. Each extraction candidate states whether it is in-repo only, external-facing API, or future standalone repo candidate. Existing features must be preserved unless a separate bead explicitly decides to remove them.
notes:
Created after reviewing the current pane-orchestrator, zjstatus/bar, popup/sidebar, and screen renderer code. This epic supersedes ad hoc repo-splitting discussion; code movement still happens in child implementation beads.
Current related work: yazelix-9opk.5.1 covers the maintainer crate split; yazelix-subsys.1 covers yazelix_screen extraction; yazelix-subsys.2 and .3 cover the pane-orchestrator status bus and yazelix_bar; yazelix-subsys.4 retargets popup toward a standalone-capable pane-orchestrator capability; yazelix-subsys.5 records that sidebar remains a pane-orchestrator capability for now.
2026-04-26 plan-space review: keep the subsystem plan feature-preserving and split by stable seams, not by premature repo boundaries. New child beads now separate screen renderer/style/extension work, status schema/transport/AI facts, bar static extraction/dynamic widgets/AI widgets, and popup adapter isolation/extraction gate.
close_reason: all steps complete

title: Split Rust maintainer tooling into an in-repo crate
description: Move maintainer-only Rust modules and binaries out of the product runtime crate without offloading them to another repository. The target crate is rust_core/yazelix_maintainer, depending on yazelix_core for product contract APIs while yazelix_core remains independent of maintainer tooling.
design: Mechanical crate split: move repo_* modules, workspace_session_contract.rs, yzx_repo_validator, and yzx_repo_maintainer into yazelix_maintainer; update CI, nushell/scripts/yzx/dev.nu, docs, and packaging references to use -p yazelix_maintainer for maintainer commands; update packaging/rust_core_helper.nix so runtime builds/tests target only yazelix_core and shipped helper binaries.
acceptance_criteria: yazelix_core no longer exposes repo_* maintainer modules or yzx_repo_* binaries; yzx_repo_validator and yzx_repo_maintainer command names still work through the new crate; runtime package helper builds only product binaries; CI and yzx dev maintainer routes use the maintainer crate; cargo test -p yazelix_core and cargo test -p yazelix_maintainer pass; docs/rust_maintainer_tooling_boundary.md remains accurate.
notes:
Created from the yazelix-9opk.5 boundary decision. Do not create an external repository for this step.
Rust dependency gate: do not add new crates for the split. Reuse the existing yazelix_core dependency set from the moved maintainer modules, and depend on yazelix_core by path only where product contract APIs are needed. Rejected alternatives: offloading to another repository now, adding a CLI framework, or duplicating product runtime modules inside the maintainer crate.
close_reason: Completed: split repo validators, maintainer workflows, workspace-session validator, and yzx_repo_* binaries into rust_core/yazelix_maintainer; updated CI, yzx dev, docs, and runtime package helper.

title: Add Rust ownership and LOC budget after canonical cleanup
description:
Why this issue exists and what needs to be done

Yazelix already has a Nushell budget because shell LOC was a known risk. After Rust becomes canonical and the cleanup audit deletes obvious waste, Rust needs a lightweight guardrail too, or LOC can grow again through validators, wrappers, duplicated command metadata, and maintainer-only helpers.

Add a Rust ownership/budget manifest and validator only after the audit establishes the canonical shape. The validator should prevent accidental Rust surface growth without freezing useful product work.
design: Do not add this before cleanup, because it would lock in current bloat. Use the final inventory to define families such as runtime_commands, materialization, config_contracts, workspace_session, maintainer_tooling, zellij_plugin, and tests. Prefer budgets per ownership family over one blunt global ceiling.
acceptance_criteria: A Rust ownership/budget manifest exists with measured LOC/file counts by family; a repo validator checks unexpected files and no-growth ceilings; the validator is wired into the appropriate maintainer gate; ceilings reflect post-cleanup canonical code, not the pre-audit baseline.
notes: 2026-04-26 implementation start: no new crates. Use existing repo validator patterns and a checked-in manifest; budgets should be family-based and advisory enough to prevent accidental growth without freezing product work.
close_reason: Completed: added config_metadata/rust_ownership_budget.toml, validate-rust-ownership-budget, CI/static gate wiring, docs, and regression coverage for unexpected files plus LOC growth.

title: Separate or offload maintainer-only Rust tooling
description:
Why this issue exists and what needs to be done

A large share of Rust LOC appears to be maintainer tooling, validators, update workflows, sweep runners, repo sync, release bumping, and packaging checks. Some of this belongs in Yazelix because it protects the product, but some may be better isolated from the user runtime crate or eventually moved to another repo/tooling crate.

Evaluate whether maintainer-only Rust subsystems should stay in yazelix_core, move into a separate crate inside this repo, or be offloaded to a separate repository.
design: Reason from first principles: runtime users should not pay conceptual or packaging complexity for maintainer-only machinery unless the shared code genuinely benefits product safety. Consider repo_contract_validation.rs, repo_update_workflow.rs, repo_sweep_runner.rs, repo_issue_sync.rs, repo_plugin_build.rs, repo_version_bump.rs, yzx_repo_maintainer, and yzx_repo_validator. Do not offload code just to reduce local LOC if it makes releases less reproducible or splits contracts across repos.
acceptance_criteria: There is a keep/split/offload decision for each maintainer-only Rust subsystem; any accepted split has a concrete implementation bead; any rejected offload has a short rationale; runtime package impact is explicit.
notes:
Initial maintainer-heavy candidates by LOC: repo_contract_validation.rs 3547, repo_update_workflow.rs 1438, repo_validation.rs 1142, repo_sweep_runner.rs 1064, repo_issue_sync.rs 670, repo_test_runner.rs 591, repo_plugin_build.rs 476, repo_version_bump.rs 469, bin/yzx_repo_maintainer.rs 283, bin/yzx_repo_validator.rs 243.
Completed 2026-04-26: decision recorded in docs/rust_maintainer_tooling_boundary.md. Keep maintainer-only Rust tooling in this repository, reject external offload for now, and split it into an in-repo yazelix_maintainer crate. Created implementation bead yazelix-9opk.5.1 and made yazelix-9opk.6 wait for that split so the Rust LOC budget codifies the post-split shape.
close_reason: Decision recorded: split maintainer Rust tooling into an in-repo crate; reject external offload for now

title: Show only major releases in README latest block
description: Change the generated README Latest Tagged Releases block to show the latest major release tags only, e.g. v16 and v15, instead of point releases like v16.1.
acceptance_criteria: README Latest Tagged Releases shows v16 and v15, no v16.1; sync-readme-surface keeps that output; validate-readme-version passes.
notes: Implemented major-only README latest release selection. The generator now filters release entries to pure major tags, so v16.1 resolves to the v16/v15 block; README rendering trims trailing periods from generated bullet text to preserve README style. Verification: sync-readme-surface --version v16.1, cargo fmt --check, validate-readme-version, validate-upgrade-contract, cargo test -p yazelix_core repo_contract_validation, and git diff --check.
close_reason: Completed

title: Guard package-time Rust tests from host-tool dependencies
description: A default Cargo integration test that shell-executed `nix eval` broke Home Manager package builds because Nix package test sandboxes do not provide the host `nix` command or the full repository root. Add a durable guard/policy so package-time Rust tests stay hermetic, while Nix-dependent validation lives in maintainer validators or explicit non-package lanes.
acceptance_criteria: A validator or test-lint catches default/package-time Rust tests that invoke host tools such as `nix`, `home-manager`, or repository paths outside the packaged Rust source; Nix-dependent checks are documented as maintainer/package gates instead; the previous `home_manager_option_metadata` failure mode is covered by a non-package validation path.
close_reason: Completed: added package-time Rust test purity validator, wired it into maintainer test runner, documented the package-vs-maintainer gate split, and verified the prior Nix-in-Cargo-test failure mode is rejected.

title: Add fast Rust inner-loop check commands
description: Current Yazelix Rust edits are too often verified through `nix develop -c ...`, which can realize dev-shell/package derivations before running simple `cargo fmt` or focused tests. Add a maintainer workflow surface for fast Rust inner-loop checks that runs Cargo directly from an already-prepared environment, keeps Nix package validation as an explicit final gate, and documents when to use each path.
acceptance_criteria: A maintainer command or documented workflow exists for fast Rust fmt/check/test without re-entering `nix develop` each time; package/Home Manager validation remains a separate explicit gate; AGENTS/docs explain the intended inner-loop vs package-gate split; verification demonstrates the fast path on a focused Rust test.
notes: Local maintainer setup now installs cargo, rustc, rustfmt, clippy, and rust-analyzer through /home/lucca/.config/home-manager/home.nix. Direct cargo fmt --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml --all -- --check works without nix develop and completed in under a second on this host. Repo task still remains open: add a Yazelix-maintained fast command/workflow plus docs/AGENTS guidance, and keep nix build/Home Manager validation as the explicit package gate.
close_reason: Completed: added direct yzx dev rust fmt/check/test commands, documented fast inner-loop usage, and verified focused Rust tests/checks run without entering nix develop.

title: Fix Home Manager option-doc store-path warning
description: Home Manager option documentation can warn that options.json references the Yazelix flake source store path without proper context. Stabilize the Yazelix module declaration path so third-party option metadata does not leak /nix/store/...-source into generated option docs.
notes: Follow-up after local Home Manager switch: the visible options.json warning path was the root Home Manager flake's nixpkgs source (/nix/store/...-source), not the Yazelix source. The repo change still stabilizes Yazelix module declaration metadata, but the remaining warning is from Home Manager's generated manual/options docs. Local setup workaround applied in /home/lucca/.config/home-manager/home.nix: manual.manpages.enable = false.
close_reason: Completed: Home Manager module now sets a stable logical _file declaration path, and the config-surface validator defends that programs.yazelix option metadata no longer leaks source paths into options.json.

title: Implement boids screen as a scaled yazelix_screen animation surface
description: Add a real boids implementation for yzx screen on top of the internal yazelix_screen framebuffer and animation extension seam. Replace canned boids frames with deterministic boid state updates and scaled terminal rendering that preserves readable size in both yzx screen and welcome contexts.
design: Do not hardcode a tiny braille-only visual. Use the screen frame-producer contract from yazelix-subsys.1.3, with deterministic positions/velocities, separation/alignment/cohesion steering, and a renderer style that can map to full_block or scaled dotted/braille cells without changing terminal-output internals. Keep the first pass O(n^2) unless profiling proves it is too slow.
acceptance_criteria: Boids render at a readable scale comparable to current Game of Life cells; no empty-line skipping appears in yzx screen; welcome-screen and screen-mode aspect ratios are explicitly checked; full_block and dotted-style rendering choices remain possible through the screen subsystem; deterministic tests cover state update and frame footprint.
notes:
Rust dependency gate: keep this in-house on top of the planned framebuffer work and current crossterm backend; do not add ratatui for the first pass. Production crates: reuse crossterm only. Dev-only crates: none required initially. External context: Craig Reynolds' steering-behavior writeup describes flocking as the combination of separation, alignment, and cohesion behaviors, which should drive the simulation model (https://www.red3d.com/cwr/steer/gdc99/index.html). Unicode Braille Patterns U+2800..U+28FF provide 256 glyphs, which makes a 2x4 subcell packer a better fit than coarse arrow glyphs for sparse moving boids (https://unicode.org/charts/PDF/U2800.pdf). Crossterm already provides cursor movement and terminal control commands, so the renderer can stay on the current terminal backend without a TUI framework (https://docs.rs/crossterm/latest/crossterm/cursor/). Inference from those sources: implement a BoidsScreenState with deterministic seed, velocity/position updates, simple O(n^2) steering first, braille-packed framebuffer rendering, and optional short trails; revisit ratatui only if yzx screen later needs overlays or app-style widgets.
2026-04-26 subsystem review: boids should build on the new internal yazelix_screen crate rather than expanding front_door_render.rs further. This bead is now blocked by yazelix-subsys.1 so the simulation lands against the stable screen/framebuffer subsystem boundary.
Rust dependency gate update before implementation: keep the boids simulation in-house in yazelix_screen on top of ScreenFrameProducer and the existing terminal framebuffer. Production crates: no additions beyond crossterm already owned by yazelix_screen. Dev-only crates: none. In-house logic: deterministic boid initialization, separation/alignment/cohesion update, speed limiting, wraparound, and scaled full_block/dotted rendering. Rejected alternatives: random-number crates are unnecessary for deterministic startup visuals; spatial-index crates are premature for the small flock size; ratatui remains unnecessary because this is an animation producer, not an app UI. Packaging impact: no new external dependencies or binaries.
Implemented: added deterministic in-house BoidsAnimation to yazelix_screen as a ScreenFrameProducer. yzx screen boids now uses live boid state with separation/alignment/cohesion, speed limiting, wraparound, resize support, and scaled 2-column by 1-row full_block/dotted rendering through the shared framebuffer. The old canned boids frames remain only for the welcome animation. Verification: cargo fmt --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml --all -- --check; cargo check --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core -p yazelix_screen; cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_screen; cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core front_door_render; cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core --test yzx_control_front_door.
close_reason: Completed: yzx screen boids now uses scaled deterministic yazelix_screen animation instead of canned frames.

title: Refactor yzx screen around a Rust-owned framebuffer renderer
description: yzx screen rendering bugs keep recurring because front_door_render.rs still assembles animation frames directly as strings and glyph-specific terminal output. Introduce a Rust-owned internal framebuffer/render contract for yzx screen so styles render into explicit cells before flushing to the terminal. Keep the backend focused on the current front-door screen surface rather than turning Yazelix into a full TUI app.
notes:
Rust dependency gate: default to an in-house framebuffer/cell model plus the existing crossterm backend. Production crates: keep current crossterm usage; add no new production crate by default. Dev-only crates: none required initially. Rejected alternative: ratatui for the first pass, because yzx screen is a fullscreen animation surface rather than a widget-heavy TUI and ratatui would add framework surface without solving row-gap or glyph-contract bugs by itself. Revisit ratatui only if yzx screen later needs overlays, controls, inspectors, or other real application-style widgets.
Implemented first framebuffer slice for Game of Life: introduced explicit ScreenFrame/ScreenCell rendering, removed quadrant glyph packing for this path, and render live cells as 2x2 full-block tiles so canonical glider silhouettes survive terminals with row gaps or odd block glyph metrics. Verification: nix develop -c cargo fmt --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml --all; nix develop -c cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core front_door_render::tests -- --nocapture.
Second framebuffer slice: replaced 2x2 full-block Game of Life renderer with braille-packed cells. Each live cell now paints a 2x2 braille-dot tile, preserving glider shape without making welcome-screen gliders double-height. Also removed the package-breaking Home Manager option metadata integration test from the Cargo default test set; the stronger Nix-eval metadata check remains in the maintainer config-surface validator. Verification: nix develop -c cargo fmt --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml --all; nix develop -c cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core front_door_render::tests -- --nocapture; nix build .#runtime --no-link; nix develop -c cargo run --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core --bin yzx_repo_validator -- validate-config-surface-contract.
Third renderer slice: restored the old Nushell Game of Life visual contract after braille proved too tiny and still visually gappy. Rust now uses grid_width = inner_width / 2, grid_height = body_height, one life row per terminal row, and two full-block glyphs per live cell. Added regressions for the full-block glider silhouette and no inserted renderer rows. Verification: nix develop -c cargo fmt --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml --all; nix develop -c cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core front_door_render::tests -- --nocapture; nix build .#runtime --no-link.
Fourth renderer slice: added a real core.game_of_life_cell_style option with full_block default and dotted as a hardcoded scale-4 braille texture. Both styles keep the same two-terminal-columns by one-terminal-row footprint, so dotted cannot shrink gliders or insert empty rows. Also fixed yzx screen raw alternate-screen output by disabling autowrap while in screen mode and rendering each frame with absolute row cursor addresses instead of println newlines; this targets the every-other-row skipping seen only in yzx screen. Verification: nix develop -c cargo fmt --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml --all; nix develop -c cargo fmt --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml --all -- --check; nix develop -c cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core front_door_render; nix develop -c cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core normalizes_default_config_with_parser_keys_and_transforms; nix develop -c cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core --test yzx_core_owned_facts startup_facts_compute_reports_retained_startup_and_session_fields; nix develop -c cargo run --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core --bin yzx_repo_validator -- validate-config-surface-contract.
close_reason: Completed: current front-door renderer uses the Rust framebuffer/screen-frame contract for Game of Life, preserves glider footprint without inserted row gaps, supports full_block/dotted styles, and yzx screen writes absolute rows without newline-driven skipping.

title: Normalize Rust formatting across both Rust workspaces
notes:
Rust dependency gate: no dependency changes. This is a repo-wide rustfmt normalization only. Production crates unchanged, dev-only crates unchanged, no in-house logic added, no rejected alternatives relevant beyond using rustfmt via the existing maintainer shell/toolchain.
Ran full rustfmt normalization with nix develop -c cargo fmt for rust_core and rust_plugins/zellij_pane_orchestrator, then verified with cargo fmt --check on both workspaces. This also cleared the immediate drift that the new file-scoped prek hooks would have flagged in touched files.

Core hook: rustfmt --edition 2024 --check for ^rust_core/.*\.rs$. Pane orchestrator hook: rustfmt --edition 2021 --check for ^rust_plugins/zellij_pane_orchestrator/.*\.rs$. Updated docs/yazelix_collection.md to recommend  for incremental checks instead of . Verification: core file check passed for rust_core/yazelix_core/src/front_door_render.rs; plugin hook command correctly surfaced existing formatting debt in rust_plugins/zellij_pane_orchestrator/src/active_tab_session_state.rs.
close_reason: Completed

title: Move sidebar Yazi identity fully into the pane orchestrator
description:
Why this issue exists and what needs to be done

The current workspace model still keeps a sidebar Yazi cache on disk because the pane orchestrator does not fully own Yazi instance identity and cwd. That cache is explicitly treated as an integration cache in the workspace contract, which means the architecture already wants it to be secondary rather than permanent.

Explore the path to making sidebar Yazi identity and cwd fully plugin-owned so the filesystem cache can be deleted or reduced to a narrow fallback. The goal is to simplify the workspace model and remove another source of state drift between the live session and the cache layer.
acceptance_criteria: Sidebar Yazi pane identity and cwd are owned or derived by the pane orchestrator; any remaining filesystem cache is narrow and secondary; tab-locality is defended so one tab cannot pick another tab sidebar state by recency; current Yazi file tree behavior remains unchanged for users.
notes: 2026-04-26 implementation start: no new crates. Use existing pane-orchestrator/session-state Rust surfaces; keep current user Yazi behavior unchanged while removing recency-based sidebar cache as the primary identity source.
close_reason: Completed: sidebar Yazi identity/cwd now publish only into the pane orchestrator; the old filesystem identity cache was removed from the Yazi adapter and the ownership docs were updated

title: Defend pane-orchestrator sync freshness in maintainer checks
description:
Why this issue exists and what needs to be done

Pane-orchestrator Rust edits are not live until the wasm is rebuilt and synced, but the repo still relies heavily on maintainer discipline to remember that boundary. That makes it easy to ship code changes that are correct in source but stale in tracked or runtime plugin artifacts.

Add a defended maintainer workflow that makes stale pane-orchestrator sync state visible early, ideally through validator coverage or another explicit maintainer check, so source edits and synced wasm paths stop drifting silently.
close_reason: Completed: build_pane_orchestrator --sync now writes a tracked source/wasm sync stamp, validator detects stale source or wasm drift, and maintainer tests run the check before functional suites.

title: Teach yzx doctor to detect workspace asset drift
description:
Why this issue exists and what needs to be done

The workspace layer depends on exact layouts, wrapper scripts, plugin artifacts, and integration files. Today most of that drift only shows up after a live session breaks, or in maintainer-only reasoning.

Extend doctor so it can detect the common workspace asset drift cases directly and explain them in user-facing terms: missing or stale plugin artifacts, missing managed wrapper scripts, missing built-in layouts, and other session-critical workspace asset failures.
close_reason: Implemented workspace asset drift diagnostics in yzx doctor

title: Make built-in layout families machine-readable
description:
Why this issue exists and what needs to be done

Yazelix layout-family behavior is currently real product logic, but most of that contract is still implicit across KDL files, Rust plugin code, and docs. That makes sidebar-family evolution easy to break and hard to validate mechanically.

Introduce one maintained machine-readable contract for the built-in layout families and use it to validate the tracked KDL files and the pane-orchestrator expectations, so layout-family work stops depending on scattered string knowledge.
close_reason: Added machine-readable built-in Zellij layout family contract

title: Add maintainer session-state inspect surface
description:
Why this issue exists and what needs to be done

The workspace/session model is now explicitly plugin-owned, but there is still no first-class maintainer inspection surface that prints the active tab session state in one deliberate format. Debugging workspace bugs still pushes maintainers toward ad hoc pipes, raw plugin responses, or code reading.

Add a maintainer-facing inspect command that dumps the active workspace/session snapshot clearly enough to debug managed pane identity, focus context, workspace root, layout/sidebar state, and sidebar Yazi identity without bespoke probes.
close_reason: Added yzx dev inspect_session backed by pane-orchestrator session state

title: Add startup handoff context capture for repair-path crashes
description:
Why this issue exists and what needs to be done

 points at a one-time post-welcome startup crash that likely lives in the repair-then-handoff seam. Right now the repo has runtime logs and profiling, but it does not leave behind one compact, durable startup-context artifact focused on the exact handoff inputs when generated-state repair just ran.

Add a targeted startup-context capture path for repair-driven launches so the next failure leaves behind the working directory, layout path, session mode, materialization outcome, and other handoff facts needed to diagnose the crash without guessing from partial logs.
close_reason: Completed: repair-driven startup now records a durable handoff JSON artifact with working dir, layout, shell, session, and materialization facts before the Zellij handoff.

title: Add workspace session contract validator
description:
Why this issue exists and what needs to be done

The live Yazelix workspace layer depends on exact built-in layouts, wrapper script paths, tracked plugin artifacts, and the internal yzx_control zellij subcommand seam. Those contracts are currently defended mostly by runtime behavior and scattered tests, which leaves room for silent repo drift.

Add a repo validator that checks the built-in workspace/session assets and the shipped Yazi/Zellij integration references, centralize the internal zellij subcommand list so help text and validators use one source of truth, and add Rust tests for the validator contract.
notes: Implemented the workspace/session contract validator, wired it into yzx_repo_validator and the static maintainer checks, and added Rust coverage for the internal zellij command surface and bundled Yazi entrypoint validation.
close_reason: Added workspace/session contract validator and maintainer gate

tricks like checking config hooks in .bashrc and .config/nu (or similar)

description:

now that using the sidebar is good and easy, might not make sense disabling it anymore

what could be more useful is a setting for new tabs to always start with the sidebar closed
acceptance_criteria: The enable_sidebar decision preserves current sidebar users unless a replacement option covers the same need; a new-tab default closed/open policy is evaluated; Home Manager and direct config behavior stay in sync; no external user config is taken over automatically.
notes: 2026-04-26 subsystem review: evaluate removal of enable_sidebar only after the sidebar capability boundary is clear. The likely better user option is initial_sidebar_state or start_new_tabs_with_sidebar_closed, not silently deleting sidebar behavior.
close_reason: Completed: replaced the primary sidebar-disable surface with editor.initial_sidebar_state open/closed, kept legacy enable_sidebar as compatibility input, added collapsed managed-sidebar startup layouts, and synced the pane orchestrator so closed-base startup still opens/toggles through the managed sidebar contract.

title: Repair stale GitHub/Beads validator and sync contract
description:
Why this issue exists and what needs to be done:
The GitHub/Beads contract sync path still calls a stale Nushell validator entrypoint that imports a removed module, so the contract cannot be verified or synced cleanly through the maintained flow.

What needs to be done:
Restore the validator entrypoint so it delegates to the current owner, run the sync flow to repair lifecycle drift, and re-verify the contract.
close_reason: Completed

title: Reduce Yazelix Home Manager activation JSON noise
description:
Why this issue exists and what needs to be done:
Home Manager activation currently prints the full runtime-materialization repair JSON payload from Yazelix, which makes successful switches noisy and hard to scan.

What needs to be done:
Update the activation path to emit a concise success summary instead of dumping the full JSON payload while preserving actionable warnings/errors and verify the Home Manager path still succeeds.
close_reason: Completed

title: Clarify Home Manager path-input update semantics and mixed-ownership diagnostics
notes: Documented that yzx update home_manager still matters for path: inputs because flake.lock pins a snapshot, and surfaced the mixed Home Manager/default-profile ownership collision in yzx doctor and yzx home_manager prepare output.

title: Investigate one-time post-welcome startup crash during generated-state repair
notes: User reported a single startup crash on 2026-04-24 after the welcome screen. Before reproduction, 'yzx status' reported generated-state refresh_required. After the failed first launch, status reported generated state up to date, and ~/.local/share/yazelix generated files under configs/yazi and configs/zellij updated at 2026-04-24 11:44. Subsequent launches worked. Working hypothesis: one-time failure in the repair-then-handoff seam after startup welcome, likely around generated-state materialization or the first Zellij handoff after repair. No steady-state startup failure currently reproduces.
close_reason: Completed: the concrete startup handoff context capture work was finished in yazelix-lh93.1, giving future repair-path crashes a durable handoff artifact for diagnosis.

title: Return popup and menu wrappers to Nushell
notes: User requested restoring popup/menu launch trampolines from POSIX sh back to Nushell for consistency/readability. No new crates; in-house path/launcher changes only. Scope: restore nushell/scripts/zellij_wrappers/yzx_popup_program.nu and yzx_menu_popup.nu, point pane orchestrator transient contract back to Nu wrappers, remove POSIX trampolines if unused, update budget/docs, rebuild/sync pane orchestrator wasm, and validate popup/menu transient behavior contract.
close_reason: Restored popup/menu wrappers to Nushell, updated the pane-orchestrator transient contract to the Nu wrapper paths, deleted the POSIX trampolines, ratcheted the canonical Nu budget/docs to 1880 LOC / 14 files, rebuilt/synced the pane orchestrator wasm, and revalidated transient tests plus spec/budget/syntax guards.

title: Replace or explicitly keep the popup, menu, and sidebar Nu wrappers
description: Re-audit the tiny popup/menu/sidebar wrapper files and either replace them with cleaner POSIX/Rust owners or explicitly keep them as the final launch trampolines. Small size alone is not enough justification.
design: If a wrapper only sets fixed env and execs another command, POSIX may be cleaner. If Nu remains the best owner because of environment or caller expectations, record that explicitly and stop treating it as vague future debt.
acceptance_criteria:
1. The popup, menu, and sidebar wrappers are either reduced further or explicitly retained
2. Launch and toggle behavior still works after any change
3. The final allowlist names each surviving wrapper individually
close_reason: Completed at the measured 1844 LOC / 12 file Nushell floor.

title: Decide whether menu.nu and welcome.nu are true Nushell survivors
description: Make an explicit decision on whether `yzx/menu.nu` and `setup/welcome.nu` are true irreducible Nu survivors. These files are interactive and shell-heavy, but they should not remain by default if a cleaner owner exists.
design: Judge them against the survivor contract, not aesthetics. If the remaining ownership is mainly `fzf`, `input listen`, ANSI rendering, or direct interactive tool control, record them as survivors. If the files still carry deterministic metadata or report shaping, cut that first.
acceptance_criteria:
1. The bead records whether `menu.nu` and `welcome.nu` stay or shrink further
2. Any retained ownership is tied to the irreducible-core contract, not habit
3. Any further deletions discovered here are spun into implementation work rather than left implicit

title: Reduce setup/environment.nu to shellhook and env mutation only
description: Reduce `setup/environment.nu` to shellhook and environment mutation only. It still mixes generated extern sync, runtime chmod policy, welcome routing, logging policy, and startup-facts interpretation that should be minimized or pushed below the Nu boundary.
design: Keep only the shellhook work that truly needs Nu env mutation. Everything else should either move into Rust-owned startup/setup helpers or into tiny fixed scripts.
acceptance_criteria:
1. `environment.nu` reads as shellhook/env setup only
2. Startup setup behavior still works for installer, maintainer shell, and normal startup entrypoints
3. The remaining file does not duplicate policy already owned elsewhere
close_reason: Completed at the measured 1844 LOC / 12 file Nushell floor.

title: Delete duplicate startup policy from start_yazelix_inner.nu
description: Delete duplicate startup policy from `core/start_yazelix_inner.nu`, especially the remaining failure classification, startup-facts handling, materialization orchestration, and attach-versus-launch shaping that do not need to live in Nushell.
design: Nu should consume final startup facts and perform the last shell-boundary actions, not re-derive policy. Push deterministic startup decisions down and keep only the narrow handoff to `zellij`.
acceptance_criteria:
1. `start_yazelix_inner.nu` is reduced to the final startup shell boundary
2. Duplicate startup policy and formatting no longer live in Nu
3. Warm, cold, and persistent-session startup behavior remain defended
close_reason: Completed at the measured 1844 LOC / 12 file Nushell floor.

title: Reduce plugin_build.nu and sweep helpers to pure execution seams
description: Re-audit `maintainer/plugin_build.nu` plus the remaining sweep helper files after the larger maintainer cuts. Keep only the minimal shell ownership required for cargo/wasm builds, syncing, and external process control.
design: Delete helper glue that only exists because earlier Nu owners existed. Prefer fixed POSIX helpers or Rust-owned planning over medium-sized Nu scripts whose main job is command assembly.
acceptance_criteria:
1. `plugin_build.nu` and the sweep helper floor are reduced to genuine execution seams
2. Pane-orchestrator build/sync still works through the supported maintainer flow
3. No stale helper debt survives just because it is already written in Nu

title: Move update_workflow.nu policy below the shell boundary
description: Move deterministic update-workflow planning out of `maintainer/update_workflow.nu`. That file still owns pin extraction, package/version shaping, canary planning, activation selection, and report formatting that do not inherently need Nushell.
design: Keep only the true shell boundary around `nix`, `git`, Home Manager, and filesystem mutation. The logic that decides what to run, how to classify results, and how to shape the final report should move below that boundary.
acceptance_criteria:
1. `update_workflow.nu` loses deterministic planning and report shaping
2. The maintainer update path still supports canaries and activation modes
3. The surviving Nu code reads like shell orchestration, not policy code

title: Collapse yzx/dev.nu to dispatch-only maintainer shims
description: Shrink `yzx/dev.nu` until it is only a thin command shim or remove subcommands that no longer need Nushell at all. The current file still carries command assembly, profiling harness policy, and maintainer routing that can move below the shell boundary.
design: Keep only a minimal public shell surface for commands that must live in Nu. Move scenario planning, report printing, and argument shaping into Rust-owned maintainer binaries where that deletes real Nu ownership.
acceptance_criteria:
1. `yzx/dev.nu` is materially smaller or thinner
2. Maintainer subcommands still work through the supported public surface
3. No deleted policy is recreated in another broad Nu helper

title: Delete JSON envelope and error-surface ownership from yzx_core_bridge.nu
description: Delete the JSON envelope parsing, structured error rendering, and transport policy that still lives in `utils/yzx_core_bridge.nu`. Nu should not keep reshaping Rust-owned envelopes or reclassifying deterministic failures.
design: Prefer direct Rust command surfaces whose stdout/stderr contract is already final for the caller. If a tiny Nu transport helper survives, it should pass argv/env through and little else.
acceptance_criteria:
1. `yzx_core_bridge.nu` loses broad JSON/error/report shaping logic
2. Rust-owned command surfaces present final structured results or final user-facing failures
3. Any remaining Nu bridge code is visibly transport-only

title: Move runtime-root and helper discovery below the Nushell boundary
description: Delete the runtime-root inference and helper-discovery policy that still lives in `utils/runtime_paths.nu` and adjacent helper-resolution code. The Nu surface should stop guessing where the runtime is, which helper binary to use, and whether a source checkout or installed runtime should win.
design: Prefer one Rust-owned runtime contract that returns the resolved paths Nu actually needs, or a fixed executable path surface that avoids re-derivation. Keep only the minimum shell-facing path expansion that truly cannot leave Nu.
acceptance_criteria:
1. `runtime_paths.nu` no longer owns broad runtime/helper resolution policy
2. Source-checkout versus installed-runtime precedence is owned below the Nu boundary
3. The surviving Nu path surface is tiny and directly justified

title: Ratchet the canonical Nushell budget to the irreducible core
description: After the deletion work lands, remeasure `nushell/scripts/**/*.nu`, lower the canonical ceiling again, and record the final irreducible allowlist. This is the closeout bead for the irreducible-core tranche.
design: Treat the measured allowlist as the source of truth. Do not keep a loose ceiling for convenience. If a nonzero survivor floor remains, record the exact files and why they still qualify as irreducible rather than leaving another fuzzy target behind.
acceptance_criteria:
1. The canonical Nushell budget and spec/docs reflect the new measured floor
2. The surviving file allowlist is explicit and defended by the irreducible-core decision
3. The epic closes with one clear stop condition or a lower final ceiling, not another vague follow-up target
notes: Closed 2026-04-24: canonical Nu budget ratcheted to the measured allowlist at 1844 LOC / 12 files. Exact surviving files and family ceilings now live in config_metadata/nushell_budget.toml plus docs/specs/provable_nushell_floor_budget.md, docs/specs/maintainer_and_validator_nushell_budget.md, docs/specs/runtime_shell_floor_budgets.md, and docs/specs/front_door_and_data_nushell_budget.md.

title: Audit front-door UI and wrapper survivors for final Nu retention

title: Collapse maintainer and dev orchestration to thin shell shims
description: The largest remaining Nu block is maintainer and dev orchestration: `maintainer/update_workflow.nu`, `maintainer/plugin_build.nu`, `yzx/dev.nu`, and the sweep helper files. Delete deterministic planning, pin/version shaping, report generation, and command dispatch policy from that surface.
design: Move deterministic maintainer logic into `yzx_repo_maintainer` or another Rust owner. Keep Nushell or POSIX only where it is genuinely acting as the shell boundary for `nix`, `git`, `cargo`, wasm sync, or other external tool execution. Thin wrappers are acceptable; policy-heavy orchestrators are not.
acceptance_criteria:
1. `yzx/dev.nu` becomes a thin maintainer surface or disappears for commands that no longer need Nu
2. `update_workflow.nu` and `plugin_build.nu` lose deterministic policy that can live below the shell boundary
3. The maintainer/dev Nu family drops materially without regressing the supported workflows
notes:
Rust dependency gate 2026-04-24: keep maintainer-owner cuts in-house with existing std + toml + serde_json only. No new crates. Use repo_maintainer plus existing active_config_surface, config_normalize, runtime_materialization, and zellij_materialization modules instead of shell-heavy Nu policy.
Closed 2026-04-24: update_workflow.nu, update_yazi_plugins.nu, plugin_build.nu, config_sweep_runner.nu, and the dev/sweep helpers are deleted. Rust now owns update workflow policy, vendored Yazi refresh, pane-orchestrator build/sync, sweep planning, and nu-lint execution. The surviving maintainer Nu floor is yzx/dev.nu at 425 LOC, retained for public routing plus the startup-profile shell harness.

title: Collapse the runtime helper bridge to a transport-only seam

design: This is not a request to invent a new abstraction layer. Prefer direct Rust binaries or library-owned contracts that Nu can call without re-parsing envelopes or re-deriving paths. Keep only truly shell-specific helpers in Nu, and prefer deletion over moving logic sideways into another helper file.
acceptance_criteria:
1. `yzx_core_bridge.nu` and `runtime_paths.nu` no longer own broad deterministic policy
2. Runtime/helper resolution and structured error/report shaping move below the Nu boundary
3. The surviving helper surface is transport-only and explicitly justified

Closed 2026-04-24: runtime path/helper policy shrank to a measured 611 LOC across six Nu helper files. Dead helper modules config_files.nu, logging.nu, and terminal_assets.nu are deleted. runtime_paths.nu and yzx_core_bridge.nu were trimmed so the remaining helper floor is explicit env/path transport consumed by the surviving shell owners.
