acceptance_criteria: 1. If a family is chosen, the public-owner deletion lane is implemented or decomposed honestly. 2. If no family is chosen, the bead closes with the explicit stop condition. 3. The result either shrinks the direct yzx.rs route table materially or proves why that cannot happen yet.
notes:
Implementation lane opened with `yzx home_manager` as the chosen public-family cut. Scope for completion:
- add Rust control-plane ownership for `yzx home_manager` and `yzx home_manager prepare`
- preserve preview output, empty-state output, `--apply`, `--yes`, timestamped archive suffixes, and next-step guidance
- route the home_manager family through Rust control in the shared command surface
- delete `nushell/scripts/yzx/home_manager.nu` after parity is defended
Implemented the chosen public-family cut:
- added Rust control-plane ownership for `yzx home_manager` and `yzx home_manager prepare`
- extracted a shared `install_ownership_request_from_env` helper so `yzx update` and `yzx home_manager` consume the same install-ownership report request path
- moved the shared public command surface to route `home_manager` through Rust control instead of direct Nu modules
- deleted `nushell/scripts/yzx/home_manager.nu` and removed its runtime-contract expectation
- updated focused CLI tests so the defended contract now runs through the public `yzx` route, not a direct Nushell import
- updated migration docs/spec notes so `yzx home_manager` is no longer described as a surviving Nu owner
close_reason: Completed. `yzx home_manager` is now Rust-owned end to end at the public owner layer, and the surviving Nushell family owner was deleted.

title: Choose the next public family or record a no-go
description: Choose the next public yzx family to migrate, or record an explicit no-go if no family currently makes sense.
design: Use the deletion budget from the previous bead. The answer may be a concrete family or a no-go, but it must be honest. Do not force a family just to keep a Clap story alive.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Records one concrete next family or an explicit no-go. 2. Names the exact public-owner seam to delete if a family is chosen. 3. Keeps no-go as an acceptable outcome.
notes:
Decision: go with `yzx home_manager` as the next honest public-family Rust owner cut.

Selection criteria applied:
- real end-to-end deletion of a surviving public Nushell owner
- minimal shell-only UI logic that would just migrate wrappers instead of ownership
- meaningful shrink of the remaining direct internal-Nu family surface in the shared public command schema
- no duplication with existing Rust metadata/help/schema ownership

Stop condition for this bead: either `yzx home_manager` becomes Rust-owned and the Nu family owner is deleted, or the implementation reveals a hidden shell-bound blocker serious enough to record a no-go. Current expectation is go.
close_reason: Completed. The go/no-go review stayed go: `yzx home_manager` delivered real public-owner deletion value without creating a second metadata or route registry.

title: Define the deletion budget for the next public yzx family
description: Define the deletion budget for the next public yzx command family beyond env/run/update/status.
design: Compare candidate families by real owner deletion, not by which one is easiest to parse in Rust. The decision should name surviving Nu owners, shell heaviness, renderer heaviness, direct-route table shrink value, and whether the cut would make a future Clap rewrite materially more honest.
acceptance_criteria: 1. The candidate families are compared explicitly. 2. The deletion budget is named in terms of real owner removal. 3. The decision rejects families that are mostly shell-heavy or renderer-heavy without a real deletion story.
notes:
Deletion budget decision:
- Candidate families reviewed: home_manager, doctor, config, plus heavier shell/UI families such as launch, desktop, edit, and import
- Chosen target: `yzx home_manager`
- Why this is the next honest public-family deletion: the install-ownership evaluation and takeover-artifact classification are already Rust-owned in `install_ownership_report`, while the remaining public Nushell owner is a small wrapper plus archive/apply behavior
- Rejected for this lane: `yzx doctor` still fronts a materially larger interactive repair surface; `yzx config` still owns more of its behavior directly and would delete less of an already-Rust seam; launch/desktop/edit/import are too shell-heavy for an honest scoped public-owner cut
- Exact deletion budget: move `yzx home_manager` and `yzx home_manager prepare` into Rust control-plane ownership, preserve preview/apply/archive/confirmation semantics, switch the shared public command surface to Rust control routing for this family, then delete `nushell/scripts/yzx/home_manager.nu` as the public owner
- Rust dependency gate: no new production crates; use std plus existing `yazelix_core` modules because this work is filesystem moves, timestamp formatting, env/config path resolution, and existing install-ownership report consumption rather than domain logic that justifies another crate
close_reason: Completed. The deletion-budget review chose `yzx home_manager` as the next honest public-family Rust owner cut because install-ownership evaluation was already Rust-owned and the remaining Nushell surface was only a thin public wrapper plus archive/apply behavior.

title: Decide the shared public yzx schema owner and dependency gate
description: Decide whether command_metadata.rs should grow into the shared command-surface owner or whether a new shared schema module should exist.
design: This bead must satisfy the Rust dependency gate for later Rust implementation work: list production crates, dev-only crates, in-house logic, rejected alternatives, and packaging impact. Compare at least: extending command_metadata.rs in place; introducing a new shared schema module that feeds both metadata and routing; and generating one surface from the other. The chosen shape must preserve a single source for help, externs, and menu metadata.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Records a crate-vs-in-house decision for the shared-schema lane. 2. Chooses the schema owner shape explicitly. 3. Names the fields the shared schema must represent. 4. Names the current code that should disappear once the schema lands. 5. Rejects any shape that creates a second public command registry.
notes:
2026-04-21 dependency gate decision

Chosen owner shape
- Add a new shared Rust module for the public yzx command surface instead of growing command_metadata.rs in place
- command_metadata.rs should shrink to rendering/export helpers plus extern-bridge sync that consume the shared surface
- yzx.rs route planning should consume the same shared surface instead of keeping a second handwritten registry

Why this shape
- command_metadata.rs already mixes flat public metadata with extern rendering and bridge sync; adding routing policy, owner state, module targets, and subcommand error policy there would make it a grab-bag owner
- A neutral shared surface can feed both metadata renderers and route planning without creating a second registry
- Generating one surface ad hoc from the other still leaves one side authoritative and the other derived by convention instead of by explicit shared structure
- Clap must not become the schema owner; that would pre-approve the parser choice before the delete-first prerequisites land

Crate gate
- Production crates: no new crates; reuse current std, serde, serde_json, sha2, and existing bridge/control-plane crates already in yazelix_core
- Dev-only crates: no new crates required for this lane; reuse the current Rust/Nu test harnesses already in tree
- In-house logic: command-family schema, alias/subcommand matching, internal Nu route planning, help/extern/menu projections
- Rejected alternatives: extend command_metadata.rs directly; make yzx.rs remain authoritative and regenerate metadata from it; make Clap the schema owner; keep separate flat metadata and manual route tables
- Packaging impact: none expected because this is an internal Rust ownership refactor inside the existing yzx/yzx_core helpers

Shared surface fields that must exist
- public command id/full name
- category
- description and optional menu-facing override text
- parameter list
- route owner kind: root-help, root-version, rust-control, or internal-nu
- family root token
- alias/subcommand tokens after the family root
- internal Nu module path when route owner is internal-nu
- concrete exported Nu command name when route owner is internal-nu
- family fallback/error policy for missing or unknown subcommands
- menu visibility/category policy so the palette stops reverse-engineering categories from command names

Current code that should disappear once the shared surface lands
- the hard-coded top-level command allowlist inside classify_root_route in rust_core/yazelix_core/src/bin/yzx.rs
- the manual per-family match tree in plan_internal_nu_route in rust_core/yazelix_core/src/bin/yzx.rs
- the manually curated command vector in yzx_command_metadata() in rust_core/yazelix_core/src/command_metadata.rs
- the menu-side category guessing and command-shape reconstruction in nushell/scripts/yzx/menu.nu where the Rust metadata already knows the command surface

Guardrail
- Do not land any shape that creates a second public command registry or a schema that only covers help/externs while yzx.rs keeps its own route table.
close_reason: Completed

title: Unify the public yzx command-surface schema
description: Create one shared source of truth for the public yzx command surface instead of keeping command_metadata.rs and yzx.rs route definitions as separate surfaces.
design: The shared surface must represent top-level commands, grouped subcommands, aliases, parameter/help shape, route ownership, internal Nu module path and command name where relevant, and error/help policy. Do not lose features or silently widen Rust ownership.
acceptance_criteria: 1. One shared schema owns public command metadata and parser-shape information. 2. yzx.rs route planning can consume that schema without behavior drift. 3. Root help, externs, and menu/palette metadata stay single-source. 4. Hand-written duplicated route tables become deletable.
close_reason: Completed

title: Prepare the public yzx command surface so a future Clap rewrite could be honest

Before Clap is reconsidered, Yazelix needs one shared public command-surface schema, stronger root parity coverage, and either one more real public-family deletion beyond yzx_control or an explicit proof that no such family exists.

Preserve all current command behavior, aliases, grouped subcommands, direct internal Nu module routing, error policy, and argv passthrough. If these prerequisites still do not make Clap deletion-positive, keep the no-go.

A next-family deletion lane exists beyond yzx_control. 4. A future Clap re-evaluation bead is blocked on those prerequisites instead of being assumed ready.
notes:
2026-04-21 progress

Completed the shared-schema and parity-hardening sub-lanes. Remaining work in this epic is the next public-family deletion decision (2p32.3) and the downstream Clap re-evaluation gate (2p32.4).

title: Implement or explicitly reject the scoped Clap root router rewrite
description: After the go/no-go decision and parity harness, either implement the scoped Clap root router rewrite or close with the explicit no-go stop condition.
design: If yazelix-rxym.1 approves Clap, implement only the scoped rewrite in rust_core/yazelix_core/src/bin/yzx.rs and related tests/docs. Preserve all existing command behavior and keep Nu-owned command bodies as explicit internal helper routes. Do not port launch, enter, restart, doctor, menu, edit, import, desktop, dev, tutor, keys, popup, screen, or other Nu-owned command bodies as part of this bead. Do not introduce a second command metadata registry: command_metadata.rs or an explicit successor remains the single source for root help, externs, and menu/palette metadata. If yazelix-rxym.1 records no-go, close this bead by recording the stop condition rather than adding Clap.
acceptance_criteria: 1. If implemented, the hand-rolled parser/help/router code named by rxym.1 is deleted or materially simplified. 2. If not implemented, the bead records the no-go stop condition and leaves code unchanged. 3. No command family loses behavior, aliases, subcommands, direct Nu module routing, or argv passthrough. 4. command_metadata/help/extern/menu ownership remains single-source. 5. Cargo fmt/check/test and the parity tests from rxym.2 pass. 6. Any new Rust tests follow lane and strength-marker rules.
close_reason: Closed without implementation: the scoped Clap rewrite failed the deletion-budget gate and must not add Clap in the current shape.

title: Defend current yzx root parity before any Clap rewrite
description: Add or tighten high-signal parity coverage for the current Rust yzx root router before changing it. The goal is to prevent the Clap rewrite from losing aliases, subcommands, direct module routing, or argv passthrough behavior.
design: Use the parity matrix from yazelix-rxym.1. Prefer focused Rust unit tests for pure parser/planner behavior and existing Nushell CLI probe tests for launcher/direct-module behavior. Do not add weak help-output inventory tests that only duplicate command lists. Defend real behavior: route class, error class/code, direct module path, command_name, tail argv, and rendered Nu invocation quoting for spaces/quotes/--. Keep test lane and strength markers compliant with AGENTS.md.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Current root parser/planner behavior has focused parity coverage before the rewrite. 2. The coverage defends no-arg help, help/version flags, unknown top-level command errors, yzx_control routing, direct internal Nu route planning, grouped subcommands, aliases, required/unknown subcommand errors, and argv passthrough/quoting. 3. Existing direct-module CLI probe tests remain aligned with the route plan. 4. New tests follow lane and strength-marker rules. 5. Cargo test for the yzx root tests and relevant Nushell CLI probe tests pass.
close_reason: Closed without implementation: the go/no-go decision rejected the scoped Clap rewrite, so a pre-rewrite parity harness is not needed for this lane.

title: Define the Clap go/no-go, deletion budget, and metadata ownership
description: Decide whether Clap is justified for the existing Rust-owned yzx root router before implementation. This bead may approve a scoped rewrite or record a no-go if Clap would add dependency/duplication risk without deleting enough current ownership.
design: Record the Rust dependency gate: production crates, dev-only crates, in-house logic, rejected alternatives, packaging impact, and why a broad CLI crate is justified for a public binary if accepted. The decision must compare at least three shapes: keep in-house parsing; Clap for top-level routing only while command_metadata.rs remains the help/extern/menu source; and Clap as, or feeding, the single command metadata source. Name the exact current code in rust_core/yazelix_core/src/bin/yzx.rs that can disappear or shrink: RootRoute/classify_root_route, root help/version flag matching, top-level command-family matching, unknown-command handling, and any duplicated help surface. Also name the code that must stay unless a separate owner cut exists: resolve_rust_control_path, resolve_nu_bin, resolve_internal_nu_module_path, render_nu_command_script/render_nu_arg shell-bound quoting, run_child, and explicit internal Nu module routing for Nu-owned families.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Records a go/no-go decision for Clap, not just an assumption. 2. Lists production crates, dev-only crates, in-house logic, rejected alternatives, and packaging impact. 3. Proves the chosen shape keeps a single source for public command metadata/help/extern/menu data. 4. Lists exact yzx.rs code to delete or shrink and exact code to preserve. 5. Lists every behavior that must remain parity-protected before implementation: no-arg help; help/-h/--help; version flags -V/--version/-v/--version-short; unknown top-level command error; env/run/update/status yzx_control routing; required-subcommand errors for desktop; unknown-subcommand errors for dev/import; config reset, edit config, home_manager prepare, keys aliases, tutor aliases; direct Nu module routing; argv passthrough for spaces, quotes, and --. 6. If the deletion budget is not enough, it defines the stop condition and instructs downstream beads to close without implementation.
notes:
Decision: no-go for a scoped Clap rewrite in the current shape.

Compared shapes:
1. Keep in-house parsing in rust_core/yazelix_core/src/bin/yzx.rs
- Production crates: existing serde, serde_json, thiserror, lexopt in workspace; no new crate required for yzx root
- Dev-only crates: existing assert_cmd and fixture/test crates only
- Logic kept in-house: RootRoute/classify_root_route, explicit NuRoutePlan planning, helper resolution, Nu argv quoting, direct internal Nu route handoff
- Packaging impact: none
- Result: preserves single metadata source and explicit route ownership with no new dependency

2. Clap for top-level routing only while command_metadata.rs remains the help/extern/menu source
- Production crate candidate: clap
- Rejected alternative for now
- Why rejected: deletes classify_root_route and some manual help/version handling, but still requires a parallel route definition for top-level commands, aliases, grouped subcommands, and direct Nu family dispatch. command_metadata.rs would remain a separate public metadata registry, so Clap becomes a second command surface description instead of a real owner cut.
- Packaging impact: adds a broad public-CLI dependency for limited deletion

3. Clap as, or feeding, the single metadata source
- Rejected as out of scope
- Why rejected: would require redesigning command_metadata.rs, render_yzx_help, yzx-command-metadata.externs, and menu/palette metadata around Clap or a new shared schema. That is no longer a scoped router rewrite; it is a broader public command metadata ownership rewrite.

Deletion budget outcome:
- Code that could shrink with Clap: RootRoute/classify_root_route, root help/version flag matching, top-level command-family matching, some unknown-command handling, and some required/unknown subcommand matching inside plan_internal_nu_route
- Code that must remain unless a separate owner cut exists: resolve_rust_control_path, resolve_nu_bin, resolve_internal_nu_module_path, render_nu_string_literal/render_nu_arg/render_nu_command_script, run_child, and the explicit internal Nu module routing for config/cwd/desktop/dev/doctor/edit/enter/home_manager/import/keys/launch/menu/popup/restart/reveal/screen/sponsor/tutor/whats_new/why
- Net result: not enough real deletion. The surviving direct-route table and single-source metadata constraint dominate the surface.

Required parity surface if revisited later:
- no-arg help
- help/-h/--help
- version flags -V/--version/-v/--version-short
- unknown top-level command error class/code
- env/run/update/status Rust routing
- required-subcommand errors for desktop
- unknown-subcommand errors for dev/import
- config reset, edit config, home_manager prepare
- keys aliases, tutor aliases
- direct Nu module routing
- argv passthrough for spaces, quotes, and --

Explicit stop condition:
Do not add Clap unless a future plan also deletes the separate route-definition duplication by making command_metadata.rs or an explicit successor the single source for both public metadata and parser shape, or otherwise removes a materially larger portion of the direct internal route table than this scoped lane can.
close_reason: Completed: recorded a no-go decision because a scoped Clap rewrite would duplicate command metadata and explicit route definitions without deleting enough current ownership.

title: Evaluate scoped Clap ownership for the Rust yzx root router
description: Evaluate whether Clap should replace the hand-rolled public Rust yzx root router, and implement only if it deletes enough parser/help/router code without duplicating command metadata or weakening explicit internal Nu route boundaries. The scope is the existing Rust root surface in rust_core/yazelix_core/src/bin/yzx.rs: help/version, yzx_control-backed leaves, yzx status, and explicit internal Nu routes for intentionally Nu-owned families.

It must first prove that Clap removes meaningful hand-rolled parser/help/router ownership without creating a second public command registry beside command_metadata.rs. If Clap only becomes a nicer shim over the same manual route table, or if it obscures direct Nu helper routing, record a no-go and close the implementation bead instead of adding the dependency. Preserve all current user-visible behavior: root help/version, usage errors, env/run/update/status Rust routing, direct internal Nu module routes, aliases, subcommands, and argv passthrough/quoting.
acceptance_criteria: 1. The lane records a go/no-go decision for Clap before implementation. 2. The decision names the exact parser/help/router code that can disappear and the exact code that must remain. 3. The lane keeps command_metadata.rs or an explicit successor as the single public command metadata source; no parallel Clap-only registry is allowed. 4. Nu-owned command bodies remain explicit internal routes and are not silently reclassified as Rust-owned. 5. A parity harness exists before any router rewrite. 6. If Clap is not deletion-positive, the implementation bead closes with the stop condition instead of adding the crate.

Implementation contract from `yazelix-q0o9.1`: delete `core/materialization_orchestrator.nu` only by making Rust own env/request construction for plan/materialize/repair. Do not inline duplicate Nu context builders into startup, doctor, and doctor-report callers. Keep the startup profile boundary comparable as `materialization_orchestrator/materialize_runtime_state`.
Rust dependency gate for q0o9.2: production crates: no new crates; keep existing serde/serde_json/lexopt usage already used by yzx_core command entrypoints. Dev-only crates: no new crates. Build in-house: env-derived materialization plan/materialize/repair request construction using existing control_plane.rs helpers and std path/env logic. Rejected alternatives: shelling back into Nushell to build JSON, adding a new wrapper crate, or introducing a template/config framework for this narrow request-construction cut. Packaging impact: none expected beyond the existing shipped libexec/yzx_core helper; command surface stays private helper-only.
Implemented: deleted `nushell/scripts/core/materialization_orchestrator.nu`; added `--from-env` support to private `yzx_core runtime-materialization.plan`, `.materialize`, and `.repair` commands using Rust `control_plane.rs` request construction; moved startup and doctor progress/error rendering into their caller files; repointed doctor runtime reporting, generated-config tests, and maintainer update canaries to direct Rust helper calls. Verification: validate_syntax, validate_specs, cargo fmt --check, cargo check -p yazelix_core, cargo test runtime_materialization, generated config canonical tests, doctor --fix missing-layout regression, maintainer startup profile materialization-boundary regression, update canaries for default and shell_layout, and git diff --check.

Surviving Nu responsibilities after the cut: startup keeps the profile wrapper and user-facing pre-Zellij failure message; doctor keeps `--fix` progress/error reporting; doctor report keeps the structured runtime findings call and reads the Rust-computed layout path; maintainer update canaries keep a stable force-repair path without importing a product-side shared Nu bridge. Verification for implementation: generated config canonical materialization tests, `yzx doctor --fix` missing-managed-layout repair regression, maintainer startup-profile materialization boundary regression, update-canary forced repair path, validate_syntax, validate_specs, and git diff --check.

- added `nushell/scripts/utils/yzx_core_bridge.nu` as the single minimal Nu owner for generic `yzx_core` helper path resolution, argv/JSON transport, and shared error-surface rendering

title: Collapse the remaining yzx_core and yzx_control bridge owners

title: Avoid stale repo-checkout yzx_core helper selection during profiling
description: Repo-checkout helper resolution can prefer a stale local rust_core/target/release/yzx_core even when that binary is older than the current helper contract. During the v15.3 startup-profile comparison, `yzx dev profile` from the repo checkout failed because the stale release helper rejected `runtime-materialization.materialize`; the same profiling run worked immediately when `YAZELIX_YZX_CORE_BIN` was forced to the current debug helper. This should not require tribal knowledge or a manual env override.
design: Delete first. Do not add another override layer or profiling-only special case. Fix helper resolution so repo-checkout flows choose the freshest compatible built helper instead of blindly preferring target/release when it exists. The likely seam is nushell/scripts/utils/config_parser.nu::get_source_checkout_yzx_core_helper_path / resolve_yzx_core_helper_path. Keep the packaged runtime helper first when it exists, keep YAZELIX_YZX_CORE_BIN as the explicit override, and then make the source-checkout fallback robust against stale debug-vs-release drift. A good solution either proves helper compatibility before choosing a candidate or prefers the newest built candidate rather than the first path in a fixed list.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Repo-checkout commands such as `yzx dev profile` do not fail just because rust_core/target/release/yzx_core is stale while another current local helper exists. 2. The source-checkout fallback no longer blindly prefers target/release over a newer compatible debug build. 3. The fix does not require users to set YAZELIX_YZX_CORE_BIN manually for normal repo work. 4. A focused regression defends the stale-release/current-debug failure mode.
notes:
Implemented 2026-04-21:
- Updated nushell/scripts/utils/config_parser.nu so source-checkout fallback chooses the freshest local yzx_core build by modification time instead of blindly preferring rust_core/target/release/yzx_core. This fixes the profiled repo-checkout failure mode where an older release binary rejected newer helper commands while a current debug build existed.
- Aligned nushell/scripts/dev/yzx_test_helpers.nu::resolve_test_yzx_core_bin with the same freshest-local-helper rule so maintainer tests do not keep selecting a stale release artifact.
- Added a focused regression in nushell/scripts/dev/test_yzx_generated_configs.nu that installs a stale failing release helper plus a newer working debug helper and proves both resolve_yzx_core_helper_path and run_yzx_core_request_json_command use the newer debug build without requiring YAZELIX_YZX_CORE_BIN.

Verification:
- nix develop -c nu -c source nushell/scripts/dev/test_yzx_generated_configs.nu; test_run_yzx_core_request_prefers_newer_source_checkout_helper_over_stale_release\n- nix develop -c nu nushell/scripts/dev/test_yzx_generated_configs.nu\n- nix develop -c nu nushell/scripts/dev/validate_syntax.nu\n- yzx dev profile\n- git diff --check
close_reason: Completed: source-checkout helper selection now prefers the freshest local yzx_core build and the stale release/current debug regression is covered.

title: Fix maintainer-shell extern bridge bootstrap warning
description: The maintainer shell emitted a startup warning while regenerating the Nushell yzx extern bridge. The first shellHook pass was resolving the packaged runtime tree, so extern bridge sync used the packaged libexec/yzx_core instead of the explicit maintainer helper override.
design: Delete first. Fix the bootstrap ownership seam in maintainer_shell.nix so noninteractive startup probes resolve the live checkout runtime before bridge sync runs. Keep those probes on --no-config-file because they are bootstrap steps, not interactive shells. Do not paper over the warning with retries or fallback suppression.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Fresh nix develop startup no longer emits the yzx extern bridge warning. 2. The maintainer shell exports the live checkout as YAZELIX_RUNTIME_DIR when launched from the repo. 3. Focused extern-bridge behavior checks still pass.
notes:
Root cause: the maintainer shell hook was bootstrapping against the packaged runtime tree on first shell entry. During that first pass, get_yazelix_runtime_dir resolved the installed /nix/store runtime, so resolve_yzx_core_helper_path chose that runtime's libexec/yzx_core before the explicit maintainer helper override. That packaged helper rejected the newer yzx command-metadata bridge command surface and emitted the startup warning.

Fix: 1) maintainer_shell.nix now pins YAZELIX_RUNTIME_DIR to the live checkout root before runtime-env bootstrap when the current working tree is a real repo root, so shellHook startup and later live-shell calls resolve the same runtime owner. 2) the shellHook Nu probes stay on --no-config-file because they are noninteractive bootstrap steps. 3) the generated extern bridge now reads extern_content from yzx-command-metadata.list, and the redundant yzx-command-metadata.externs helper surface was removed from yzx_core, tests, and docs.

Validation: nix develop -c bash -lc 'printf ok\\n; printf runtime=%s\\n "$YAZELIX_RUNTIME_DIR"'; nix develop -c cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml --test yzx_core_command_metadata; nix develop -c nu -c 'source nushell/scripts/dev/test_shell_managed_config_contracts.nu; test_yzx_extern_bridge_reuses_current_fingerprint; test_yzx_extern_bridge_keeps_previous_bridge_when_refresh_fails'; nix develop -c nu nushell/scripts/dev/validate_specs.nu; git diff --check; rg -n "yzx-command-metadata\\.externs" -S . (no matches).
close_reason: Completed: maintainer-shell bootstrap now resolves the live checkout runtime before extern bridge sync, so fresh nix develop startup no longer emits the stale yzx_core warning.

title: Decide whether yzx doctor can be the next public Rust owner cut
description: After env/run/update/status, doctor is the only remaining public family that looks even plausibly close to a real Rust public-owner cut. Verify whether doctor can move without carrying the current Nushell doctor owner cluster forward in parallel.
design: Audit the surviving doctor owners in core/yazelix.nu, utils/doctor.nu, doctor_helix_report.nu, doctor_runtime_report.nu, install_ownership_report.nu, and any fix or repair helpers they call. A good outcome is either: 1) a concrete deletion budget for a future public Rust doctor cut, or 2) an explicit no-go decision that doctor is still too mixed. Do not count a Rust parser above the same doctor.nu tree as progress.
acceptance_criteria: 1. The bead names the exact Nushell doctor owners that would have to disappear or materially shrink for a public Rust doctor cut to count. 2. It states clearly whether doctor is actually the next viable mixed public family or not. 3. If viable, it names the parity harness and remaining Nushell rendering/fix roles. If not viable, it records the blocker owners explicitly.
notes:
2026-04-21 audit outcome:
- No-go for v15.x as the next public Rust owner cut.
- The public wrapper at nushell/scripts/core/yzx_doctor.nu is only 25 lines, so a Rust-root doctor move would mostly delete a tiny parser shim rather than a real family owner.
- The meaningful surviving doctor ownership is still Nushell-side: nushell/scripts/utils/doctor.nu (365 lines) owns family assembly, summary building, human rendering, and the interactive --fix flow; doctor_helix_report.nu (80 lines), doctor_runtime_report.nu (96 lines), and install_ownership_report.nu (89 lines) still shape requests and package Rust findings for the family.
- Rust already owns most typed evaluation behind the family: doctor-config.evaluate, doctor-helix.evaluate, doctor-runtime.evaluate, and install-ownership.evaluate. The remaining deletion budget is therefore not "move yzx doctor to Rust" but "delete or split the remaining doctor.nu report/fix owner cluster."
- A future public Rust doctor cut is only worth reopening if the family is first split into: 1) a read-only structured report owner that materially shrinks or deletes utils/doctor.nu and the report shims, and 2) an explicitly Nushell-owned interactive repair surface for --fix and human rendering. Until then, moving the public parser would add another layer above the same mixed family.
- Parity harness if reopened later: keep nushell/scripts/dev/test_yzx_doctor_commands.nu and the helix/runtime doctor contract tests as the family-level regression suite.
close_reason: Completed: audited doctor ownership and recorded a v15.x no-go decision because the real family remains a mixed Nushell report and fix owner cluster.

title: Split the remaining core/yazelix.nu owner lump into explicit internal families
description: core/yazelix.nu is no longer the public yzx registry, but it still owns a mixed internal cluster: doctor, restart, cwd, reveal, why, and sponsor. That lump makes later public Rust family cuts fuzzier than they need to be. Split the remaining internal command owners so future CLI decisions act on honest families instead of one catch-all module.
design: Keep this delete-first. Do not add new Rust ownership here. Move the surviving internal command bodies behind explicit modules or thin family entrypoints so yzx_internal_dispatch.nu no longer relies on core/yazelix.nu as a mixed owner bucket. The goal is clearer command-family boundaries, not framework churn.
acceptance_criteria: 1. core/yazelix.nu no longer serves as a mixed internal owner for unrelated command families. 2. yzx_internal_dispatch.nu routes the surviving Nushell-owned families through explicit family modules or clearly bounded entrypoints. 3. Later public Rust family beads can name exact internal Nu owners without referring to the old catch-all module.
notes:
Split the remaining mixed core/yazelix.nu owner into explicit internal families: core/yzx_support.nu (why/sponsor), core/yzx_workspace.nu (cwd/reveal), core/yzx_session.nu (restart), and core/yzx_doctor.nu (doctor). Updated yzx_internal_dispatch.nu to route those families directly instead of importing the old catch-all module. Rewrote the CLI rewrite and migration docs so later Rust beads name the real surviving Nu owners. Also updated the stale reveal regression to defend the new internal-dispatch contract instead of the pre-Rust-root helper path.

Validation: nix develop -c nu nushell/scripts/dev/validate_syntax.nu; nix develop -c nu nushell/scripts/dev/validate_specs.nu; nix develop -c nu -c 'source nushell/scripts/dev/test_yzx_workspace_commands.nu; test_yzx_cwd_requires_zellij'; nix develop -c nu -c 'source nushell/scripts/dev/test_yzx_workspace_commands.nu; test_doctor_fix_repairs_missing_managed_generated_layout'; nix develop -c nu -c 'source nushell/scripts/dev/test_yzx_maintainer.nu; test_home_manager_profile_restart_uses_owner_wrapper_without_manual_surfaces'; nix develop -c nu -c 'source nushell/scripts/dev/test_yzx_workspace_commands.nu; test_yzx_cli_reveal_routes_through_internal_dispatch'; nix develop -c nu -c 'use "nushell/scripts/core/yazelix.nu" *; yzx why'; git diff --check
close_reason: Completed: split the mixed core/yazelix.nu owner into explicit internal Nu families and updated the ownership docs/tests to match.

title: Audit remaining public yzx families after the first Rust root cut
description: Verify yazelix-2jkb against the actual post-qsb5 codebase. Audit the public Rust root, internal Nu dispatcher, and the remaining command-family owners so later public CLI work is driven by real deletion budgets instead of stale rewrite language.
design: Use the live code, not old planning assumptions. Read rust_core/yazelix_core/src/bin/yzx.rs, nushell/scripts/core/yzx_internal_dispatch.nu, core/yazelix.nu, and the doctor/launch families. Classify each remaining public family as either: 1) plausible next mixed Rust cut, 2) explicit no-go for now, or 3) likely Nushell survivor. Also name the better near-term Rust deletion lanes outside the public CLI epic when those are stronger than another root cut.
acceptance_criteria: 1. The audit names the remaining public families after qsb5 and x3u2 using the current code, not stale registry assumptions. 2. It identifies at least one plausible next mixed Rust candidate or states explicitly that none are ready. 3. It records the major no-go families and the better non-CLI Rust deletion lanes if they are stronger next moves.
notes:
2026-04-20 audit outcome:
- Confirmed that qsb5 is complete and should be treated as landed prerequisite work, not a still-open gate
- Confirmed that the public Rust root now owns env/run/update/status via yzx.rs + yzx_control, and nushell_externs.nu is only startup compatibility glue
- Remaining public family classification:
  - Closest plausible next mixed Rust cut: doctor
  - Explicit no-go for now: launch/enter/restart
  - Likely Nushell survivors: config/edit/import, menu/popup/screen/keys/tutor/whats_new, desktop/home_manager/dev, why/sponsor
- Structural cleanup before later public cuts: split the remaining core/yazelix.nu owner lump so doctor/restart/cwd/reveal stop riding together

close_reason: Completed: audited the live post-qsb5 public command surface, identified doctor as the only plausible next mixed Rust family, marked launch and interactive UX families as no-go or Nushell survivors, and redirected near-term Rust effort toward stronger non-CLI deletion lanes where appropriate.

title: Audit and clean stale in-progress tracker state after the Rust rewrite commit burst
description: Recent Rust/Nushell migration work landed as a large local commit burst with many beads closed correctly, but the tracker still shows multiple simultaneous in-progress issues and the worktree currently mixes distinct lanes \(finished pane-orchestrator cleanup plus active status migration and unrelated local changes\). Audit the remaining in-progress claims and bring Beads/worktree state back into an honest shape.
design: Keep this delete-first. Do not create more umbrella planning. First identify which in-progress beads are genuinely active versus stale claims, then either close them, unclaim/update them with explicit remaining work, or create a narrow follow-up bead if the current scope has split. Separate finished-but-uncommitted code from unrelated active lanes so future commits map cleanly back to Beads.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Every current in-progress bead is either confirmed active with current notes or moved out of in-progress. 2. Finished local work and active follow-up work are split into clear commit lanes or explicitly documented as intentionally mixed. 3. The cleanup leaves a trustworthy next-work picture in Beads for the Rust rewrite sequence.
notes: Cleanup result: reopened stale in-progress claims `yazelix-0dra`, `yazelix-ueaf`, `yazelix-tlh0`, `yazelix-kgb`, and `yazelix-2k9` with explicit notes about what remains. Confirmed `yazelix-x3u2` as the only currently active non-cleanup lane in the dirty tree. Current worktree mapping is now documented honestly: pane-orchestrator/session-truth cleanup files are finished-but-uncommitted, status-migration files belong to `x3u2`, and unrelated local changes still need separation before the next commit.
close_reason: Completed: stale in-progress claims were reopened with explicit remaining work, x3u2 was confirmed as the only active implementation lane, and the mixed worktree state is now documented honestly

title: Non-Ghostty terminal configs and orchestrator materialization in Rust

This deletes the remaining non-Ghostty terminal config Nushell owners and the dispatcher surface. Only start after Ghostty materialization proves the pattern.
close_reason: Implemented terminal-materialization.generate in Rust, owning WezTerm, Kitty, Alacritty, and Foot config generation plus the unified orchestrator. Deleted terminal_renderers.nu entirely (-197 lines) and reduced terminal_configs.nu from 147 to 89 lines (-58). All targeted tests pass.

title: Ghostty terminal config materialization in Rust
description: Move Ghostty config generation (including shader asset syncing, cursor trail randomization, transparency, glow levels) from utils/terminal_renderers.nu and utils/terminal_ghostty_assets.nu to a Rust-owned terminal-materialization.generate sub-surface. Ghostty is the largest and most distinct terminal config surface. This must delete the Ghostty-specific Nushell owners entirely, not just add Rust helpers alongside them.

Deleted terminal_ghostty_assets.nu entirely (-43 lines) and removed Ghostty functions from terminal_renderers.nu (-192 lines). terminal_configs.nu now calls Rust via thin wrapper. title: Move yzx status to Rust public owner cut
description: Move yzx status from InternalNu routing to RustControl in yzx.rs, making Rust the single public owner for parsing, help, and execution. status.compute is already Rust-owned in yzx_core. This follows the mixed-family pattern from the qsb5 beads (env/run/update). It deletes the public registry role of core/yazelix.nu for the status family.
notes:
2026-04-20 tracker hygiene: confirmed active. The current dirty tree contains the expected status-migration files: `rust_core/yazelix_core/src/bin/yzx.rs`, `rust_core/yazelix_core/src/bin/yzx_control.rs`, `rust_core/yazelix_core/src/status_report.rs`, `nushell/scripts/core/yazelix.nu`, `nushell/scripts/core/yzx_internal_dispatch.nu`, `nushell/scripts/utils/status_report.nu`, and the focused status regression test updates. The tree is still mixed with finished 0w1u.3.1 cleanup files and unrelated local changes, so the next commit should separate those lanes deliberately.
2026-04-20 reopen after verification: the state-path drift was fixed, but the public Rust-owned status surface still has contract regressions. `yzx status --help` and `yzx status --versions` both silently execute the default status path even though command metadata and docs still advertise those flags. Remaining work: keep status fully Rust-owned while restoring flag/help parity and collapsing request/path assembly onto one shared materialization-request builder.
2026-04-20 completion fixup: restored the remaining public status contract in the Rust owner. `yzx status` now parses `--help`, `--json`, and `--versions` explicitly; the optional versions report is emitted for both human and JSON modes; runtime-materialization request assembly now uses one shared control-plane helper with the canonical `state/rebuild_hash` path plus layout-override parity; and duplicate version-file parsing was removed by sharing `read_yazelix_version_from_runtime` between `yzx` and `yzx_control`. Verified with focused public-status Nu regressions, cargo check, cargo test for `yzx`, control-plane lib tests, validate_syntax, and git diff --check.
close_reason: Completed: Rust-owned yzx status now uses the canonical materialized-state/layout request builder and restores help/versions/json parity without reviving the old Nu owner

title: Full-owner terminal config materialization in Rust
description: Move terminal config generation (Ghostty, WezTerm, Kitty, Alacritty, Foot) from utils/terminal_configs.nu and utils/terminal_renderers.nu to a Rust-owned terminal-materialization.generate command. This is the largest remaining deterministic config-generation family. Follow the proven full-owner pattern from Yazi/Zellij/Helix materialization. Delete the Nushell owner end-to-end.
close_reason: Both children complete: gite (Ghostty materialization, -240 lines) and c8wc (non-Ghostty terminals + orchestrator, -255 lines). Total terminal materialization family deleted from Nushell.

title: Full-owner Helix config materialization in Rust
description: Move Helix config generation from setup/helix_config_merger.nu to a Rust-owned helix-materialization.generate command, following the proven Yazi/Zellij full-owner pattern. Helix config generation is deterministic, self-contained, and has no shell-bound execution. Delete the Nushell owner end-to-end.
close_reason: Implemented helix-materialization.generate in Rust, owning template reading, user config deep-merge, reveal binding enforcement, generated config writing, and import notice logic. Replaced 156-line Nushell helix_config_merger.nu with a ~55-line thin wrapper. All existing tests pass.

title: Rust-own Zellij materialization end-to-end and delete the remaining Nushell Zellij generator owners
description: Use the Yazi full-owner deletion as the template for the next serious generator cut. Zellij is the next largest coherent materialization owner family and should move to Rust only if the Nu generator owners disappear end-to-end.
design: Delete first. Rust should own Zellij config merge policy, semantic KDL block rendering, owned top-level settings, plugin path/sync expectations, layout generation, generation-state reuse decisions, generated-file writes, and managed-state ownership for the generated Zellij surface. Target deleting or materially demoting nushell/scripts/setup/zellij_config_merger.nu, zellij_semantic_blocks.nu, zellij_base_config.nu, zellij_owned_settings.nu, zellij_plugin_paths.nu, zellij_generation_state.nu, and nushell/scripts/utils/layout_generator.nu. Reject any result where Rust computes more plan data but Nu remains the writer/orchestrator owner. Rust dependency gate: production crates stay in-house/std plus existing workspace crates serde, serde_json, toml, lexopt, sha2, thiserror; no new production crate unless a real KDL parser/formatter is required and recorded before use. Dev-only crates stay with existing assert_cmd, pretty_assertions, tempfile. Packaging impact should remain inside yzx_core.
acceptance_criteria: 1. The surviving Zellij generation path deletes or materially demotes the current Nushell Zellij owner family rather than preserving it behind more Rust planning calls. 2. Rust becomes the end-to-end owner for generated Zellij config, layouts, plugin expectations, and generation-state write decisions. 3. Existing generated-config behavior remains defended by focused tests. 4. The slice yields visible shipped Nushell reduction in the Zellij materialization family.
close_reason: Completed: Rust yzx_core now owns Zellij config/layout/plugin/generation-state materialization end-to-end; zellij_config_merger.nu is a thin wrapper; zellij_semantic_blocks.nu, zellij_base_config.nu, zellij_owned_settings.nu, zellij_plugin_paths.nu, zellij_generation_state.nu, and layout_generator.nu are deleted. Verified cargo check, zellij_materialization Rust tests, Zellij plugin contracts, shell managed config contracts, Nu syntax, and spec validation. Broader generated-config suite still has one Yazi warm static-asset sentinel failure tracked under yazelix-8h9y.

title: Rust-own Yazi materialization end-to-end and delete the remaining Nushell Yazi generator owners
description: Use the generator audit outcome from yazelix-ulb2.3: Yazi is the first full-owner materialization target because it is the smallest coherent generator family that can still delete real shipped Nushell ownership. This task exists to transfer the Yazi generated surface end-to-end instead of adding another planner around the existing Nu writers.
design: Delete first. A successful slice deletes or materially demotes nushell/scripts/setup/yazi_config_merger.nu, yazi_bundled_assets.nu, and yazi_user_overrides.nu as authoritative owners. Rust should own Yazi merge policy, plugin and theme selection, bundled asset sync expectations, generated-file writes, and managed-state ownership for the generated Yazi surface. Do not accept a result where Rust computes more plan data but Nushell still owns the same writer/orchestrator seam. Keep only thin command or process glue in Nushell if the owner family genuinely disappears.
acceptance_criteria: 1. The surviving Yazi generation path deletes or materially demotes the current Nushell Yazi owner family rather than preserving it behind more Rust planning calls. 2. Rust becomes the end-to-end owner for the generated Yazi surface, including managed-file write decisions and bundled asset expectations. 3. Existing Yazi-generated-config behavior remains defended by focused tests. 4. The slice yields visible shipped Nushell reduction in the Yazi materialization family.
notes: Audit basis from yazelix-ulb2.3: the Yazi family is about 739 raw Nushell lines across yazi_config_merger.nu, yazi_bundled_assets.nu, and yazi_user_overrides.nu, with bounded outputs and lower product-risk than the larger Zellij family. Zellij remains the likely second full-owner materialization lane after this one proves that a whole generator family can actually disappear.
close_reason: Completed

title: Replace stale inventory and transition specs that still point at deleted Nushell owners
description: The current inventory/spec layer is stale enough to misdirect the rewrite. Several docs still reference deleted product-side owners such as nushell/scripts/yzx/env.nu, nushell/scripts/yzx/run.nu, launch_state.nu, and the old config-migration files. subsystem_code_inventory.md is no longer a trustworthy delete-first inventory, and multiple transition specs still describe migrations that already landed or backend-era surfaces that no longer exist. This task exists to make the roadmap describe the real surviving owners before more serious Rust-deletion work proceeds.
design: Delete and rewrite aggressively. Replace the old subsystem LOC snapshot with a delete-first inventory organized around: stale/dead references, bridge layers to collapse, major Rust-port candidates, and likely Nu survivors. Rewrite spec_inventory.md accordingly. Delete or demote transition docs that are effectively complete or now misleading, and update any planning docs worth keeping so they stop citing deleted files. Do not preserve stale planning text just because it once matched a transition state.
acceptance_criteria: 1. The main inventory document reflects the actual surviving Nu owners and the real delete-first targets. 2. Spec inventory and related roadmap docs no longer present already-landed transitions or deleted files as current work. 3. The resulting docs give a credible ordered shortlist for serious Nu deletion. 4. Broken references to deleted Nu owner files are materially reduced or eliminated in the affected inventory/transition docs.
notes: Initial stale references found in subsystem_code_inventory.md, streamlining_audit_2026_04.md, yzx_env_run_rust_owner_transition.md, yzx_command_surface_backend_coupling.md, backend_capability_contract.md, v16_rust_cli_rewrite_evaluation.md, launch_bootstrap_rust_migration.md, and adjacent docs that still point at deleted owners.
close_reason: Completed

title: Delete the remaining yzx_core/yzx_control Nushell bridge owners
description: The helper-backed Rust slices landed, but a substantial product-side Nu bridge layer still survives around yzx_core and yzx_control. The current delete-first inventory puts the residual bridge/report family at roughly 1.5k shipped Nu lines across config_parser.nu, runtime_contract_checker.nu, generated_runtime_state.nu, runtime_env.nu, status_report.nu, config_state.nu, doctor_*_report.nu, install_ownership_report.nu, and the Yazi/Zellij render-plan bridges. This task exists to collapse that family instead of letting bridge code become a permanent second owner.
design: Delete first. Treat the target files as one surviving owner family: helper resolution, argv construction, envelope parsing, duplicated error-surface plumbing, and thin report-plan adapters should be centralized or deleted. Prefer fewer Rust entrypoints and fewer Nu wrappers, not more one-command bridge modules. A successful slice can keep minimal human-facing rendering and shell/process glue in Nu, but it must delete or collapse the current bridge/report owners materially. Do not accept a result that merely moves helper args around while the same file set remains in place.
acceptance_criteria: 1. One or more of config_parser.nu, runtime_contract_checker.nu, generated_runtime_state.nu, runtime_env.nu, status_report.nu, config_state.nu, doctor_*_report.nu, install_ownership_report.nu, yazi_render_plan.nu, or zellij_render_plan.nu is deleted outright or reduced to clearly thin glue. 2. The surviving bridge path has fewer public/authoritative owners than today and no duplicated helper resolution or error-envelope parsing stack. 3. Tests defend the remaining runtime/report contracts without discovery noise. 4. The slice yields visible shipped Nu reduction in this bridge family, not just line movement.
notes: Inventory basis: the current bridge/report layer is the clearest next deletion target after the earlier env/run, status, update, doctor, and bootstrap/materialization slices. This task should stay focused on product-side owner deletion, not maintainer-test growth.
close_reason: Completed

title: Rust-own public command metadata/help/completion and delete the extern/public-registry seam
description: The next step toward any sane v16 Rust/clap yzx rewrite is not another helper-backed leaf command. It is deleting the current public command-discovery and metadata split between nushell/scripts/core/yazelix.nu, nushell/scripts/yzx/*.nu, and the generated extern bridge. This bead should move the public command metadata/help/completion ownership for the Rust-owned and mixed control-plane families onto a single Rust-owned source of truth, while deleting generated extern regeneration as the authoritative steady-state discovery path and materially shrinking or demoting the public command registry in core/yazelix.nu. The outcome should make a broader clap yzx root possible without permitting a clap-as-thin-shim design.
design: Delete first. Do not accept a design where Rust adds a top-level dispatcher but the existing Nushell command tree remains the public parser/help/completion owner. Prefer a Rust-owned command metadata model that can drive public help, completion, and migrated-family dispatch coherently, while intentionally still allowing some commands to remain Nushell-owned as internal helpers. Scope this around the command-owner seam, not around shell execution: do not move detached terminal execution, shell quoting, renderer/template ownership, or broad startup orchestration into Rust just because command metadata moved. Coordinate explicitly with yzx_control, shells/posix/yzx_cli.sh, core/yazelix.nu, and nushell_externs ownership so the steady-state source of truth is singular rather than mirrored.
acceptance_criteria: 1. There is one declared source of truth for public command metadata/help/completion for the migrated command families, and it is not the old generated extern plus export use Nushell tree split. 2. The generated extern bridge stops being the authoritative steady-state command-discovery owner; any remaining extern compatibility is clearly secondary/compat-only. 3. nushell/scripts/core/yazelix.nu materially shrinks or loses public-registry ownership for the migrated command families. 4. The bead defines or lands focused parity checks for public help/completion/exit behavior that defend the new owner boundary. 5. The resulting shape moves Yazelix materially closer to a real v16 clap rewrite and explicitly rejects clap as a thin shim over the unchanged public Nushell tree.
notes: Why this exists: docs/specs/v16_rust_cli_rewrite_evaluation.md says the clap path is only justified if we delete the public command registry, the generated extern bridge as command-discovery owner, and public Nushell wrapper parsing for migrated families. ulb2.1 and the current helper/core work got us closer, but they did not remove the metadata/help/completion seam. This bead should name the exact families moved onto the Rust metadata owner first (likely root/help/version/completion plus the already Rust-owned control-plane leaves), the exact Nushell public exports/extern generation that disappear or become compatibility-only, and the parity harness needed to defend help/completion/exit behavior during the hybrid stage. Reject any solution that keeps generated extern discovery or export use ../yzx/*.nu as the long-term public command source of truth.
close_reason: Completed: yzx_core now owns public command metadata/help/extern rendering, Nushell extern/menu surfaces consume Rust metadata instead of scope probes, core/yazelix.nu dropped the yzx update* discovery stubs, and focused Rust/Nu parity checks cover externs, root help, menu catalog, and launcher dispatch.

title: Rust-own startup and launch preflight/bootstrap orchestration
description: Move the remaining deterministic startup/launch bootstrap orchestration out of nushell/scripts/utils/environment_bootstrap.nu, nushell/scripts/utils/runtime_contract_checker.nu, nushell/scripts/core/start_yazelix.nu, and the adjacent launch preflight flow so Rust becomes the source of truth for preflight result selection, working-dir/runtime-script/terminal validation outcomes, and bootstrap state decisions. This slice should cover the current check_startup_preflight / check_launch_preflight result plumbing, the duplicated require_runtime_check/result extraction logic in start_yazelix.nu and launch_yazelix.nu, and the environment-bootstrap classification that still reassembles Rust-owned config/config-state/runtime-contract data in Nushell. Keep Nushell responsible for actually launching processes, exporting env vars, printing progress, and interactive shell behavior.
design:
Delete-first boundary: do not keep runtime_contract_checker.nu as the authoritative owner behind a thin wrapper. Prefer extending existing runtime-contract / runtime-env / status or control-plane seams so Rust returns the preflight/bootstrap decisions directly, and Nushell only executes the resulting shell/process actions. A successful outcome materially shrinks runtime_contract_checker.nu and the startup/launch wrappers instead of adding more check-result unpacking glue.

Boundaries for this child:
- In scope: runtime_contract_checker.nu result extraction/helpers, environment_bootstrap.nu orchestration, and duplicated startup/launch preflight selection in start_yazelix.nu and launch_yazelix.nu.
- Likely Rust touchpoints: runtime_contract, runtime_env, status/control-plane request shapes, and any narrow startup/launch preflight evaluation command needed to centralize the deterministic decisions.
- Preserve Nu-only responsibilities: shell invocation, terminal launch commands, env injection, progress output, and interactive UX.
- Non-goals: Yazi/Zellij generated config materialization (ulb2.5.1), doctor findings, pane-orchestrator/sidebar truth work, or macOS launcher work.
acceptance_criteria:
1. runtime_contract_checker.nu and environment_bootstrap.nu are materially shrunk and no longer own the main startup/launch/bootstrap classification logic.
2. start_yazelix.nu and launch_yazelix.nu lose duplicated preflight-result extraction/validation logic.
3. Remaining Nushell code in this area is clearly process-launch/env glue rather than a second preflight/bootstrap decision owner.
4. Focused tests defend real startup/launch preflight behavior rather than helper-discovery noise.
notes:
Composer 2 handoff: start by comparing the startup and launch preflight flows side by side. The highest-value deletion seam is the repeated 'call runtime-contract helper -> select required checks -> require_runtime_check -> extract path/candidates' pattern plus environment_bootstrap reassembling Rust-owned config/config-state decisions.

Suggested attack plan:
1. Inventory the duplicated startup/launch preflight extraction logic and identify the common request/response shape that Rust should own.
2. Separate bootstrap state decisions from the process-execution layer in start_yazelix.nu / launch_yazelix.nu.
3. Keep process launching and shell env wiring in Nushell, but centralize all deterministic validation/classification in Rust.
4. Preserve current startup and launch failure messages unless a Rust-owned message clearly deletes duplicate prose owners.
5. Add focused tests for real preflight/bootstrap behavior, especially missing check results, invalid working dirs, and terminal support selection.

Stop if the work mostly creates another helper wrapper while runtime_contract_checker.nu still owns result selection and classification.
close_reason: Rust: startup-launch-preflight.evaluate; Nu thin preflight + bootstrap

title: Rust-own generated runtime materialization orchestration and thin merger Nu owners
description: Move the remaining deterministic generated-state orchestration out of nushell/scripts/utils/generated_runtime_state.nu and the Yazi/Zellij merger owners so Rust becomes the source of truth for whether generated runtime artifacts need refresh, which artifacts are expected, and which render/materialization steps should run. This slice should target the current generated_runtime_state + merger cluster: plan/apply orchestration, refresh/noop/repair classification, and the surviving deterministic merge decisions in yazi_config_merger.nu and zellij_config_merger.nu that are already adjacent to Rust-owned render plans. Keep Nushell responsible only for shell-side file writes, plugin sync/process execution, and human-facing progress output where Rust would not simplify the system.
design:
Delete-first boundary: do not preserve generated_runtime_state.nu as the real owner behind more argv builders. Prefer extending the existing runtime-materialization / render-plan seams so Rust returns the orchestration decisions directly and Nushell only performs file IO and host actions. A successful outcome materially shrinks generated_runtime_state.nu and the merger modules rather than just moving conditionals around.

Boundaries for this child:
- In scope: generated_runtime_state.nu orchestration, the decision glue around compute_runtime_materialization_plan / apply_runtime_materialization, and deterministic merge/render decisions in yazi_config_merger.nu / zellij_config_merger.nu that can be centralized in Rust.
- Likely Rust touchpoints: runtime_materialization, yazi_render_plan, zellij_render_plan, and adjacent helper request/response shapes.
- Preserve Nu-only responsibilities: plugin wasm sync, filesystem writes, progress printing, user override file reads, and shell/process integration.
- Non-goals: pane-orchestrator truth work under 0w1u.*, doctor findings under ulb2.4.*, macOS launcher work, or broad environment bootstrap ownership outside the generated-state/materialization slice.
acceptance_criteria:
1. generated_runtime_state.nu is materially shrunk and no longer owns the main generated-state orchestration/classification logic.
2. yazi_config_merger.nu and/or zellij_config_merger.nu lose real deterministic merge/render decision logic rather than only rearranging helper calls.
3. The remaining Nushell layer is clearly file-write/process glue and progress rendering, not a second orchestration owner.
4. Focused tests defend real generated-state/materialization behavior, especially noop/repair/refresh transitions.
notes:
Composer 2 handoff, tightened from code inspection:

Primary delete seam:
- `nushell/scripts/utils/generated_runtime_state.nu` is still the real owner of generated-state orchestration. The highest-value functions are `regenerate_runtime_configs`, `record_current_materialized_state`, and `repair_generated_runtime_state`.
- Today those functions still decide noop vs repair, when to print progress, when to call Yazi vs Zellij generation, when `should_sync_static_assets` matters, and how plan/apply results map into the user-facing repair flow.

Concrete adjacent owners to shrink in the same slice:
- `nushell/scripts/setup/yazi_config_merger.nu`: keep file writes and user override reads in Nushell, but target the remaining deterministic decisions around generated `yazi.toml` / `theme.toml` / `init.lua` content that are already adjacent to `compute_yazi_render_plan`.
- `nushell/scripts/setup/zellij_config_merger.nu`: keep plugin wasm sync, layout generation side effects, and writes in Nushell, but target the deterministic generation/reuse decisions and render-plan-owned config assembly around `generate_merged_zellij_config`.

Explicit non-goals for this child:
- Do not absorb plugin sync (`sync_pane_orchestrator_runtime_wasm`, `sync_zjstatus_runtime_wasm`) into Rust.
- Do not move direct filesystem writes or user override file reads into Rust unless doing so clearly deletes more Nushell than it adds.
- Do not broaden into `runtime_contract_checker.nu`, `environment_bootstrap.nu`, `start_yazelix.nu`, or `launch_yazelix.nu`; that is reserved for `yazelix-ulb2.5.2`.

Dependency decision for this Rust bead:
- Production crates: none added; reuse existing serde/serde_json/toml/lexopt and current in-tree modules.
- Dev-only crates: none added.
- In-house logic: generated-state orchestration/result modeling should stay local because the contract is Yazelix-specific and already adjacent to `runtime_materialization`, `yazi_render_plan`, and `zellij_render_plan`.
- Rejected alternatives: adding templating/render helper crates or filesystem abstraction crates would increase packaging churn without deleting the real Nushell owners.
- Packaging impact: none expected beyond rebuilding the existing rust_core helper.

Suggested implementation order:
1. First collapse `generated_runtime_state.nu` so Rust owns the plan/apply/repair classification and Nushell becomes progress + side-effect glue.
2. Only then pull the remaining deterministic Yazi/Zellij generation decisions into Rust if they delete real owner logic in the merger modules.
3. Stop if the result still leaves `generated_runtime_state.nu` deciding when regeneration happens.
close_reason: Rust: runtime-materialization.repair-evaluate + Nu thin repair/regenerate bridge

title: Rust-own config doctor findings and shrink check_configuration
description: Move the remaining config-surface doctor classification out of nushell/scripts/utils/doctor.nu so Rust owns the findings contract for configuration presence, legacy-surface detection, and stale-schema diagnostics. This slice should cover the current check_configuration() family: primary surface reconciliation, custom-vs-default config detection, legacy yazelix.nix / root-level surface warnings, missing-config errors, and stale or unsupported yazelix.toml reporting. Keep Nushell responsible for rendering doctor output and shell-side fixes such as creating a default yazelix.toml.
design:
Delete-first boundary: do not leave check_configuration() in doctor.nu as the authoritative owner behind a thin wrapper. Prefer extending the existing yzx_core config/reporting seam so Rust returns the structured doctor findings directly, reusing config.normalize or adjacent diagnostics data where possible. A successful outcome materially shrinks check_configuration(), and may also shrink config_diagnostics.nu / config_surfaces.nu bridge code where they only assemble deterministic doctor-state decisions.

Boundaries for this child:
- In scope: doctor.nu check_configuration(), the config-doctor result shapes it emits today, reconcile_primary_config_surfaces usage as it relates to doctor classification, and stale-schema warning assembly that currently depends on build_config_diagnostic_report.
- Likely Rust touchpoints: extend yzx_core with a config-doctor evaluation that can classify config presence / default-template mode / legacy-surface errors alongside diagnostic-report issue counts.
- Preserve existing doctor UX: custom-config ok row, stale-or-unsupported warning text, legacy yazelix.nix detection, and no-config/default-template messaging should still render through doctor.nu. fix_available for the default-template case must still let Nushell run fix_create_config.
- Non-goals: live Zellij plugin health, runtime distribution / shared preflight (done in ulb2.4.3), Helix findings (done in ulb2.4.2), install ownership / launcher provenance (done in ulb2.4.1), and broad bootstrap/generated-state orchestration from ulb2.5.
acceptance_criteria:
1. doctor.nu no longer owns the main config doctor classification logic; it consumes Rust-owned structured findings for configuration presence, legacy surfaces, and stale-schema reporting.
2. check_configuration() is materially shrunk or deleted, and nearby config-diagnostic bridge code loses real shipped decision logic instead of only moving lines around.
3. Doctor output and --fix behavior remain intact, including fix_create_config for the default-template case.
4. Focused tests defend real config doctor finding payloads rather than command-discovery noise.
notes: Dependency decision for this Rust bead: use existing in-tree crates and std only. Production crates: none added; reuse existing serde/serde_json/toml/lexopt already present in yazelix_core. Dev-only crates: none added. In-house logic: config doctor finding classification and JSON bridge types should stay local because the domain contract is project-specific and already adjacent to config_normalize. Rejected alternatives: adding a new error/reporting helper crate or filesystem abstraction crate would add packaging churn without buying stable parsing beyond existing TOML/JSON support. Packaging impact: none expected beyond rebuilding the existing rust_core helper.
close_reason: Completed

title: Rust-own shared runtime preflight and runtime-distribution findings
description: Move the remaining deterministic shared-runtime doctor/preflight findings out of nushell/scripts/utils/doctor.nu so Rust owns the classification and Nushell only renders results and performs shell-side fixes. This slice should cover the shared runtime preflight family currently assembled in check_shared_runtime_preflight() plus the runtime distribution capability profile currently owned by runtime_distribution_capabilities.nu, while leaving live Zellij/plugin health and broad config diagnostics untouched.
design:
Delete-first boundary: do not add another parallel helper that leaves doctor.nu as the real owner. Prefer extending existing runtime-contract.evaluate or adjacent Rust findings data so doctor.nu consumes one Rust-owned structured result for (1) generated layout and runtime-script preflight findings already derived from runtime-contract checks, and (2) runtime/distribution capability classification currently inferred from install ownership plus runtime-root shape. The expected end state is that doctor.nu loses most of the decision glue in check_shared_runtime_preflight(), runtime_distribution_capabilities.nu materially shrinks or disappears, and any remaining Nushell code is only bridge glue, rendering, or --fix side effects like repair_generated_runtime_state.

Boundaries for this child:
- In scope: doctor.nu shared runtime preflight assembly, runtime_distribution_capabilities.nu, any small bridge helpers needed to pass inputs to Rust, and focused tests covering doctor findings contracts.
- Likely Rust touchpoints: extend yzx_core findings or adjacent runtime-contract/reporting seams rather than inventing a standalone ad-hoc command if the same structured result can serve doctor/preflight consumers.
- Preserve existing fix behavior: generated-state failures that point at managed layout artifacts must still surface fix_action = repair_generated_runtime_state from Nushell.
- Non-goals: check_zellij_plugin_health(), pane-orchestrator live session reasoning, install ownership itself (already moved in ulb2.4.1), Helix findings (done in ulb2.4.2), and broad config validation/reporting.
acceptance_criteria:
1. doctor.nu no longer owns the main shared-runtime preflight and runtime-distribution classification logic; it consumes Rust-owned structured findings for those families.
2. runtime_distribution_capabilities.nu is materially shrunk or deleted, and check_shared_runtime_preflight() in doctor.nu loses real decision glue rather than merely forwarding the same logic.
3. Doctor output and --fix behavior remain intact, including repair_generated_runtime_state for managed generated-layout failures.
4. Focused tests defend the runtime-distribution and shared-runtime doctor findings contracts instead of command-discovery noise.
notes:
Composer 2 handoff: start by inventorying what is still authoritatively decided in doctor.nu after ulb2.4.2. The highest-value deletion seam is the combo of build_runtime_distribution_doctor_result + get_runtime_distribution_capability_profile + check_shared_runtime_preflight() record shaping. runtime_contract_checker.nu is already mostly a bridge, so the goal is not to move that bridge wholesale but to stop doctor.nu from re-classifying or re-assembling deterministic findings around it.

Suggested attack plan:
1. Trace the exact doctor result shapes emitted today for runtime distribution capability and shared runtime preflight.
2. Decide whether to extend an existing Rust data shape or add one narrow findings envelope that groups both families together for doctor consumption.
3. Keep Nushell ownership only for fix mapping (repair_generated_runtime_state) and user-facing rendering.
4. Add focused tests that defend real doctor finding payloads, especially the managed-layout generated-state repair path and runtime/distribution mode messaging.

Stop if the implementation mostly adds JSON argv builders while leaving doctor.nu or runtime_distribution_capabilities.nu as the true decision owners.
close_reason: Added yazelix_core doctor_runtime_report and yzx_core doctor-runtime.evaluate; Nushell doctor_runtime_report.nu bridges install_io + materialization plan into Rust; doctor.nu consumes distribution and shared preflight from Rust; deleted runtime_distribution_capabilities.nu; tests cover distribution/fix_action and CLI envelope.

title: Rust-own Helix doctor findings and shrink doctor_helix.nu
description: Move the Helix-focused doctor findings family behind a Rust-owned findings seam so nushell/scripts/utils/doctor_helix.nu stops being the main owner of Helix runtime conflict detection, runtime-health classification, and managed Helix reveal-integration checks. Keep shell-side fix actions and human rendering in Nushell, but delete substantive Helix reasoning from doctor_helix.nu and route yzx doctor through Rust-owned structured findings.
design: Composer 2 prep: treat this as the next bounded child under ulb2.4 instead of attempting the whole remaining doctor surface at once. Boundary: only the Helix doctor family in doctor_helix.nu plus the corresponding doctor.nu call sites. Rust should own structured findings for (1) Helix runtime conflicts, (2) Helix runtime health from hx --health / runtime directory inventory, and (3) managed Helix reveal-integration contract checks. Nushell keeps fix_helix_runtime_conflicts and any direct shell/file mutations. Prefer extending one doctor/findings seam in yzx_core rather than adding another ad-hoc helper command; a narrow helix-only request/response within that seam is acceptable for this slice. Reuse existing config.normalize or runtime-materialization seams where possible, but do not leave doctor_helix.nu as the authoritative classifier after the bridge lands. Keep doctor output text and --fix wiring in Nushell. Target file set: rust_core/yazelix_core/src/... new or extended doctor findings module; nushell/scripts/utils/doctor_helix.nu; nushell/scripts/utils/doctor.nu; tests in nushell/scripts/dev/test_yzx_doctor_commands.nu and focused Rust tests. Explicit non-goals: zellij plugin health, install ownership/provenance, shared runtime preflight, and broad config-surface reporting in this child.
acceptance_criteria: 1. doctor_helix.nu materially shrinks or becomes mostly bridge/fix glue instead of owning Helix classification. 2. yzx doctor still reports Helix runtime conflicts, runtime health, and managed reveal-integration findings through Rust-owned structured data. 3. --fix still works for Helix runtime conflict remediation from Nushell. 4. Tests defend Helix doctor behavior/regressions, not command discovery.
notes: Composer 2 handoff notes: start by inventorying the three exported doctor_helix.nu families and mapping each current result shape to a Rust finding schema. Keep the slice bounded; do not mix in zellij plugin or generic config work. High-signal existing tests live in nushell/scripts/dev/test_yzx_doctor_commands.nu; preserve or retarget only the Helix-relevant ones. Success metric is shipped Nushell owner deletion, not extra helper coverage.
close_reason: Helix doctor classification lives in yazelix_core::doctor_helix_report; yzx_core doctor-helix.evaluate bridges from doctor_helix_report.nu; doctor_helix.nu retains fix_helix_runtime_conflicts only; doctor.nu consumes collect_helix_doctor_results once; Rust and CLI integration tests added.

title: Collapse duplicated post-retarget sidebar and editor sync orchestration across open and zoxide flows
description: managed_editor.nu and zoxide_open_in_editor.nu both run the same retarget_workspace_for_path to optional editor handling to sidebar sync sequence. Collapse that duplicated live-state orchestration so one narrow path owns post-retarget editor and Yazi sync behavior.
design: Prefer deleting duplicated Nushell branches over adding another abstraction layer. If a small new orchestrator pipe materially simplifies ordering or error handling, keep it narrow to plugin-owned state transitions only. Path resolution, editor choice, and ya emit-to execution should stay outside Rust unless a broader contract changes.
acceptance_criteria: 1. The repeated retarget then sidebar-sync sequences in managed_editor.nu and zoxide_open_in_editor.nu materially shrink. 2. One post-retarget sync path remains for shared behavior. 3. Remaining Nushell clearly owns only intent, path resolution, and external tool execution. 4. Tests defend shared retarget and sidebar-sync behavior instead of per-call duplication.
close_reason: Completed: managed_editor.nu and zoxide_open_in_editor.nu now share one post-retarget sync path for optional missing-editor follow-up plus sidebar/current-Yazi sync behavior, and focused Yazi regressions defend the shared retarget-sync contract.

title: Retire get_active_sidebar_yazi_state and make the session snapshot the only live sidebar and Yazi read seam
description: The pane orchestrator now exposes sidebar_yazi inside get_active_tab_session_state, but Nushell still carries a second live read seam through get_active_sidebar_yazi_state_from_plugin and yazi_sidebar_state.nu. Migrate live consumers to the typed session snapshot and delete the duplicate active-tab read path.
design: Delete first. Reuse sidebar_yazi from the versioned session payload instead of adding another compatibility adapter. Keep ya emit-to execution and Yazi or Lua UI work outside Rust. If consumers still need readiness or failure detail, extend the versioned session seam rather than preserving a parallel raw pipe contract.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Live sidebar and Yazi consumers read active-tab identity from get_active_tab_session_state. 2. yazi_sidebar_state.nu and duplicate helper paths are deleted or materially reduced. 3. get_active_sidebar_yazi_state stops acting as a production ownership seam and is removed or clearly compatibility-only. 4. Regression checks defend missing-sidebar and cross-tab targeting behavior.
