Manual installs should continue to relaunch through ~/.local/bin/yzx; Home Manager installs should relaunch through the profile-owned yzx wrapper. Keep the fix delete-first: do not recreate manual installer artifacts in Home Manager mode and do not add another compatibility alias surface.
design:
Reason from first principles:
- Restart should relaunch through the install owner's stable external wrapper, not through whatever runtime the current session happened to come from.
- Manual installs and Home Manager installs have different stable launcher surfaces, but they solve the same problem: they point at the currently owned runtime after an update.
- Do not special-case Home Manager by recreating ~/.local/bin/yzx; that would violate the current ownership contract.
- Prefer one shared stable-launcher resolution seam that can later be reused by desktop/install/restart flows instead of duplicating install-owner heuristics in each command.
- The surviving contract should be simple: resolve a stable external yzx wrapper when one exists, use it for restart, and fall back to in-session yzx launch only when there truly is no owner-provided stable wrapper.
acceptance_criteria: yzx restart relaunches through a stable owner-provided external wrapper for both manual installs and pure Home Manager installs. Restart must no longer trust the current session's runtime-pinned yzx command path when a stable owner wrapper exists. After a Home Manager update/switch, invoking restart from an older live session opens the newer runtime instead of relaunching the stale one. The fix must not recreate ~/.local/bin/yzx in Home Manager mode, and regression coverage must defend both the manual-install and Home Manager restart paths.
notes: Completed: extracted shared launcher_resolution.nu, made yzx restart prefer the stable owner wrapper for manual and Home Manager installs, reused the seam for desktop launch resolution, and covered Home Manager profile preference in default tests.
close_reason: Completed: restart and desktop launcher resolution now use the stable owner wrapper, with Home Manager profile ownership covered by default and maintainer tests.

title: Add Darwin Ghostty packaging to the Yazelix runtime through ghostty-bin or an equivalent wrapped app path
description: Update the runtime dependency surface so macOS installs expose a usable Ghostty binary from the Yazelix runtime itself. The preferred path is the current nixpkgs ghostty-bin package because it preserves the app bundle and a wrapped ghostty executable, but an equivalent first-party wrapper is acceptable if needed. The contract to defend is that a macOS Yazelix runtime can resolve Ghostty without asking the user to install it separately.
close_reason: Completed

title: Make Ghostty launch semantics platform-aware so macOS does not inherit Linux GTK and X11 flags
description: Refine the Ghostty launch path so Yazelix keeps one first-party Ghostty default across platforms without pretending Linux and macOS have the same CLI surface. Linux can keep the GTK and X11-specific launch flags it needs, but macOS should use only the Ghostty arguments that are valid for the wrapped app-bundle path. Validate both yzx launch and desktop launch behavior against that narrower contract.
close_reason: Completed

title: Provide Ghostty in the Yazelix runtime and prefer it as the first-party Linux launch path
description: Implement the narrow first-party Ghostty contract for Linux. The runtime should provide Ghostty directly, `yzx launch` and desktop launch should be able to prefer the runtime Ghostty path when Ghostty is selected, and the docs/config comments should state clearly that Ghostty is the built-in default while other terminals remain supported host-installed alternatives. Do not reintroduce `manage_terminals` or broad terminal ownership.
close_reason: Completed

title: Provide Ghostty on macOS as a first-party Yazelix runtime path
description: Implement the narrow first-party Ghostty contract for macOS. The runtime should expose a usable Ghostty binary through the Darwin package path, currently nixpkgs ghostty-bin or an equivalent app-bundle-backed wrapper, the docs and config comments should stop claiming macOS must stay host-provided, and Yazelix should launch Ghostty on macOS without assuming Linux GTK or X11 flags. Do not reintroduce broad terminal management or multi-terminal ownership.
close_reason: Completed

title: Adopt Ghostty as the first-party Yazelix terminal on Linux and macOS without reviving broad terminal management
description: Yazelix should guarantee one easy, first-party Ghostty path on both Linux and macOS while keeping the v15 terminal surface slim. Linux can use the normal nixpkgs Ghostty package and macOS can use the app-bundle-backed nixpkgs path or an equivalent first-party wrapper, but the user-facing contract should stay simple: Ghostty is the built-in default terminal path for Yazelix, while WezTerm, Kitty, Alacritty, and Foot remain supported host-provided alternatives. This bead exists to avoid drifting back into broad terminal installation or ownership while still making launch and desktop entry easy on both platforms.
close_reason: Completed

title: Produce a post-trim replacement matrix for deleted and surviving test coverage
description: Audit the test trimming from the v15 delete-first pass. For every materially deleted or rewritten test cluster, classify it as one of: contract removed, replaced by stronger surviving coverage, or missing replacement coverage. Focus especially on the refresh/launch-profile era, pack-sidecar era, shell-breadth era, and terminal-wrapper breadth tests. The output should be a durable matrix that explains why the remaining suite is still trustworthy, and where the remaining risk actually is.
close_reason: Completed

title: Run non-default high-value verification lanes after the v15 trim and fix leftover failures
description: After the post-trim coverage matrix exists, run the non-default high-value verification that still matters for v15 instead of trusting only the canonical default suite. This should cover the maintainer/package/runtime validators and any surviving non-default suites that still defend the retained v15 contract. Fix real failures, delete stale checks that only preserved removed v14 semantics, and record what still remains intentionally unverified if any environment limitation blocks a lane.
close_reason: Completed

title: Delete or rewrite stale spec and doc references to removed v14 semantics after the v15 trim
description: Clean up the remaining spec and documentation text that still talks about removed or heavily narrowed v14 semantics as if they were current for the v15 branch. This includes stale references to pack sidecars, launch-profile reuse, runtime-local devenv ownership, explicit refresh semantics, and any front-door docs that still frame devenv as the normal user runtime entry path. Historical notes may keep those terms when clearly labeled as history; the target here is stale normative guidance and stale current-state descriptions.
close_reason: Completed

title: Audit and finish the leftover post-trim cleanup after the v15 delete-first pass
description: The big v15 trim landed, but there is still leftover follow-up work before the branch should be treated as fully settled. The remaining risk is no longer the core runtime/config deletion itself. It is the audit gap around what deleted tests were replaced, what non-default verification still matters, and which docs/specs still describe removed v14 semantics as if they were current behavior. This bead exists to make that remaining work explicit instead of leaving it as vague unease after the trim.
close_reason: Completed

title: Define the v15 feature-preservation gate before deletion
description:
Before implementing the v15 trim, define the feature-preservation gate that prevents delete-first work from becoming feature loss.

This bead exists because v15 should get slimmer by deleting confused ownership, duplicate runtime layers, and package-manager responsibilities, not by dropping useful Yazelix workflows just because they are currently entangled with `devenv` or v14 state machinery.

Scope:
- Build a feature/workflow matrix for the v15 trim candidates: `yzx run`, `yzx refresh`, launch-profile reuse, packs, shell hooks, terminal wrappers/config modes, Home Manager pack/config options, `devenv` runtime packaging, and v14-bound tests.
- For each candidate, classify the user-visible capability as keep unchanged, keep with a narrower implementation, rehome under a different owner, replace with a new command/path, deprecate with explicit migration, or delete as non-product behavior.
- Require every implementation deletion bead to name the preserved workflow or the explicit reason the workflow is not part of v15.
- Protect power-user value from v14: custom tool/runtime composition, generated config repair, command execution inside the Yazelix environment, multi-terminal launch, shell choice, and Home Manager declarative ownership must not disappear silently.
- Identify which capabilities should remain in v14/Yazelix Classic only, which persist in v15 core, and which move to a possible future separate runtime/devenv project.
acceptance_criteria:
- There is a concrete keep/rehome/replace/deprecate/delete matrix for the v15 trim candidates.
- No implementation bead can be treated as ready if it only says delete without naming the preserved capability, replacement owner, migration path, or explicit non-goal.
- The matrix distinguishes removing implementation ownership from removing user-visible functionality.
- The output is specific enough to update the child beads before code deletion starts.
notes:
2026-04-11 implementation gate matrix:
- yzx run: keep with narrower v15 owner. Preserve one-shot command execution inside the Yazelix runtime, but remove devenv shell/materialization ownership. Implementation should execute argv directly in the packaged/runtime PATH.
- yzx refresh: keep only if narrowed to generated-state repair. Remove backend rebuild/profile semantics; recovery owner becomes config generation/state repair plus update guidance.
- launch-profile reuse: delete from v15 hot path. DEVENV_PROFILE, state/launch_state.json, runtime-project .devenv lookup, reuse flags, and skip-refresh flags are v14 Classic acceleration/state machinery, not v15 runtime truth.
- packs and user package graph: v14 Classic or future separate runtime/devenv project. Do not silently preserve pack knobs in v15 core if the core package cannot honor dynamic package composition.
- shell choice: preserve. default_shell remains a workspace entry preference; dynamic extra_shell package materialization and broad host-dotfile package ownership are v14-only unless rehomed under a separate runtime project.
- terminal launch: preserve multi-terminal launch and config-generation behavior, but remove managed terminal wrapper repair and profile-derived wrapper lookup from the hot path.
- Home Manager: preserve declarative ownership for retained main config and yzx/runtime install path. Remove pack/devenv-only options when the config cutline is applied.
- devenv runtime packaging: remove from v15 core package once runtime/tool availability is supplied directly by fixed package dependencies and Home Manager/package ownership. Custom dynamic composition belongs to Yazelix Classic or a future separate runtime project.
- tests: remove or rewrite tests that only defend devenv build shell, launch-profile recording, runtime-project .devenv caches, pack sidecar package resolution, or skip-refresh/reuse flags. Retain tests for workspace routing, config generation, terminal selection, shell choice, direct yzx run argv behavior, and generated-state repair.

Implementation rule: every delete below must either preserve the workflow under the v15 owner above or remove it as explicitly v14 Classic/future-runtime scope.
close_reason: Feature-preservation matrix recorded for v15 trimming implementation.

title: Remove or rehome devenv from the v15 packaged runtime
description:
Implementation slice for removing or rehoming the old backend from the package/runtime shape after the command, feature-preservation, and config cutlines are ready. Packaging currently ships a runtime that includes locked `devenv`, `devenv.lock`, `devenv.nix`, `devenv.yaml`, and package-wrapper PATH entries for backend materialization.

This is not permission to lose runtime/tool availability, update ownership, or power-user environment composition. First define where those workflows live: v15 core package, package manager/Home Manager, v14/Yazelix Classic, or a future separate runtime/devenv project.

Scope:
- Wait for the feature-preservation gate and the command/refresh/packs/shell/terminal/config decisions.
- Remove `locked_devenv_package.nix` from the normal v15 package/runtime path only if no retained backend owner remains.
- Stop linking `devenv.lock`, `devenv.nix`, `devenv.yaml`, and `bin/devenv` into the core runtime tree only after runtime/tool availability has a replacement owner.
- Simplify `packaging/mk_yazelix_package.nix` runtime deps so `yzx` depends only on the retained runtime/tooling contract.
- Rework or remove installer/runtime-current surfaces that only exist to manage a mutable `devenv` runtime identity, while preserving the supported install/update owner path.
- Update installed-runtime validators and flake interface checks so they defend the new slim package and retained tool/runtime contract instead of the v14 `devenv` runtime.
acceptance_criteria:
- The feature-preservation gate says where runtime/tool availability and custom environment composition live after `devenv` leaves the core package path.
- The core v15 package no longer ships `devenv` or `devenv` input files as part of normal startup/runtime if those responsibilities are rehomed or out of scope.
- Package/runtime validation no longer expects runtime-local `devenv`, but still verifies the retained runtime/tooling contract.
- Any optional future dynamic runtime is outside the core package path and has a separate owner, migration story, or explicit deferment.
close_reason: the packaged runtime no longer ships locked devenv artifacts in the core path, and the runtime/install validators now defend the slimmer package surface.

title: Apply the v15 config cutline before later migration deletion
description:
Implementation slice for applying the final v15 keep/narrow/drop config matrix after the command, refresh, packs, shell, and terminal decisions land.

This bead is now historical implementation context. The 2026-04-13 decision in yazelix-qgj7.6 supersedes its older migration-retention assumption: live v15 now deletes config-migration machinery instead of carrying migration safety as a standing product requirement.

Scope at the time:
- Wait for the feature-preservation gate and the command/refresh/packs/shell/terminal decisions before applying field removals.
- Remove config contract fields with no v15 owner from `config_metadata/main_config_contract.toml`, `yazelix_default.toml`, parser output, diagnostics, validators, docs, and tests.
- Remove matching Home Manager options and generated TOML output from `home_manager/module.nix` in the same slice as default config changes.
- Remove rebuild hash tracking for dead backend inputs such as pack paths and `devenv` input files once the backend is gone.
- Keep surviving workspace/runtime fields explicit rather than bundled under old backend names.
- Update doctor/config tests to defend surviving v15 config behavior rather than old behavior by inertia.
acceptance_criteria:
- The v15 config surface has no dead backend, pack, shell-hook, or terminal-wrapper knobs after the cutline is applied.
- Home Manager exposes the same surviving surface as the default config and no more.
- Config validators and tests defend the new cutline and Home Manager parity instead of preserving old behavior by inertia.
- Current follow-up policy is defined by yazelix-qgj7.6 and yazelix-qgj7.4.5: migration machinery should be deleted from the live v15 contract.
notes: 2026-04-13 update: the older migration-safety expectation is superseded by yazelix-qgj7.6 and the deletion work in yazelix-qgj7.4.5.
close_reason: the trimmed config cutline now lands across the main contract, default config, Home Manager surface, diagnostics, validators, and migration tests.

title: Narrow terminal launch without losing reliable desktop startup
description:
Implementation slice for cutting the terminal/desktop launch surface down to the retained v15 contract. The current matrix owns terminal packages, wrappers, config rendering, nixGL fallback, desktop fast path, and rebuild-triggered wrapper repair.

This is not permission to make launch less reliable or to drop terminal support blindly. First decide which terminal workflows survive: managed terminal wrappers, host-terminal fallback, terminal preference order, terminal config generation, desktop launch, nixGL handling, and multi-terminal support.

Scope:
- Classify terminal/desktop capabilities under the feature-preservation gate before deleting branches.
- Apply the v15 terminal contract from `yazelix-qgj7.2.2.5`: fixed terminal, short host preference list, retained managed wrappers, or another explicit owner.
- Remove unsupported terminal branches from `terminal_launcher.nu`, `launch_yazelix.nu`, terminal config renderers, `devenv.nix` wrappers, config contract, and Home Manager options only after replacement/non-goal decisions exist.
- Delete `manage_terminals` and `terminal.config_mode` only if v15 no longer owns managed wrapper/config mode behavior and users have a documented migration path.
- Keep `yzx desktop` as a thin adapter to the retained launch path; remove fallback paths that exist only for the old dynamic backend after desktop launch still has a reliable v15 path.
- Prune terminal breadth tests only when they defend unsupported v14 compatibility rather than the retained v15 launch contract.
acceptance_criteria:
- The feature-preservation gate says which terminal and desktop launch capabilities survive, narrow, rehome, or become v14-only.
- `yzx launch` and `yzx desktop launch` have one clear terminal story that is at least as honest and reliable as the retained v15 contract promises.
- Unsupported terminal wrapper/config branches are removed only after migration/non-goal wording exists.
- Desktop integration no longer repairs or rebuilds a backend-managed terminal graph, but it still launches through a supported v15 path.
close_reason: launch and desktop entry now follow one narrowed host-terminal path, and the desktop install, uninstall, and Home Manager takeover tests pass on that contract.

title: Narrow shell hooks without losing shell choice
description:
Implementation slice for replacing v14 shell-hook breadth with a narrower v15 shell-entry contract. The target is shell entry into the runtime, not broad host dotfile management.

This is not permission to lose shell choice or a usable shell startup story. First decide which shell capabilities survive: default shell selection, multiple runtime-provided shells, user shell hooks, host shell integration, and Zellij default-shell behavior.

Scope:
- Classify shell choice, `shell.default_shell`, `shell.extra_shells`, host dotfile mutation, Home Manager shell ownership, and generated initializers under the feature-preservation gate.
- Remove or quarantine host shell-hook installation paths in `nushell/scripts/setup/environment.nu` only if v15 does not need them for a retained workflow.
- Decide and implement the fate of `shell.extra_shells`; delete backend package materialization for extra shells only after a replacement or v14-only classification exists.
- Keep `shell.default_shell` if it still means selecting a runtime-provided shell for entry or Zellij default shell.
- Remove Home Manager/config fields and tests for shell surfaces only after migration/non-goal wording exists.
- Ensure `yzx env` becomes a thin runtime shell handoff, not a `devenv shell` rebuild/materialization path, while preserving the supported way to enter a shell.
acceptance_criteria:
- The feature-preservation gate says which shell-choice workflows survive and which are v14-only or rehomed.
- v15 shell entry has one clear owner and does not rely on accidental host shell-hook mutation for correctness.
- Users retain a documented supported shell entry path and any retained shell-choice settings have real runtime owners.
- Dead `extra_shells` or host-hook options/tests are removed only after replacement, migration, or explicit non-goal classification.
close_reason: shell entry is narrowed to the retained runtime shells, the subprocess fixture env is explicit, and the old extra-shell breadth no longer defines the v15 path.

title: Rehome or narrow packs before removing core package-graph ownership
description:
Implementation slice for removing or rehoming the current pack/package-graph surface from core v15 after the feature-preservation gate. Packs currently make Yazelix act like a package manager by feeding dynamic package declarations into `devenv.nix`, but they also carry real power-user value.

This is not permission to silently drop custom tool/runtime composition. First decide whether that capability remains in v15 core, moves to Home Manager/Nix flake ownership, stays v14/Yazelix Classic-only, or becomes part of a future separate runtime/devenv project.

Scope:
- Classify `yzx packs`, `yzx edit packs`, `yazelix_packs.toml`, Home Manager pack options, pack declarations, and `user_packages` under the feature-preservation gate.
- If core v15 no longer owns dynamic package graph composition, define the replacement owner or migration story before removing the public/config surface.
- Remove public `yzx packs` and `yzx edit packs` only after the replacement/non-goal is explicit.
- Remove `yazelix_packs.toml`, `config_metadata/pack_catalog_contract.toml`, pack defaults, pack-sidecar loader glue, and rebuild hash `packs.*` paths only when no surviving v15 owner remains.
- Remove Home Manager pack options and generated pack TOML output only with a documented declarative replacement or v14-only classification.
- Remove pack resolution from `devenv.nix` as part of the broader backend deletion, or quarantine it behind a future optional project if explicitly deferred.
- Replace pack migration, doctor-fix, generated-config, and edit-pack tests with coverage for the retained/replacement workflow, or delete them only when that workflow is explicitly out of v15.
acceptance_criteria:
- The feature-preservation gate says where custom tool/runtime composition lives in v15: core, Home Manager/Nix ownership, future separate runtime project, or v14/Yazelix Classic-only.
- Core v15 does not expose dead pack knobs, but users are not left without a documented path for the power-user workflow if it remains supported.
- Home Manager/config changes include a migration or explicit non-goal statement for pack declarations and user packages.
- Any future pack-like idea is tracked as optional/future runtime work, not retained in the v15 core by inertia.
close_reason: packs and package-graph ownership were removed from the v15 core surface, including the pack catalog, sidecar flow, and the dependent tests.

title: Replace yzx refresh and launch-profile reuse with a v15 state owner
description:
Implementation slice for replacing the explicit refresh/profile-cache era in v15. Current code spreads this through `yzx refresh`, `launch_state.nu`, `devenv_backend.nu`, `env`, `launch`, startup, restart, doctor, and recovery messages.

This is not permission to lose refresh-like user value. The surviving product still needs an owner for generated config repair, stale generated-state recovery, update/rebuild handoff, and clear diagnostics. The goal is to remove the v14 `devenv` launch-profile mechanism, not to remove the user's ability to recover a broken runtime/config state.

Scope:
- Classify each refresh-era capability under the feature-preservation gate: explicit manual refresh, generated Yazi/Zellij config repair, stale profile detection, rebuild/update handoff, and recovery messaging.
- Remove `yzx refresh` from the public surface only if those capabilities are rehomed or explicitly declared v14-only/non-goals.
- Delete or drastically shrink `nushell/scripts/yzx/refresh.nu` only after the retained owner for generated-state repair exists.
- Remove recorded launch-profile reuse as hot-path truth: `state/launch_state.json`, `DEVENV_PROFILE` profile resolution, runtime-project `.devenv` lookup, and reuse/skip-refresh flags unless a smaller surviving contract proves them necessary.
- Simplify `yzx env`, `yzx launch`, `yzx enter`, startup, restart, and desktop fallback so they do not independently own `devenv` refresh/materialization decisions.
- Replace v14-bound refresh/profile tests with tests for the surviving state-repair/update/recovery contract, or delete them only when the workflow is explicitly v14-only.
acceptance_criteria:
- The feature-preservation gate names the retained owner for generated-config repair and stale generated-state recovery.
- Hot-path launch/enter/env no longer calls v14 `devenv` refresh or recorded launch-profile reuse.
- If `yzx refresh` is removed, recovery guidance points at a retained v15 command/path and not at a missing capability.
- Tests that only defend `devenv build shell` profile recording are removed or replaced by focused coverage for the retained recovery/update state contract.
notes:
2026-04-11 implementation checkpoint:
- Removed launch-profile reuse and explicit devenv refresh from `yzx launch`, `yzx enter`, `yzx env`, `yzx restart`, `start_yazelix.nu`, `launch_yazelix.nu`, popup/editor env recovery, doctor, and config-state freshness.
- Narrowed `yzx refresh` to generated Yazi/Zellij config repair plus materialized generated-state hashing; it no longer builds through `devenv` or records `DEVENV_PROFILE`.
- Added `runtime_env.nu` as the v15 state owner for runtime PATH/env activation.
- Focus checks passed: touched modules parse; full `core/yazelix.nu` imports; `yzx refresh --verbose` repaired generated state without invoking a build.
- Remaining before close: remove dead helper files/tests/docs that still defend launch_state/devenv_backend behavior.
close_reason: yzx refresh is now only generated-state repair, launch-profile reuse state was deleted, and the refresh/profile-era tests were removed or replaced.

title: Rehome or retire yzx run without losing environment command execution
description:
Implementation slice for the v15 trim. `yzx run` is currently a thin backend shell passthrough around `run_in_devenv_shell_command`, so the current implementation has no clear owner once v15 stops materializing a dynamic `devenv` environment.

This is not permission to drop the underlying user workflow. First decide whether v15 still needs a way to execute commands inside the Yazelix-provided environment. If yes, rehome or replace that capability under the retained runtime/entry owner. If no, document why it is v14/Yazelix Classic-only or maintainer-only.

Scope:
- Classify the `yzx run` workflow under the feature-preservation gate before editing code.
- If the public command dies, remove `yzx run` from exports, command palette/catalog, docs, and tests only after the replacement or non-goal is explicit.
- Delete `nushell/scripts/yzx/run.nu` only if no surviving internal helper needs it.
- Replace the run passthrough tests with coverage for the surviving command-execution workflow, or delete them only if that workflow is explicitly out of scope.
- If maintainers still need a repo command runner, keep that under maintainer/dev workflow instead of an accidental user-facing backend shell.
acceptance_criteria:
- The feature-preservation gate says whether command execution inside the Yazelix environment is kept, rehomed, replaced, maintainer-only, or v14-only.
- If `yzx run` is removed, users have a documented replacement path or an explicit migration/non-goal statement.
- No default-lane test exists solely to defend the old `devenv` passthrough, but surviving command-execution behavior has focused coverage if it remains part of v15.
- `run_in_devenv_shell_command` has no remaining caller whose only purpose is the old public runner surface.
notes:
2026-04-11 implementation checkpoint:
- Kept `yzx run` as a v15 user command.
- Rehomed execution from `run_in_devenv_shell_command` to direct argv execution under `runtime_env.nu`, preserving command execution inside the Yazelix runtime PATH/env.
- Focus check passed: `nu -c "use nushell/scripts/yzx/run.nu *; yzx run nu -c \"print 42\""` printed 42.
- Remaining before close: docs/help/test pruning must remove old devenv passthrough assumptions or replace them with direct runtime argv coverage.
close_reason: yzx run now executes directly under the runtime env contract, and the old devenv passthrough assumptions were removed from tests and user-facing docs.

title: Prune weak and v14-bound Nushell tests without losing retained coverage
description:
Prune weak and v14-bound Nushell tests during the v15 trim without deleting coverage for surviving workflows.

This bead exists to stop test LOC from fossilizing old architecture. The current suite still contains tests that are either low-uniqueness implementation trivia or tightly coupled to the `devenv` / `yzx refresh` / launch-profile / host-shell-hook era. Those tests should not force v15 to keep v14 internals.

The safety rule is equally important: test deletion follows the feature-preservation gate. If a user-visible workflow survives under a new owner, replace old tests with focused coverage for the new owner instead of deleting coverage.

Concrete candidates currently visible from the code:

Refresh / launch-profile era candidates:
- whole file `nushell/scripts/dev/test_yzx_refresh_commands.nu`, unless a retained generated-state repair owner needs some replacement coverage
- `test_yzx_doctor_reports_launch_profile_freshness_states`
- `test_profile_resolution_policies_separate_runtime_owned_and_current_session_state`
- `test_yzx_enter_forwards_refresh_intent_to_startup_entrypoint`
- `test_startup_refresh_activates_built_profile_without_second_shell_entry`
- `test_yzx_launch_refresh_uses_fresh_launch_profile_after_rebuild`
- `test_runtime_entry_state_models_live_session_refresh_intent`
- `test_runtime_entry_context_samples_environment_once`
- `test_startup_transition_prefers_fresh_runtime_profile_after_rebuild`
- `test_launch_transition_blocks_live_session_fast_path_during_refresh`
- `test_env_transition_keeps_cached_profile_reuse_explicit`
- `test_backend_shell_transition_tracks_rebuild_intent`
- `test_source_devenv_shell_clears_inherited_runtime_aliases`
- `test_build_shell_output_records_runtime_owned_profile_without_runtime_project_artifacts`

Weak / low-uniqueness / shell-hook trivia candidates:
- `test_generate_merged_zellij_config_wraps_nu_default_shell`
- `test_managed_nushell_config_sources_optional_user_hook`
- `test_generated_nushell_shell_hook_uses_managed_config_only`
- `test_managed_bash_config_sources_optional_user_hook`
- `test_managed_fish_config_does_not_export_helix_mode_env`
- `test_standard_shellhook_setup_skips_legacy_yzx_wrapper`
- `test_home_manager_shellhook_setup_skips_host_shell_surfaces`
- `test_nushell_initializer_restores_current_path_first`
design:
Be delete-first about v14-bound coverage, but preserve coverage for retained behavior.

Rules for this pass:
- If a test mainly defends `devenv`, `yzx refresh`, cached launch profiles, `DEVENV_PROFILE`, pack graph materialization, terminal wrapper breadth, or host shell-hook bootstrapping, assume it is obsolete unless the feature-preservation gate says that workflow survives.
- If the workflow survives under a new owner, replace the old test with a focused behavior test for the new owner.
- If a test mainly asserts implementation text, generated snippet shape, or low-level helper trivia, do not keep it just because it once caught a bug.
- Prefer deleting whole obsolete files over preserving tiny islands of era-specific coverage.
- Preserve workspace proof tests unless the implementation truly changes them: `yzx cwd`, `yzx reveal`, `yzx popup`, `yzx menu`, sidebar/Yazi refresh identity, layout/sidebar plugin state, and runtime-root-only leaf dispatch are part of the surviving app layer.

Classify candidates into:
1. delete now because the workflow is not part of v15
2. replace with new-owner coverage
3. delete when refresh/profile semantics die
4. delete when shell/terminal/config policy narrows
5. keep because it defends a surviving v15 contract
acceptance_criteria:
- There is a concrete kill/replace/keep list for weak and v14-bound tests.
- No test is deleted solely because its implementation owner changes; surviving workflows get replacement coverage.
- Workspace/app-layer tests are preserved unless the retained v15 behavior explicitly changes.
- The result cuts real v14 test LOC without weakening coverage for v15 user-visible contracts.
notes:
2026-04-10 code investigation update:
- Confirmed high-confidence delete-with-refresh candidates: whole `nushell/scripts/dev/test_yzx_refresh_commands.nu`; `test_yzx_run_passes_dash_prefixed_args_through_unchanged`; `test_yzx_run_treats_child_verbose_flag_as_child_argv`; launch-profile doctor test; runtime-entry state/transition tests around refresh/profile reuse; launch/enter refresh tests.
- Confirmed broader test fallout: stale-config diagnostics currently probes `yzx refresh`; startup/generated-layout failures currently tell users to run `yzx refresh`; shell-managed config tests contain `yzx refresh` recovery wording; installed runtime validators assume runtime-local `devenv` and `runtime/current`.
- Confirmed pack-sidecar test clusters: config migration E2E splits `[packs]` into `yazelix_packs.toml`, generated-config tests require pack sidecar copies and Home Manager sibling symlink behavior, doctor fix tests relocate pack config, edit tests assert `yzx edit packs`. These should die or move if packs leave core v15.
- Preserve the workspace proof tests unless the implementation changes them: `yzx cwd`, `yzx reveal`, `yzx popup`, `yzx menu`, sidebar/Yazi refresh identity, layout/sidebar plugin state, and runtime-root-only leaf dispatch are part of the surviving app layer.
- Practical sequencing: first classify the tests by the implementation deletion bead they belong to. Do not prune workspace leaf tests just because they mention runtime roots; prune tests that defend backend materialization, pack graph ownership, shell-hook host mutation, terminal wrapper breadth, or `DEVENV_PROFILE` caching.
close_reason: weak and v14-bound refresh, launch-profile, pack-sidecar, and shell-breadth tests were deleted or replaced, and the canonical yzx suite passes 75/75.

title: Narrow shell entry to runtime-provided shells and decide whether shell.extra_shells survives
description:
Narrow shell entry to runtime-provided shells and decide whether `shell.extra_shells` survives.

Current code evidence:
- `nushell/scripts/yzx/env.nu`
- `nushell/scripts/setup/environment.nu`
- `nushell/scripts/utils/common.nu` shell resolution helpers
- `config_metadata/main_config_contract.toml`
- `home_manager/module.nix`

The current v15 lean keeps multiple shells, but makes them runtime-provided and treats shell support as entry policy rather than backend-materialization policy.
design:
Define the v15 shell contract explicitly:
- runtime-provided shells, not host-provided shells
- `yzx env` as a thin handoff into the selected built shell
- decide what `shell.default_shell` still means
- decide whether `shell.extra_shells` survives, narrows, or dies
- decide which shell-hook generation and host dotfile ownership assumptions remain valid

The goal is to keep real shell choice without preserving the current shell/package-management swamp.
acceptance_criteria: There is a concrete v15 shell-entry contract covering runtime-provided shells, `yzx env`, `shell.default_shell`, the fate of `shell.extra_shells`, Home Manager shell ownership, and any retained user shell-hook story. The result is specific enough to drive later deletions in `env.nu`, `environment.nu`, the config contract, and Home Manager parity without losing shell choice silently.
notes:
2026-04-10 code investigation update:
- `nushell/scripts/setup/environment.nu` is the shell-hook/host-dotfile center. It parses `shell.default_shell` and `shell.extra_shells`, builds `shells_to_configure = [nu, bash] + default_shell + extra_shells`, detects Home Manager/read-only surfaces, generates shell initializers, syncs yzx extern bridges, and optionally edits bash/nushell/fish/zsh hook surfaces.
- `devenv.nix` is the package side of the same policy: `defaultShell`, `extraShells`, `includeFish`, `includeZsh`, and shell packages all exist to materialize those choices in the backend.
- `yzx env` currently remains backend-heavy: it resolves refresh requests, runtime entry context, launch-profile reuse, and falls back to `run_in_devenv_shell_command` for shell entry.
- For v15, keep only the shell choice that has a runtime owner. `shell.default_shell` may survive as entry policy if the runtime ships supported shells; `shell.extra_shells`, host dotfile hook installation, and dynamic backend shell-package materialization are guilty until proven needed.
- The deletion pass should separate current-terminal entry from host shell customization. Entering Yazelix is product behavior; rewriting arbitrary host shell configs is integration baggage unless v15 explicitly keeps it.
close_reason: the shell entry contract is narrowed to the retained runtime shells, with the broader extra-shell era trimmed out of the core v15 surface.

title: Narrow terminal and desktop ownership for v15
description:
Narrow terminal and desktop ownership for v15.

Current code evidence:
- `nushell/scripts/utils/terminal_launcher.nu`
- `nushell/scripts/core/launch_yazelix.nu`
- `nushell/scripts/yzx/desktop.nu`
- `config_metadata/main_config_contract.toml`

The current launch/desktop path still absorbs a large terminal matrix: wrappers, detection, config modes, managed-vs-user terminal policy, and desktop launch coupling.
design:
Reason from first principles:
- `yzx launch` and `yzx desktop` should survive only if they own a smaller, clearer promise
- the current terminal matrix should not survive by default just because it exists
- decide the supported terminal breadth for v15
- decide whether `manage_terminals` and `terminal.config_mode` still belong in the product
- keep desktop integration aligned with the retained launch story, not with historical compatibility paths
acceptance_criteria: There is a concrete v15 terminal/desktop contract naming the supported terminal breadth, the fate of `terminal.terminals`, `manage_terminals`, `terminal.config_mode`, nixGL handling, desktop launch, and the narrower ownership model for `yzx launch` and `yzx desktop`. The result preserves a reliable launch path for retained workflows and records a migration path or explicit non-goal for removed terminal wrapper/config behavior.
notes:
2026-04-10 code investigation update:
- `nushell/scripts/utils/terminal_launcher.nu` carries the terminal matrix: five terminal command renderers, wrapper-vs-direct detection, profile wrapper lookup, `DEVENV_PROFILE` profile-bin lookup, nixGL fallback search, config-mode resolution, detached launch probing, and terminal-specific cwd/title/class flags.
- `nushell/scripts/core/launch_yazelix.nu` adds runtime behavior on top: requested-terminal validation, desktop fast-path candidate selection, fallback bootstrap terminals, generated-terminal-config repair, `manage_terminals`, `terminal_config_mode`, and generated config checks.
- `nushell/scripts/yzx/launch.nu` couples terminal launch back into backend refresh by checking wrapper store paths, forcing rebuilds when managed terminal wrappers are stale, and falling back to `run_in_devenv_shell_command`.
- `nushell/scripts/yzx/desktop.nu` is mostly thin desktop integration, but `desktop launch` still has a fast path/fallback split through `launch_yazelix.nu` and `yzx launch --home`.
- `devenv.nix` builds terminal wrappers for Ghostty/Kitty/WezTerm/Alacritty/Foot and reads `terminal.terminals`, `manage_terminals`, and `terminal.config_mode`.
- For v15, decide whether Yazelix owns a fixed terminal path, a short host-terminal preference list, or managed terminal wrappers. Do not preserve the whole matrix just because v14 made it work.
close_reason: terminal and desktop ownership are narrowed to the retained host-terminal launch contract, without the old broad wrapper-management story.

title: Define the v15 config cutline before later migration deletion
description:
Define the v15 config cutline before any serious Rust rewrite starts.

Current code evidence:
- `config_metadata/main_config_contract.toml`
- `config_metadata/pack_catalog_contract.toml`
- `home_manager/module.nix`
- `docs/specs/config_surface_backend_dependence_matrix.md`

The point is to leave one explicit keep/narrow/rehome/drop config surface for the trimmed v15 product, rather than carrying the current 44-field contract into a rewrite and deciding later what should have died. The cutline must preserve meaning where workflows survive, remove dead knobs where ownership is gone, and keep Home Manager parity exact.
design:
Build this only after the command, refresh/state, packs, shell, and terminal decisions are clearer.

The output should:
- list which main-config fields survive untouched
- list which fields narrow in meaning
- list which fields die with `devenv`, explicit refresh, packs, or terminal breadth
- identify the Home Manager parity impact
- define the threshold at which the surviving surface is small enough that Rust work stops being mixed with product deletion
acceptance_criteria: There is a concrete v15 keep/narrow/rehome/drop config matrix and a clear statement of the trim threshold that should be reached before Rust becomes the next main lane. The result is specific enough to guide config-contract edits, Home Manager narrowing, migration/non-goal wording for removed fields, and later Rust planning. Home Manager exposes the same surviving surface as default config and no dead v14 backend knobs.
notes:
2026-04-10 code investigation update:
- The config cutline has to follow the stronger v15 goal, not just package-runtime-first. The existing specs distinguish package-runtime-only from backend-free; v15 is now aiming at the backend-free/slimmer path by dropping `devenv` and package-manager behavior.
- Backend/package graph fields to question first: `core.recommended_deps`, `core.yazi_extensions`, `core.yazi_media`, `core.max_jobs`, `core.build_cores`, `helix.mode`, `helix.runtime_path`, `shell.extra_shells`, `terminal.terminals` when used for package materialization, and all pack sidecar paths.
- Runtime/workspace fields are stronger survivors: sidebar/editor workspace UX, Zellij session/layout UX, Yazi UX, welcome UX if startup UI stays, and tool command locators if host/runtime prerequisites stay explicit.
- Likely deletion/narrowing after the child decisions: `core.refresh_output`, pack sidecar config and Home Manager pack options, `shell.extra_shells`, dynamic build knobs, managed-terminal wrapper policy, and rebuild hash tracking for `devenv.lock`/`devenv.nix`/`devenv.yaml`.
- Home Manager parity is a real part of the cutline: removing a config field means removing the HM option and generated TOML output too, not leaving declarative dead knobs behind.
- Validators/tests to revisit after the cutline: `validate_config_surface_contract.nu`, pack catalog validation, generated config tests around pack sidecars, shell-hook tests, terminal config mode tests, and installed-runtime validators that assume `devenv` is part of the runtime.
2026-04-13 update: superseded in retention policy by yazelix-qgj7.6. The config cutline remains useful as historical narrowing context, but the live v15 contract now deletes config-migration machinery instead of preserving migration safety as a standing product requirement.
close_reason: the v15 config cutline is now applied across config metadata, shipped defaults, Home Manager parity, diagnostics, and generated-config tests.

title: Define the v15 retained command surface and hot-path runtime model
description:
Define the retained v15 command surface and the hot-path runtime model.

Ground this in the current code paths:
- `nushell/scripts/yzx/env.nu`
- `nushell/scripts/yzx/launch.nu`
- `nushell/scripts/yzx/refresh.nu`
- `nushell/scripts/yzx/run.nu`
- `nushell/scripts/yzx/desktop.nu`
- `nushell/scripts/utils/launch_state.nu`

The current v15 lean is:
- keep `yzx launch`, `yzx env`, `yzx update`, and `yzx desktop` as thin commands with clear owners
- decide whether `yzx run` and `yzx refresh` are retained, replaced, rehomed to maintainer/dev workflow, or marked v14/Yazelix Classic-only
- make `yzx env` and `yzx launch` enter an already-built runtime instead of paying a dynamic backend/materialization cost on the hot path
- make `yzx update` the cold path that swaps to a newer built runtime

The command decision must preserve the user workflow where it remains part of v15. Do not delete a command just because its v14 implementation is backend-owned; first decide whether the capability survives under a new owner.
design:
Reason from first principles:
- command count is not the real problem
- hot-path latency and mixed ownership are the real problem
- if `yzx env`/`yzx launch` only activate a built runtime, the old `devenv` entry semantics stop being necessary
- `yzx run` and `yzx refresh` must justify themselves as first-class product commands, be replaced under a retained owner, move to maintainer/dev workflow, or be explicitly declared v14/Yazelix Classic-only
- command deletion must not remove environment command execution, generated-state repair, desktop launch, or update ownership unless those are explicit v15 non-goals

Leave behind an explicit keep/rehome/replace/v14-only/drop matrix for the command surface and a one-page hot-path model for entry versus update.
acceptance_criteria: There is an explicit v15 command contract with a keep/rehome/replace/v14-only/drop decision for `launch`, `env`, `update`, `desktop`, `run`, and `refresh`. The result also defines the hot path (`env`/`launch` into a built runtime) versus the cold path (`update` as rebuild/swap work) clearly enough to drive later deletion beads. Every command removal names the preserved workflow, replacement owner, migration path, or explicit non-goal.
notes:
2026-04-10 code investigation update:
- The exported yzx surface is not one flat runtime API. `nushell/scripts/core/yazelix.nu` and `nushell/scripts/yzx/*.nu` split into workspace/app leaves, config/discoverability leaves, runtime/distribution leaves, and backend/package-manager leaves.
- Strong keep candidates for v15 are workspace/app UX: `yzx cwd`, `yzx reveal`, `yzx popup`, `yzx screen`, `yzx menu` UI, `yzx keys`, `yzx tutor`, `yzx config`, `yzx edit` except `edit packs` if packs die, and import helpers. These are not the package-manager problem.
- Strong delete/narrow candidates are the backend/package-manager leaves: `yzx run`, `yzx refresh`, `yzx packs`, `yzx gc`, and the parts of `launch`/`enter`/`restart` that call `resolve_refresh_request`, `rebuild_yazelix_environment`, `run_in_devenv_shell_command`, or recorded launch-profile reuse.
- Current `yzx launch` and `yzx enter` are mixed: public meaning should survive, but hot-path implementation still routes through refresh decisions, launch-profile reuse, managed terminal wrapper repair, and fallback `devenv shell` execution.
- Current package shape still bakes in the old backend: `packaging/mk_runtime_tree.nix` links `devenv.lock`, `devenv.nix`, `devenv.yaml`, `bin/devenv`, and `bin/nu`; `packaging/mk_yazelix_package.nix` wraps `yzx` with PATH containing locked `devenv`, `nix`, and POSIX helper tools.
- For v15, the useful contract is: hot path enters a built/runtime-provided Yazelix stack; cold path belongs to package manager/update owner; workspace/app commands remain; package-manager commands must justify themselves or die.
close_reason: the retained v15 command surface is explicit in code now: launch/env/update/desktop stay thin, run is a real argv passthrough, and refresh is only generated-state repair.

title: Replace v14 refresh and launch-profile semantics with an explicit v15 state contract
description:
Plan the replacement of v14 launch-profile reuse and explicit refresh semantics with a smaller v15 state/recovery contract.

Current code evidence:
- `nushell/scripts/yzx/refresh.nu`
- `nushell/scripts/utils/launch_state.nu`
- `nushell/scripts/utils/devenv_backend.nu`
- related specs around launch profiles, runtime activation state, and backend capabilities

If v15 enters a built runtime directly, the current `DEVENV_PROFILE` reuse path, recorded launch-profile state, and explicit refresh command need either a much smaller replacement or deletion. But refresh-like user value still has to be classified: generated config repair, stale generated-state recovery, update/rebuild handoff, and clear diagnostics must not vanish silently.
design:
Map the current state model first, then delete-first:
- what state today exists only to accelerate or validate `devenv`-backed entry?
- what state still matters when `env`/`launch` stop being backend-materialization surfaces?
- which generated-config repair and stale-state recovery workflows survive under a v15 owner?
- which files/specs/tests become dead once `yzx refresh` and launch-profile reuse disappear?

Do not design a new cache first. Delete the old `DEVENV_PROFILE`/launch-profile/rebuild-state semantics first, but keep the minimum replacement state/recovery contract that a built-runtime model still requires.
acceptance_criteria: There is an explicit deletion/replacement map for `yzx refresh`, recorded launch-profile state, `DEVENV_PROFILE`-centric fast paths, generated-config repair, stale generated-state recovery, and the related specs/tests. The result names what can be deleted outright, what needs a replacement contract, what should stay until later implementation, and what user-facing recovery guidance replaces any removed `yzx refresh` path.
notes:
2026-04-10 code investigation update:
- `nushell/scripts/yzx/refresh.nu` is almost entirely explicit backend ownership: it runs `devenv build shell`, decides refresh/noop/force, regenerates Yazi/Zellij configs, records materialized state, and records launch-profile state. If `yzx refresh` dies, this file should mostly disappear rather than be rewritten.
- `nushell/scripts/utils/launch_state.nu` is the largest state target: it persists `state/launch_state.json`, parses `DEVENV_PROFILE` from build shell output, reads runtime-project `.devenv/profile` and `.devenv/gc/shell`, resolves recorded vs live vs ambient profiles, and constructs profile-derived launch env.
- `nushell/scripts/utils/devenv_backend.nu` currently owns the shared state machine: `resolve_refresh_request`, `resolve_runtime_entry_context`, launch/startup/env/backend-shell transitions, `rebuild_yazelix_environment`, and `run_in_devenv_shell_command`. This should shrink sharply if v15 no longer materializes `devenv` on entry.
- Callers that must be simplified together: `yzx/env.nu`, `yzx/launch.nu`, `core/start_yazelix.nu`, `core/yazelix.nu` restart, `yzx/run.nu`, and `yzx/desktop.nu` fallback launch.
- Test fallout is already clear: the whole `test_yzx_refresh_commands.nu` file, launch-profile doctor tests, runtime-entry transition tests, and launch/enter refresh tests are v14-bound unless a much smaller v15 state contract replaces them.
- Delete-first rule for this bead: do not design another cache until the old `DEVENV_PROFILE`/launch-profile/rebuild-state semantics have been removed from the hot path.
close_reason: the v14 refresh and launch-profile model was replaced by the smaller v15 state contract, with launch_state removed and only generated-state repair retained.

title: Decide the fate of packs and package-graph ownership in v15
description:
Decide the fate of packs and package-graph ownership in v15.

Current code evidence:
- `nushell/scripts/yzx/packs.nu`
- `config_metadata/pack_catalog_contract.toml`
- `docs/specs/config_surface_backend_dependence_matrix.md`
- `home_manager/module.nix` pack fields

Today the entire pack surface is backend-owned and rebuild-relevant. That makes it a major v15 decision point once `devenv` and dynamic runtime composition are on the chopping block. Packs also represent a real power-user workflow: custom tool/runtime composition. The decision is about the right owner for that workflow, not just deleting the current implementation.
design:
Evaluate only real options:
- keep a narrow pack-like capability in v15 core with a clear non-`devenv` owner
- rehome custom package/tool composition to Home Manager, a user Nix flake, or another package-manager-owned path
- keep packs as v14/Yazelix Classic-only
- narrow packs to a small fixed preset model if that still solves a real v15 problem
- defer a richer pack/runtime idea to a separate future runtime/devenv project, not as core v15 baggage
- delete current pack behavior only if the workflow is explicitly out of scope or has a migration path

Do not keep the current pack surface by inertia. Also do not silently erase the power-user composition workflow just because the v14 implementation lives in `devenv.nix`.
acceptance_criteria: There is a clear keep/narrow/rehome/v14-only/drop decision for packs in v15, plus an explicit statement of what happens to `yzx packs`, `yazelix_packs.toml`, Home Manager pack options, `user_packages`, pack declarations, and pack-driven rebuild inputs. If custom tool/runtime composition remains supported, the result names its v15 owner or migration path; if it does not, the result records the explicit non-goal or v14/Yazelix Classic boundary.
notes:
2026-04-10 code investigation update:
- `nushell/scripts/yzx/packs.nu` is not just a display helper. It parses Yazelix config, inspects runtime-project `.devenv/gc/shell` and `.devenv/profile`, scans session PATH for `/nix/store`, runs `nix path-info`, measures store paths, and maps package names to pack declarations. That is package-manager/runtime-materialization logic.
- The pack surface crosses many files: `config_metadata/pack_catalog_contract.toml`, `yazelix_packs_default.toml`, `config_surfaces.nu`, `config_parser.nu`, `config_state.nu` rebuild `extra_paths`, `home_manager/module.nix` pack options, config migration tests, doctor fix tests, and generated-config tests.
- `devenv.nix` is the actual executor: it reads `yazelix_packs.toml`, rejects duplicate inline packs, resolves package names through nixpkgs/llm-agents/fenix/tru special cases, expands `pack_declarations`, and appends `packPackages` plus `user_packages` into `allDeps`.
- If v15 stops trying to be a package manager and drops `devenv`, the likely deletion is broad: `yzx packs`, `yzx edit packs`, pack sidecar generation, Home Manager `pack_names`/`pack_declarations`/`user_packages`, pack config migrations, rebuild hash extra paths, and pack catalog validation.
- Preserve only if a new owner is explicit. A future optional runtime/project could reintroduce packs later, but core v15 should not carry the current dynamic package-graph surface by inertia.
close_reason: the v15 core no longer owns packs or the package graph; removed pack surfaces are now migration-only and rejected as unsupported config.

title: Define the trim-first v15 architecture reset before later Rust work
description:
Define `yzxcore` as the historical internal codename for the v15 architecture reset in the same repo. The useful current lesson from this bead is the trim-first product reset: keep the repo, name, stars, and history while deleting confused ownership, duplicate runtime layers, and package-manager responsibilities before any later Rust work.

This bead predates the 2026-04-13 release-framing decision. It should no longer be read as saying v15.0 is Rust-first. The current contract is: v15.0 ships as the trimmed non-Rust reboot, selective Rust slices may land later in v15.x when they pay for themselves, and v16 is the Rust-forward release.

Before implementation deletion starts, classify the major old workflows as live v15.0 core, narrower v15.0 core, rehomed under a different owner, historical-only, future separate project material, or explicit non-product behavior.
design:
Reason from first principles and from the code that still defined the mammoth shape when this bead was created.

First gate the feature/workflow contract, then delete implementation ownership. The live candidates for deletion/narrowing were visible in command wrappers, launch-profile state, pack-driven package ownership, shell/terminal breadth, and the oversized config surface.

Protect the real product value while trimming: workspace/session UX, command execution inside the Yazelix environment if it remains a current promise, generated config repair/recovery only where it still belongs, shell choice where retained, reliable desktop launch, and Home Manager declarative ownership.

The point of `yzxcore` as a codename was to make seams easier to reason about, not to create a second permanent product name. The current framing supersedes the old wording: Rust is not the way to discover v15.0, and it is not a prerequisite for v15.0. Rust belongs after the product contract is smaller and clearer, with v16 as the Rust-forward release framing.
acceptance_criteria: There is a concrete historical plan for the feature-preserving v15 architecture reset in the existing Yazelix repo. The result explains why the same repo should be kept, why trim-first in the same tree was the preferred path, what the v15 product contract is, what command/workflow families survive, what gets rehomed or marked historical-only, what gets intentionally dropped as non-product behavior, and why later Rust work must wait until the surviving surface is smaller. No implementation deletion bead is ready unless it names the preserved capability, replacement owner, migration path, or explicit non-goal.
notes:
2026-04-10 investigation summary:
- v15 delete-first work should target the stronger backend-free/slim line, not merely package-runtime-first. The code still bundles workspace UX with `devenv` materialization, package graph management, shell hooks, terminal wrapper management, and installer/runtime-current assumptions.
- Code hotspots read this pass: `yzx/env.nu`, `yzx/launch.nu`, `yzx/refresh.nu`, `yzx/run.nu`, `yzx/packs.nu`, `yzx/desktop.nu`, `core/yazelix.nu`, `core/start_yazelix.nu`, `core/launch_yazelix.nu`, `utils/devenv_backend.nu`, `utils/launch_state.nu`, `utils/terminal_launcher.nu`, `setup/environment.nu`, config contracts, Home Manager module, `devenv.nix`, and packaging wrappers.
- Delete-first axis: remove package-manager/backend ownership first (`yzx run`, `yzx refresh`, launch-profile state, packs, `devenv` runtime packaging), then narrow shell/terminal/config surfaces, then prune v14-bound tests.
- Keep axis: workspace/session and app UX from the pane-orchestrator era should survive: cwd/reveal/popup/menu/screen/keys/tutor/config/import/edit non-pack surfaces, layout/sidebar behavior, and thin desktop/update integration if they have clear owners.
- Rust should remain behind the trim gate. Rewriting before deleting would preserve the two-products-in-one problem in a different language.
2026-04-13 update: superseded in release framing by yazelix-qgj7.7. v15.0 is now the trimmed non-Rust reboot; selective Rust slices may land later in v15.x, and v16 is the Rust-forward release. Treat this bead as the historical trim-first reset record, not as current evidence that v15.0 should be Rust-first.
close_reason: the trim-first v15 reset is now reflected in the live code: command, state, packs, shell, terminal, config, test, and packaged-runtime ownership are all narrowed and verified.

title: Ship v14 as the capstone release of the current Yazelix architecture and preserve it for the v15 trim-first transition
description: Plan the v14 release as the capstone of the current integrated Yazelix architecture. The goal is to ship the current mammoth honestly as v14, then use that release boundary to justify the v15 architecture reset in the same repo rather than trying to finish the new future architecture first.
design:
Reason from first principles:
- The current repo is already functional enough to earn a real release on its own terms.
- The important release boundary is narrative and architectural: v14 closes the integrated/devenv-heavy era; v15 begins the slimmer future line.
- The preservation mechanism should stay lightweight and explicit: a real v14 tag plus a preservation branch if needed, not a permanent parallel product effort.
- The output should define what still must land before v14, what should explicitly wait for v15, and how the current architecture is preserved for reference once the trim-first v15 work starts in the same tree.
- Be explicit that preservation does not imply indefinite active maintenance after v15 lands.
acceptance_criteria: There is a clear plan for shipping v14 as the capstone release of the current Yazelix architecture. The result defines what belongs in v14, what should wait for v15, and how the old architecture is preserved for reference once the same-tree trim-first v15 work begins.
notes: Completed: v14 was shipped/documented as Yazelix Classic, with the v14 tag/line preserved for bug fixes while v15 proceeds as the trim-first branch.
close_reason: Completed: v14 Classic preservation and v15 trim-first transition plan are documented.

title: Define the v15 Yazelix product contract and trim-first transition
description:
Define the post-v14 product contract for Yazelix as a trim-first transition in the existing repo.

The older framing of "Core as a supported profile" no longer matches the real question. The code now points at a more concrete v15 problem:
- which commands survive
- which backend/runtime responsibilities disappear
- which config axes survive
- which deletions must happen before any Rust rewrite is worth attempting

This bead should keep the strategic decision grounded in the current hot modules and contracts rather than in naming or branding alone.
design:
Reason from first principles and from the real code seams.

The current support cost is not just "too many commands". The main tax comes from mixed ownership across:
- `nushell/scripts/yzx/env.nu`
- `nushell/scripts/yzx/refresh.nu`
- `nushell/scripts/yzx/packs.nu`
- `nushell/scripts/utils/devenv_backend.nu`
- `nushell/scripts/utils/launch_state.nu`
- `nushell/scripts/utils/terminal_launcher.nu`
- `nushell/scripts/setup/environment.nu`
- `config_metadata/main_config_contract.toml`
- `home_manager/module.nix`

Those seams show the real v15 questions:
1. What is the retained command surface?
   - current leaning: keep `yzx launch`, `yzx env`, `yzx update`, `yzx desktop`
   - likely drop `yzx run` and `yzx refresh`
2. What is the hot-path runtime model?
   - shell entry should activate an already-built runtime, not evaluate/build a Nix environment on every entry
3. What runtime/config responsibilities should disappear with `devenv`?
   - launch-profile reuse
   - explicit refresh semantics
   - dynamic pack-driven package-graph ownership, unless a narrower replacement survives
4. What policy breadth is still justified?
   - runtime-provided shells likely stay
   - `shell.extra_shells` is still open
   - terminal breadth and desktop ownership need a narrower contract
5. What must be trimmed before Rust work begins?
   - do not mix product-boundary deletion with the language rewrite

The output should leave a concrete trim-first queue for v15, not just a philosophy statement. Keep the naming question (`profile` vs `mode` vs no special term at all) explicitly open, because current Yazelix already uses `profile` heavily for startup and `DEVENV_PROFILE` semantics.
acceptance_criteria: There is a concrete post-v14 product-contract direction for v15 Yazelix that is grounded in the current code seams. The result names the retained command surface, the hot-path runtime model, the main deletions tied to dropping `devenv`, and the trim-first sequencing that should happen before any serious Rust rewrite. The output is specific enough to drive child beads for shell/runtime entry, refresh/launch-profile deletion, packs fate, terminal/desktop narrowing, and the v15 config cutline.
notes: Completed: v15 product contract is now grounded in the README/docs and the active trim queue: narrower yzx surface, no runtime-local devenv, package-runtime ownership, Home Manager/manual owner boundaries, and trim-first before Rust rewrite.
close_reason: Completed: v15 product contract and trim-first transition are defined enough to drive implementation beads.

title: Purge stale update and uninstall ownership references after the package-runtime cleanup

Keep this delete-first: remove stale references where possible instead of layering more compatibility wording on top.

The docs should warn users not to mix both update owners for the same installed Yazelix runtime, and should stop implying that Yazelix still owns a generic runtime-update surface.

title: Add honest update wrappers for upstream installs and Home Manager
description: Reintroduce a small, honest update surface after the delete-first cleanup. Add yzx update upstream and yzx update home_manager, and make bare yzx update explain the two supported ownership paths instead of pretending Yazelix owns all updates. yzx update upstream should print an ownership warning, print the exact command, and run nix run github:luccahuguet/yazelix#install. yzx update home_manager should print the same ownership warning, print the exact nix flake lock --update-input yazelix command it will run in the current Home Manager flake directory, run only that command, and then print home-manager switch for the user to copy instead of running it. title: Stop Home Manager from materializing runtime_current as product identity
description: Rework the Home Manager path so it no longer treats `~/.local/share/yazelix/runtime/current` as the primary runtime identity. The current module still creates `xdg.dataFile."yazelix/runtime/current"`, exports that path during generated-config activation, and documents it as a first-class managed surface. Move Home Manager toward using the profile/package runtime directly where possible, keep generated config/state semantics honest, and remove the extra runtime-current indirection from the Home Manager-first product model.
close_reason: Completed

title: Delete yzx update runtime and collapse installer-artifact doctoring
description: Delete `yzx update runtime` and collapse installer-artifact doctoring after the runtime identity model no longer depends on installed-runtime ownership. Ground this in the current code cluster: `core/yazelix.nu`, `runtime_distribution_capabilities.nu`, `doctor.nu`, and `doctor_install_artifacts.nu` still spend real surface area on installer-managed, Home Manager-managed, package-runtime, and runtime-root-only branching. Replace that with a thinner story centered on package-manager updates, Home Manager reapply flows, and command checks that defend real runtime/backend prerequisites instead of installer artifact ownership.
close_reason: Completed

title: Remove runtime_current and stable-launcher ownership from shared runtime logic
description: Remove `runtime/current` and stable-launcher ownership from the shared runtime model after the package-first and Home Manager runtime-identity work is in place. Ground this in the real code paths: `common.nu` still has installed-runtime and runtime-reference helpers, `install_ownership.nu` still treats manual runtime/current and `~/.local/bin/yzx` as central ownership surfaces, and multiple commands still rely on those helpers. Simplify the common runtime identity story so package runtime roots and explicit `YAZELIX_RUNTIME_DIR` become the normal path instead of installed-runtime indirection.
notes: This bead should focus on common helpers and CLI/runtime callers after the Home Manager-specific runtime_current cleanup is settled. Callers that currently hard-code installed-runtime assumptions include `common.nu`, `install_ownership.nu`, `desktop.nu`, and adjacent doctor/update helpers.
close_reason: Completed

title: Define the package-runtime-first user and maintainer UX
description: Document the canonical user and maintainer flow for package-runtime-first Yazelix. Be explicit that the normal surfaces should be package-first commands such as `nix run .#yazelix -- ...`, `nix profile install ...#yazelix`, profile/system/Home Manager updates, and packaged-runtime dogfooding. Clarify what happens to `#install`, what remains true about `yazelix.toml` and `yazelix_packs.toml`, and how maintainer development differs from packaged-runtime validation.
close_reason: Documented the package-runtime-first user and maintainer flow and clarified the installer-first phase-1 doc

title: Simplify Yazelix to a package-runtime-first distribution model
description: Shift Yazelix away from acting as its own installer/distribution manager and toward a flake-package-first product model. The main supported install and update paths should be the flake package surface and Home Manager, not an installer-owned mutable runtime identity. Yazelix should keep owning config semantics, backend/environment behavior if retained, and workspace UX. This lane should delete installer-owned runtime/current and stable-launcher assumptions where they are no longer justified, make `#yazelix` and Home Manager the primary product surfaces, and leave behind cleaner user and maintainer flows without treating upstream nixpkgs submission as a target.
notes:
2026-04-11 plan-space review of follow-up ownership beads:
- `yazelix-4buc.12` is the shared stable external `yzx` launcher-resolution seam, not a Home Manager-specific restart patch. The surviving contract is that restart must prefer an owner-provided external wrapper over the current session runtime-pinned command path.
- `yazelix-4buc.14` preserves cleanup behavior while refusing `yzx desktop install` in Home Manager mode. Refusal must not remove the ability to clean up a stale manual entry; `yzx desktop uninstall` remains part of the recovery path.
- `yazelix-4buc.15` stays narrow: repair only an already-existing Yazelix-managed manual desktop entry, never recreate desktop integration after `yzx desktop uninstall`, and never materialize a manual desktop entry in Home Manager-managed mode. It depends on both the shared stable-launcher seam from `yazelix-4buc.12` and the Home Manager desktop ownership decision from `yazelix-4buc.14`.
- `yazelix-4buc.13` remains one non-default Home Manager smoke lane. Its purpose is to validate the narrowed owner contract without relying on any user-local compatibility launcher or desktop entry.
- `yazelix-4buc.16` documents the final stale-live-session contract only after `yazelix-4buc.12`, `yazelix-4buc.13`, `yazelix-4buc.14`, and `yazelix-4buc.15` are settled.
close_reason: Completed: the package-runtime-first ownership cleanup is finished across update flows, launcher resolution, Home Manager desktop ownership, installer desktop behavior, and stale-live-session docs.

2026-04-08 verification guidance:
- Prefer one focused regression or contract validator per surviving seam over broad command-smoke checks.
- For the current shortlist, verification should stay close to the user-visible contract: doctor output families, maintainer command routing, migration-plan behavior, generated-config semantics, and runtime/path resolution.

2026-04-08 concrete verification example:

2026-04-08 second verification example:

title: Step 3: simplify and DRY only the surviving paths
description: For candidate refactors under `yazelix-790`, simplify and apply DRY only after dead or redundant paths have been removed. The goal is to make the surviving contract smaller and clearer instead of optimizing code that should have been deleted.
notes:
2026-04-08 first concrete simplification landed:
- Simplified the surviving Home Manager activation path in `yzx/dev.nu` by replacing deprecated `nix flake lock --update-input` usage with `nix flake update <input> --flake <dir>`.
- This is the intended shape for step 3: improve a surviving path only after deleting dead alternatives and overlap first.
- The simplification also tightened the user-facing recovery guidance and the maintainer regression to one explicit command form.

2026-04-08 likely step-3 candidates after more deletion work:
- A thinner `yzx dev` dispatcher once maintainer workflow families stop sharing one implementation owner.
- Thinner doctor result composition after diagnostic families are separated.
- A narrower shared runtime/path module only after `common.nu` loses unrelated convenience and maintainer-only helpers.

2026-04-08 second concrete simplification/decomposition sequence:
- After the alias/dead-export deletions in `common.nu`, build/Nix policy moved into `build_policy.nu`, and runtime-project ownership moved into `runtime_project.nu`.
- After questioning `config_migrations.nu`, preview/load behavior moved into `config_migration_preview.nu` so plan/apply code no longer owns user-facing preview rendering and file-loading wrappers.

title: Step 2: delete unnecessary parts or processes first
description: For candidate refactors under `yazelix-790`, delete dead paths, redundant layers, convenience wrappers, or obsolete processes before introducing new abstractions. The goal is to remove what should not survive instead of reorganizing it.
notes:
2026-04-08 third concrete deletion landed:
- Deleted `doctor.nu`'s standalone shell-integration check after confirming the real install-artifact and shell-hook diagnostics already covered the supported contract.
- Step 2 now has three concrete wins: dead bootstrap path deleted, dead zellij debug exports deleted, and overlapping doctor warning deleted.

2026-04-08 next delete-first candidates from the hotspot scan:
- `common.nu`: generic aliases and unrelated maintainer/build helpers living beside runtime/path ownership.
- `config_migrations.nu`: overlap between rule metadata ownership and preview/render/apply behavior.
- `integrations/yazi.nu`: editor-open routing and file-backed sidebar identity scanning mixed into one integration layer.

2026-04-08 fourth concrete deletion landed:

- This confirms the common.nu bead should keep preferring surface deletion and caller explicitness before any new module extraction.

2026-04-08 fifth concrete delete-first result landed:
- After the first alias cut, `common.nu` no longer needed to own build policy or runtime-project ownership. Those families moved into `build_policy.nu` and `runtime_project.nu`, and the dead runtime-project internals were deleted from `common.nu`.
- This kept the refactor aligned with step 2: remove mixed ownership from the old grab-bag module instead of merely reorganizing it under a new generic helper layer.
