title: Step 1: question refactor requirements before changing code
description: For candidate refactors under `yazelix-790`, first challenge the requirements and assumptions that made the current code shape seem necessary. The goal is to identify dumb or outdated constraints before any code is moved, extracted, or optimized.
notes:
2026-04-08 LOC-driven requirement refinement:
- Current repo-local count is roughly 35.5k Nushell lines across 143 `.nu` files under `nushell/`.
- This changes the requirement from “clean up a few bad files” to “materially streamline the Nushell control plane before upstream packaging”.
- Directory concentration matters: `nushell/scripts/dev` and `nushell/scripts/utils` alone account for the majority of the Nushell surface, but even excluding `dev`, the production/control-plane surface is still very large.
- Step-1 conclusion update: upstream confidence should not depend on explaining away this size as normal. The backlog should prefer real surface reduction in `utils`, `yzx`, `setup`, and `integrations` before the nixpkgs PR bead moves forward.

2026-04-08 requirement refinement after the file audit:
- The right requirement is not “shrink every large Nushell file”.
- The right requirement is “reduce mixed-owner control-plane surface before upstreaming”.
- Large-but-cohesive modules like `utils/ascii_art.nu`, `core/yazelix.nu`, and probably `setup/yazi_config_merger.nu` should not dominate the streamlining lane.
- Under-modeled first-wave hotspots are now `utils/common.nu` and `utils/config_migrations.nu` in addition to `doctor.nu`, `yzx/dev.nu`, `integrations/yazi.nu`, and `setup/zellij_config_merger.nu`.
close_reason: Completed

title: Separate stable pane-orchestrator client API from dead zellij debug exports
description: `nushell/scripts/integrations/zellij.nu` mixes the stable workspace/pane API with several debug-only exports. Repo search shows `debug_write_literal`, `debug_send_escape`, and `debug_open_file_via_plugin` are currently only referenced at their own definitions. Delete-first goal: remove or isolate dead debug probes before doing broader API splitting so the surviving module reflects the real pane-orchestrator client contract.
notes:
2026-04-08 implementation:
- Deleted the dead `debug_write_literal`, `debug_send_escape`, and `debug_open_file_via_plugin` exports from `nushell/scripts/integrations/zellij.nu`.
- Verification: repo-wide search now finds no remaining references to those names; importing the surviving pane-orchestrator client functions still succeeds; `validate_default_test_traceability.nu` passes.
- This confirms the step-1 requirement cut: those debug probes were not part of the supported zellij-integration contract.
close_reason: Completed: deleted the dead zellij debug exports and kept the supported pane-orchestrator client API.

title: Collapse environment_bootstrap.nu onto one devenv shell-entry path
description: `nushell/scripts/utils/environment_bootstrap.nu` currently exports both `run_in_devenv_shell` and `run_in_devenv_shell_command`, but repo search shows only the command-args variant has real callers. The string-command variant appears to be dead convenience surface that still carries overlapping env-handling, refresh, and shell-entry logic. Delete-first goal: remove the unused shell-entry path instead of keeping two bootstrap APIs that do the same job slightly differently.
notes:
2026-04-08 implementation:
- Deleted the unused `run_in_devenv_shell` export from `nushell/scripts/utils/environment_bootstrap.nu`.
- Verification: repo-wide search now finds no remaining `run_in_devenv_shell` references; importing `run_in_devenv_shell_command` from the module still succeeds; `validate_default_test_traceability.nu` passes.
- This confirms the requirement cut from step 1: Yazelix only needed the argv-based shell-entry contract.
close_reason: Completed: deleted the unused string-command shell-entry export and kept the argv-based bootstrap path as the sole contract.

title: Define whether no-op yzx refresh should repair missing generated runtime configs
description: Today `yzx refresh` only regenerates runtime-owned Yazi and Zellij configs when it is already doing rebuild work. If config/input state is up to date, the command exits early with "already up to date" and does not repair missing generated runtime configs. That may be fine, or it may leave `yzx refresh` weaker as a repair entrypoint than users expect. Track the decision explicitly instead of letting the behavior drift.
design: Keep this delete-first. First decide whether missing generated runtime configs are actually part of the contract that `yzx refresh` should repair on a no-op path, or whether that responsibility belongs only to startup and Home Manager activation. If refresh should be a repair surface, prefer a narrow generated-config repair step over broad extra work or duplicate rebuild logic. If refresh should stay rebuild-only on the no-op path, document that clearly and rely on the real repair entrypoints instead.
acceptance_criteria: 1. There is an explicit decision on whether a no-op `yzx refresh` should repair missing generated Yazi/Zellij runtime configs. 2. If yes, the implementation repairs those artifacts without forcing unnecessary rebuild work. 3. If no, the decision is documented and the intended repair entrypoint is clear. 4. A focused regression or validator defends the chosen contract.
close_reason: Completed: no-op yzx refresh now repairs missing generated Yazi/Zellij runtime configs without forcing a rebuild, defended by a focused refresh regression.

title: Remove the visible Ctrl+y helper pane while preserving sidebar refresh correctness
description: `Ctrl y` still routes through a transient `Run "nu" ... close_on_exit true` helper pane so Yazelix can first ask the pane orchestrator to toggle focus and then, when the target becomes the sidebar, call `refresh_active_sidebar_yazi`. The recent focus-context fix restored correctness, but the visible helper-pane flash and remaining latency are still user-visible. Track the next-stage cleanup here: remove the visible helper pane from the `Ctrl y` path without regressing sidebar freshness or editor/sidebar focus behavior.
design: Keep this delete-first. First verify whether `Ctrl y` still needs an explicit post-focus Yazi refresh at all, given the current Yazi-side event hooks and the event-driven freshness work already landed. If the explicit refresh has become redundant, delete the wrapper flow entirely and make `Ctrl y` a pure pane-orchestrator `MessagePlugin` action. If explicit refresh is still required, delete only the visible helper pane: keep the focus toggle inside the pane orchestrator and move the refresh trigger onto a silent path such as a plugin-owned background command or equivalent worker-mediated bridge, rather than a foreground `Run "nu"` pane. Do not add polling, more sleeps, or another visible helper pane. Also do not treat the current sidebar-state cache as a stronger correctness dependency just to make `Ctrl y` faster; if the chosen fix still leans on current Yazi identity seams, record that explicitly as a temporary boundary and keep the longer-term cleanup aligned with `yazelix-0hm3`.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Pressing `Ctrl y` no longer creates a visible transient helper pane or equivalent UI flash. 2. `Ctrl y` still toggles focus correctly between the managed editor and managed sidebar, including the case where the sidebar must be opened first. 3. Sidebar freshness on `Ctrl y` focus into Yazi remains correct for the maintained Git/starship surfaces, or the work records concrete evidence that an extra explicit refresh became unnecessary and was safely deleted. 4. The chosen implementation does not add polling or broaden the sidebar cache's correctness role. 5. Focus and refresh behavior is defended by a real regression or contract test rather than a help-text or command-list assertion.
notes:
Evidence gathered for the plan:
- Current local path is `Ctrl y` in `configs/zellij/yazelix_overrides.kdl` -> `configs/zellij/scripts/toggle_editor_sidebar_focus.nu` -> `nushell/scripts/zellij_wrappers/toggle_editor_sidebar_focus.nu` -> pane-orchestrator `toggle_editor_sidebar_focus` plus `refresh_active_sidebar_yazi` when the target becomes the sidebar.
- The pane orchestrator already owns the focus decision cleanly in `rust_plugins/zellij_pane_orchestrator/src/panes.rs`; the visible glitch comes from the extra foreground wrapper pane, not from the focus policy itself.
- Zellij's plugin API supports silent background host commands via `run_command` / `run_command_with_env_variables_and_cwd`, but that requires the `RunCommand` permission. This makes a no-flash background bridge plausible without a visible `Run "nu"` pane.
- Yazi's documented plugin pub/sub surface exposes named DDS kinds such as `cd` and `tab` through `ps.sub()`. The current `sidebar-state.yazi` plugin subscribes only to `cd` and `tab`, and local Yazi source did not show a built-in DDS `focus` kind alongside those message kinds. So do not assume there is a clean Yazi focus-gain hook that can simply replace the explicit refresh.
- Because `refresh_active_sidebar_yazi` still depends on current Yazi identity lookup and `ya emit-to`, a full cleanup may overlap with `yazelix-0hm3`, but this bead should first try to delete only the visible helper pane and keep the wider ownership refactor out of scope unless it proves necessary.
close_reason: Completed: Ctrl+y now binds directly to the pane orchestrator, the transient helper-pane path is deleted, and the popup-close refresh remains the only extra Yazi refresh path.

title: Make yzx dev update activation explicit for installer vs Home Manager users
description:
`yzx dev update` currently always finishes by running `nix run .#install` unless `--no-install-runtime` is used. That conflates repo update with installer activation and is the wrong default for Home Manager users, who should reapply their Home Manager config instead of re-running the manual installer. Add an explicit activation mode so the maintainer update flow can target installer activation, Home Manager activation, or no activation at all.

Design:
Keep this delete-first. Do not add another vague boolean like `--hm` on top of the current surface. Replace the ambiguous post-update install step with an explicit activation choice, ideally something like `--activate installer|home_manager|none`. The installer mode should keep the current `nix run .#install` behavior. The Home Manager mode should skip the installer path and guide or perform the local Home Manager reapply path against the maintainer's configured Home Manager flake input. The none mode should make the current `--no-install-runtime` intent first-class. The goal is one update command that does not accidentally mix the manual installer model with the Home Manager ownership model.

Acceptance Criteria:
1. `yzx dev update` no longer assumes installer activation as the only meaningful post-update path.
2. There is an explicit supported activation mode for Home Manager users.
3. The command surface clearly distinguishes installer activation, Home Manager activation, and no activation.
4. Maintainer docs/help text explain which activation mode to use for local Home Manager testing without pushing.
close_reason: Implemented explicit yzx dev update activation modes with required --activate for real updates, Home Manager activation helpers, docs, and maintainer regressions.

title: Add a supported migration helper for manual-install to Home Manager takeover
description: Provide an explicit supported migration helper for users moving from manual flake install to Home Manager, so they do not have to guess which installer-owned artifacts to move or delete by hand. Reuse installer ownership detection from the uninstall work instead of inventing a second cleanup model.
design: Prefer one ownership engine. If the right UX is a dedicated yzx migrate-to-home-manager command, make it a thin flow over the same installer-owned artifact detection used by yzx uninstall. If a narrower uninstall submode is better, keep the command surface explicit and document exactly which paths it cleans up before Home Manager takes over. Non-goal: deleting Home Manager-managed paths or silently touching user-owned config.
acceptance_criteria: There is a supported command-driven migration path from manual install to Home Manager takeover that removes or hands off installer-owned artifacts blocking Home Manager, especially ~/.local/bin/yzx, without asking the user to guess cleanup steps. The implementation shares ownership logic with the uninstall surface instead of duplicating path rules.
notes: Keep this as the fallback/secondary path for `yazelix-isdn`, not the first design choice. The preferred takeover fix is to stop making the Home Manager path own `~/.local/bin/yzx` at all. Use this helper bead only if the ownership-boundary cleanup proves too disruptive or leaves unavoidable manual-install takeover gaps.
close_reason: Implemented via yzx home_manager prepare and shared uninstall ownership helpers.

In practice the bar can end up showing only truncated tail fragments or an effectively empty tab area, leaving the user guessing which tab is focused.

title: Add yzx uninstall --all for explicit full cleanup
description: Extend the uninstall surface with an explicit yzx uninstall --all path that removes installer-owned artifacts plus Yazelix state and managed config. Keep this as an intentional destructive path, separate from the safe default uninstall behavior. Document exactly what yzx uninstall --all deletes and keep Home Manager ownership checks in place so Yazelix does not silently fight declarative ownership.

title: Add yzx uninstall for installer-owned Yazelix artifacts
description: Add a supported uninstall command for Yazelix. The default path should remove only installer-owned artifacts such as the stable yzx shim, runtime/current, and generated desktop integration, while preserving user-managed config by default. The command should detect Home Manager ownership and refuse or warn instead of deleting paths managed by Home Manager.

title: Harden Yazelix-managed file writes with shared atomic replace helpers
description:
Imported GitHub issue #515.

## Problem

Yazelix writes several managed artifacts that users depend on immediately:

- generated configs under `~/.local/share/yazelix/configs/`
- managed files under `~/.config/yazelix/`
- copied plugin artifacts such as Zellij wasm files
- small state/cache files that are part of the managed Yazelix contract

Some of these writes are already atomic, but the repo does not use one consistent pattern everywhere. That means a runtime refresh, install, or config regeneration can still leave some managed files briefly torn, partially written, or replaced through ad-hoc logic.

This is not a request for a full transaction system. The likely problem is narrower: Yazelix needs one shared atomic replace helper for Yazelix-owned file writes, and then it should apply that helper consistently where torn writes would create real user-visible breakage.

## Proposed direction

Treat this as a hardening pass on **managed Yazelix-owned writes only**.

Goals:

- inventory the important managed write sites
- define one shared helper for atomic file replacement
- use that helper for generated configs, runtime-managed plugin copies, and other Yazelix-owned state where partial writes are unacceptable
- avoid widening this into multi-file transactions or generic filesystem abstraction

A likely helper contract is:

1. create parent directories if needed
2. write to a temporary file in the same directory
3. atomically rename into place
4. fail clearly on error

## Important boundary

This bead is **not** about automatically modifying user-owned external config outside Yazelix-owned paths. It is only about making Yazelix's own managed writes safer and more consistent.

## Acceptance criteria

- There is one shared atomic write helper for Yazelix-owned managed files instead of repeated ad-hoc temp-file logic.
- The important user-visible managed write sites use that helper consistently.
- The implementation stays narrow: atomic replace for managed artifacts, not a fake transaction layer.
- Verification shows that installs / refreshes / managed config generation still work and no managed write sites are left using weaker ad-hoc patterns without justification.
notes: 2026-04-09 implementation: added shared atomic replace helpers for Yazelix-managed text and file writes; migrated generated Zellij, Yazi, Helix, terminal, desktop-entry, shell bridge, initializer, launch-state, materialized-state, extern-bridge, upgrade-summary, restart bootstrap, config-surface copy, migration staging, and runtime plugin-copy paths to the shared helper; intentionally left maintainer-only write sites, append-only logs, and host-shell append flows outside this bead.
close_reason: Completed: Yazelix-managed file writes now use shared atomic replace helpers across the main runtime/config/state artifact paths.

description:

title: investigate session management
description: Imported GitHub issue #522.
notes:
Investigation result, 2026-04-28:
- Session management should stay Zellij-native and workspace-scoped instead of becoming a second Yazelix session database.
- The current durable owner is the pane-orchestrator active-tab session seam plus per-window config snapshots. That is the right source for tab-local workspace root, managed panes, focus/layout state, sidebar Yazi identity, and status-bus facts.
- Product command taxonomy should separate: launch/enter/restart/new window; tab/workspace navigation such as yzx warp; inspection such as yzx inspect and yzx dev inspect_session; explicit save/restore only after a resurrection contract exists.
- Do not bring back persistent_sessions as a user setting. It encourages implicit ownership across windows and conflicts with the new immutable per-window config snapshot direction.
- The next concrete session-management work is covered by yazelix-u66i for project/tab switching and by yazelix-p7qs/yazelix-l38n for explicit resurrection.
close_reason: Completed investigation. Decision: keep session management Zellij-native and workspace-scoped; route concrete work through yzx warp and explicit resurrection follow-ups.

title: Export a stable Yazelix config as a generated flake/package
description: Add an optional export path that compiles a stable Yazelix config (`yazelix.toml` + `yazelix_packs.toml`) into a generated flake/package for store-backed reuse.
design: Treat this as an optional compiled/export mode for stable configs, not as a blanket "remove devenv" rewrite. The generated output should be derived from the canonical Yazelix config contract, disposable/regenerable, and aligned with the store-backed package/runtime direction. Avoid a second hand-maintained config system or ad-hoc TOML-to-Nix duplication.
acceptance_criteria:
1. The issue is scoped as an optional export/compile path for stable configs, not a global removal of `devenv`.
2. The generated result derives from the canonical config contract rather than a second hand-maintained config system.
3. The exported output targets a store-backed flake/package shape that aligns with the existing nixpkgs package contract.
4. The generated flake/package is disposable derived output; TOML remains the editable source of truth unless a later design explicitly changes that.
5. The normal dynamic Yazelix runtime remains supported.
notes: Reframed from the vague "use the flake without devenv" wording into a narrower generator/export concept. This likely wants the config single-source work first and should stay aligned with the nixpkgs/store-backed package direction instead of inventing a third runtime model. Likely future command shape: `yzx export flake` or `yzx compile config`.
close_reason: The existing nix profile install flow already provides store-backed immutability. The acceptance criteria describe the existing runtime behavior. No compelling user story distinguishes this from normal Yazelix updates.

title: Define and reduce cross-language ownership boundaries in the runtime/workspace stack
description: Map the current hot-path behavior that crosses POSIX shell, Nushell, Rust plugins, Lua Yazi plugins, and Zellij CLI behavior, then define which language/runtime should own which category of logic going forward. Use that map to identify which seams deserve consolidation rather than letting correctness continue to span too many runtimes.
design: Treat this as an ownership-and-simplification bead, not a rewrite-everything proposal. The current stack is defensible but expensive to debug because too much user-visible behavior crosses language boundaries. Identify the long-lived stateful logic, the thin adapter logic, and the incidental bootstrap glue. Prefer moving hot-path correctness and session ownership into fewer layers while keeping the CLI UX and integration adapters intentionally thin.
acceptance_criteria: 1. There is an explicit language/runtime ownership map for CLI UX, runtime orchestration, live workspace/session state, and integration adapters. 2. The map identifies at least one high-value seam that should collapse into a stronger single owner. 3. Follow-up implementation beads become narrower and aligned with that ownership model. 4. The work informs the broader backend/Core discussion instead of competing with it.
notes:
2026-04-08 streamlining alignment:
- Keep this bead focused on cross-language hot paths, not as a blocker for every local Nushell reduction.
- Immediate Nushell-surface reduction can proceed in `doctor.nu`, `yzx/dev.nu`, `common.nu`, `config_migrations.nu`, `yazi.nu`, and `zellij_config_merger.nu` without waiting for a full language/runtime ownership map.
- The map becomes most important where correctness still crosses Nushell, Rust plugins, Lua Yazi plugins, and Zellij CLI behavior in one user-visible flow.

2026-04-09 completion:
- Added `docs/specs/cross_language_runtime_ownership.md` to map ownership across POSIX shell, Nushell, Rust plugins, Lua Yazi code, and Zellij transport.
- The map identifies the pane orchestrator as the durable owner for live workspace/session truth and keeps Lua/Yazi as an adapter layer.
close_reason: Completed

title: Make the pane orchestrator own sidebar identity and remove the sidebar cache from correctness paths
description: Stop relying on the filesystem sidebar-state cache as part of normal correctness for current-tab sidebar targeting. Move sidebar identity and related targeting knowledge behind the pane orchestrator contract so workspace retarget, reveal, refresh, and focus flows stop depending on 'last seen' Yazi cache state.
design: The current workspace/session contract is strongest where the pane orchestrator owns per-tab truth, and weakest where Yazi identity still leaks through cache files. Extend the plugin protocol or state model so the orchestrator can answer the sidebar-targeting questions Nushell actually needs. Keep any remaining filesystem cache only as integration telemetry, bootstrap hinting, or debugging aid, not as a correctness dependency for active-tab operations.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Active-tab sidebar targeting no longer depends on scanning ~/.local/share/yazelix/state/yazi/sidebar for correctness. 2. Workspace-retarget and reveal flows use plugin-owned sidebar identity or an equivalent explicit contract. 3. Cross-tab contamination risk from stale cache files is materially reduced. 4. If a cache remains, its reduced role is documented explicitly.
notes: Pipeline stage 2A. This is the first hot-path workspace ownership move: sidebar identity must stop depending on cache files before broader workspace targeting truth can move behind the pane orchestrator.
close_reason: Completed: the pane orchestrator now stores sidebar Yazi identity keyed by the managed sidebar pane id, Nushell sidebar targeting queries that plugin-owned state instead of scanning sidebar cache files, the cache is documented as telemetry only, and the synced pane-orchestrator wasm carries the new contract.

title: Split restart rebuild requirement from missing launch-profile evidence
description:
`yzx restart` can rebuild the environment twice in one invocation. The first rebuild is the explicit restart-owned `devenv build shell` pass. The second rebuild happens when the subsequent `yzx launch --force-reenter` still sees `needs_refresh = true` only because a verified launch profile has not been recorded yet, even though the environment was already rebuilt moments earlier.

This shows that Yazelix is conflating two different conditions:
- rebuild-required input drift
- missing or unverified reusable launch-profile evidence

Those conditions should not both force a rebuild. A missing verified launch profile should force a slower shell-entry path, not a second `devenv build shell` for the same inputs.
design: Keep this delete-first. Do not paper over the symptom with restart-only flags unless the broader state model remains coherent. The likely direction is to split config-state reporting so callers can distinguish `rebuild required` from `launch profile missing`, then make restart/launch/startup rebuild only on real input drift. Preserve the current ability to fall back from fast profile activation to shell entry when reusable launch evidence is absent.
acceptance_criteria: 1. `yzx restart` performs at most one rebuild for one unchanged config/input state transition. 2. Missing verified launch-profile evidence no longer forces a rebuild by itself after an explicit successful rebuild. 3. Launch and startup paths still fall back cleanly to shell entry when reusable launch-profile evidence is missing. 4. A focused regression test defends the exact double-rebuild failure mode seen in practice.
notes:
2026-04-20 tracker hygiene: moved out of in_progress. There is no active dirty tree or recent execution on this lane; the restart-vs-launch-profile double-rebuild bug still needs a focused reproduction, state-model split, and regression test before this should be claimed again.
2026-04-21 investigation: this bead describes a backend-era failure mode that no longer exists in the live v15 startup/restart path. Current `yzx restart` in `nushell/scripts/core/yzx_session.nu` just relaunches through the stable owner wrapper and then kills the old Zellij session; it does not run a restart-owned `devenv build shell` pass. Current `yzx launch` in `nushell/scripts/yzx/launch.nu` and `nushell/scripts/core/launch_yazelix.nu` only consumes `config_state.needs_refresh` to decide whether the new terminal should start with a reload-safe detached prefix. The actual startup path in `nushell/scripts/core/start_yazelix_inner.nu` always goes through generated-state materialization (`regenerate_runtime_configs`) rather than cached launch-profile reuse. There is no live `launch_state.nu`, no live `force-reenter` path, and no live `devenv build shell` restart flow in `nushell/scripts/` anymore; remaining references are historical specs/tests only. Current branch contract already states this explicitly in `docs/specs/v15_trimmed_runtime_contract.md`: generated-state repair does not rebuild or reuse a cached backend launch profile. Verified with targeted tests: `test_desktop_fast_path_uses_direct_host_terminal_during_reload_instead_of_stale_wrapper`, `test_launch_here_path_uses_requested_directory_for_nonpersistent_sessions`, and `test_home_manager_profile_restart_uses_owner_wrapper_without_manual_surfaces`.
close_reason: Obsolete on the trimmed v15 branch: the old launch-profile/devenv restart architecture was deleted, so the reported double-rebuild path no longer exists in the live restart/launch/startup code

title: Replace string-built Zellij runtime wrapper commands with direct runtime script execution
description: The Zellij wrapper layer under configs/zellij/scripts currently relies on dynamic Nushell source strings plus `nu -c` to enter runtime-owned logic. This works, but it is stringly typed, harder to test, and duplicates wrapper boilerplate.
design: Prefer a delete-first refactor. Add a shared helper that executes runtime-owned Nushell scripts directly by relative runtime path, move wrapper logic into real runtime leaf scripts, and keep tracked/generated Zellij wrapper files thin. Scope this narrowly to wrappers under configs/zellij/scripts that currently build command strings through run_runtime_nu_command. Do not widen into unrelated command-surface changes.
acceptance_criteria: 1. At least one current string-built Zellij wrapper path is replaced with direct runtime script execution through a shared helper. 2. The new pattern is capable of covering wrappers that currently build `use ...; ...` strings for runtime entry. 3. The converted wrapper surface is materially thinner or fewer lines overall for the touched paths. 4. Existing high-signal wrapper regressions still pass, and no live plugin/runtime behavior regresses.
close_reason: Completed/verified: Zellij script wrappers now use the shared run_runtime_nu_script helper to execute runtime-owned wrapper scripts directly, and no run_runtime_nu_command or zellij-wrapper nu -c string entrypoints remain.

title: Add a maintainer refresh path for vendored Yazi plugins
description: Yazelix vendors Yazi plugin code from upstream repositories, but it currently lacks a clear maintainer refresh path comparable to the vendored zjstatus update flow. Add a deterministic maintainer update path so vendored plugin drift is explicit and reproducible.
design: Treat vendored Yazi plugin refresh as a source-management problem, not a user runtime feature. Inventory which shipped plugins come from which upstreams: git.yazi is from the yazi-rs/plugins monorepo, while starship.yazi and lazygit.yazi are separate upstream repositories. Add an explicit source map that records the upstream repo, pinned revision, source path, and target vendored path for each intentionally shipped plugin. Then add one maintainer refresh command that pulls those sources into configs/yazi/plugins/, preserving any clearly-marked Yazelix-local patches instead of relying on manual copy steps or user-side ya pkg resolution. Keep the runtime deterministic: the refresh happens in the repo, maintainers review the vendored diff, and end users just consume the committed plugin code.
acceptance_criteria: 1. The repo has an explicit source-of-truth mapping for each vendored upstream Yazi plugin Yazelix ships. 2. Maintainers can run one documented command to refresh vendored Yazi plugins from those pinned upstream sources. 3. The refresh path makes it explicit which plugins come from yazi-rs/plugins versus separate upstream repositories. 4. The resulting vendored files under configs/yazi/plugins/ are reproducible and do not depend on user-side ya pkg resolution or network access. 5. Focused maintainer validation or regression coverage defends the source-map/update seam so vendored plugin drift is easier to detect.
close_reason: Completed: added a pinned source map for vendored Yazi plugin runtime files, a refresh helper with overlay patch support and dirty-worktree protection, integrated it into yzx dev update, and added a maintainer regression. The first slice intentionally manages runtime files (main.lua) rather than full README synchronization.

title: Keep runtime-project shell artifacts aligned with recorded launch state
description:
Problem:
The runtime-owned profile resolver now correctly prefers recorded launch state over the runtime project shell artifact, but ~/.local/share/yazelix/runtime/project/.devenv/gc/shell can still lag behind the latest built profile after refresh. That means user-facing behavior is fixed by precedence, while one materialized artifact remains stale and misleading.

Design:
Decide the clean contract for the runtime-project shell artifact instead of continuing to tolerate drift. Either keep the runtime-project shell artifact aligned with the latest built profile whenever refresh or rebuild succeeds, or explicitly demote that artifact to bootstrap-only/internal status and remove remaining resolver or helper dependence on it where possible. Prefer delete-first reasoning: if the stale artifact does not need to participate in runtime-owned truth, stop treating it as meaningful state rather than layering more precedence hacks on top.

Acceptance Criteria:
1. The repo has an explicit contract for how ~/.local/share/yazelix/runtime/project/.devenv/gc/shell relates to the latest recorded launch profile after refresh/build flows.
2. Refresh/build flows either keep that artifact aligned with the fresh built profile, or the code stops depending on it as runtime-owned truth outside the narrow place that truly needs it.
3. Runtime-owned profile resolution, refresh, and desktop launch no longer rely on stale runtime-project shell artifacts to behave correctly.
4. Regression coverage or validators defend the chosen contract so this drift does not silently return.
close_reason: Investigation showed runtime-project .devenv profile artifacts are optional helper state, not canonical launch truth. Spec and maintainer regression now defend build-shell output plus recorded launch state as the authoritative reusable-profile contract.

title: Investigate remaining duplicate shellhook contexts on cold profiled startup
description: The Yazelix-owned refreshed startup path no longer re-enters `devenv shell` after rebuild, but a truly cold `yzx dev profile --cold --clear-cache` run still shows two shellhook PID contexts. Warm startup does not show this duplication. This follow-up tracks the remaining deeper investigation separately from the completed refreshed-startup cleanup.
design: Treat this as a narrower cold-path investigation, not a redo of the refreshed startup ownership cleanup. The remaining duplicate shellhook contexts survive after the rebuilt-profile activation simplification, so first determine whether they come from upstream `devenv build shell` behavior, a profiling harness interaction, or another cold-only shell activation boundary. Prefer an explicit explanation and the smallest correct fix over more startup-path branching.
acceptance_criteria: 1. The remaining duplicate shellhook contexts on truly cold profiled startup have a concrete root-cause explanation. 2. If Yazelix owns the duplication, remove or narrow it without regressing visible rebuild behavior or startup correctness. 3. If the duplication is upstream or intentionally unavoidable, document that contract clearly in the bead and keep the startup profile output interpretable. 4. Warm startup remains unchanged and the existing refreshed-startup regression continues to pass.
notes: Root cause was not an unavoidable duplicate shellhook entry. The refreshed-startup path was resolving runtime-owned launch state to the .devenv/gc/shell script path itself instead of extracting the embedded DEVENV_PROFILE. Because direct activation was using a devenv-shell script path rather than the real devenv-profile, cold profiling and refreshed startup behavior became misleading. Fix: resolve shell-script candidates through readlink first, extract the embedded profile path robustly, and defend it with a workspace regression that uses a real .devenv/gc/shell fixture. After reinstalling the runtime, cold yzx dev profile --cold --clear-cache no longer records shellhook contexts at all; it goes straight from bootstrap.devenv.build_shell into inner startup. Warm profiling remains clean.
close_reason: Fixed runtime-owned profile resolution; cold startup profiling no longer shows duplicate shellhook contexts

title: Move bundled Yazi asset sync out of warm startup
description:
Bundled Yazi plugins, flavors, and the managed sidebar Starship config are currently refreshed during startup-managed Yazi generation. Those files are largely static for a given runtime, so paying that copy/sync cost on the common warm path is likely the wrong boundary.

This follow-up should evaluate moving bundled Yazi asset materialization to install/refresh/runtime-change ownership instead of launch-time ownership.
design:
Reason from first principles. The common warm path should only do work that is truly dynamic for that launch. Runtime-owned Yazi assets are mostly static for a given installed runtime, so they should not be recopied on every startup unless there is a strong correctness reason. Prefer a design that deletes launch-time bundled-asset sync instead of adding more cache metadata or broad invalidation logic.

Preserve fail-fast behavior and clear ownership boundaries between runtime-owned assets and user-owned Yazi config.
acceptance_criteria:
1. The chosen design makes explicit which Yazi files are runtime-owned static assets versus per-launch dynamic generated config.
2. Warm startup no longer recopies unchanged bundled Yazi assets on the common path.
3. Runtime updates, install, or explicit refresh still materialize the needed bundled Yazi assets correctly.
4. A focused regression defends the new ownership boundary without relying on broad cache metadata.
notes: Implemented the warm-start boundary as a guarded static-asset sync instead of a new cache layer. generate_merged_yazi_config now accepts --sync-static-assets; regenerate_runtime_configs forces that flag when config/runtime materialized state is stale, while warm current-state regeneration skips static Yazi plugin/flavor/starship recopy unless the copied asset tree is missing required runtime-owned files. The same wrapper now passes the active get_yazelix_state_dir()/configs/zellij path into generate_merged_zellij_config so generated-state repair honors explicit Yazelix state roots. Extended the existing bundled-plugin regression to prove runtime placeholders still render, warm materialization does not recopy static Yazi assets, and missing bundled plugin files self-heal. Verified: validate_syntax.nu, generated-config canonical lane, full yzx default suite 90/90, validate_default_test_count_budget.nu, validate_default_test_traceability.nu, git diff --check.
close_reason: Moved warm startup to guarded static Yazi asset sync and verified default suite.

title: Optimize Yazi config generation on warm startup
description:
The structured startup profiler now shows warm current-terminal startup is dominated by Yazi config generation after the Zellij warm-state reuse fix. This should be the next delete-first warm-start optimization slice.

Design:
Keep this delete-first. First identify which parts of managed Yazi config generation are unnecessarily redone on every warm startup and which generated artifacts can be safely reused without weakening config correctness. Prefer deleting redundant regeneration or overly broad invalidation before micro-optimizing string processing. Preserve fail-fast behavior and managed-config correctness.

Acceptance Criteria:
1. The current bottleneck inside inner.generate_yazi_config is broken down enough to show which substeps dominate.
2. Warm startup avoids clearly redundant Yazi config work when inputs have not changed.
3. The change does not make generated Yazi state stale across real config/runtime changes.
4. A focused regression or profiling-oriented check defends the intended reuse or invalidation boundary.
notes:
Attempted two delete-first optimization shapes and reverted both.

Findings:
- Full-surface generation reuse with metadata/fingerprints reduced warm generate_merged_yazi_config by roughly 140ms, but added substantial invalidation complexity around config surfaces, bundled-vs-user plugins, and random-theme behavior.
- A smaller boundary that only tried to avoid recopying unchanged bundled Yazi assets was simpler, but did not produce a meaningful end-to-end win and still added nontrivial logic.
- The structured profiler showed Yazi is a warm-path chunk, but not large enough to justify the attempted caching machinery in its current form.

Current decision:
- Optimization code was reverted.
- Bead stays open because warm Yazi generation is still one of the larger warm-start steps, but the acceptable solution bar is now higher: materially smaller state surface, clearer correctness story, or a meaningfully larger win.

Measured context from this failed pass:
- Baseline warm inner.generate_yazi_config is roughly 300ms on maintainer hardware.
- The reverted full-surface cache brought warm direct generation down by about 140ms, but at too high a complexity cost.

Next likely directions to evaluate:
- Push more work into Yazi runtime/plugin behavior so startup does not need to rewrite as much managed state.
- Identify one narrower generated artifact with a naturally safe idempotence boundary instead of caching the whole surface.
- Prefer bigger warm-start wins elsewhere before revisiting this lane.
2026-04-13 scope decision: do not implement this now. User wants to avoid optimizing this surface before the Rust migration matrix, because Yazi config generation may be rewritten or substantially reshaped later. Keep as a deferred candidate, not active pre-Rust trimming work.
2026-04-20 Zellij Rust materialization verification reran the generated-config canonical suite. All Zellij checks passed, but test_generate_merged_yazi_config_renders_runtime_placeholders_in_plugins still failed because the warm bundled-asset sentinel was removed on a no-op plan path. Treat this as follow-up under the existing warm Yazi optimization/reuse bead, not as a blocker for the Zellij owner deletion.
close_reason: Completed

title: Profile desktop and managed-terminal startup as first-class scenarios
description:
yazelix-3n1 upgraded the current-terminal profiling surface, but desktop launch and managed new-window launch remain distinct user-felt startup paths with their own prelaunch logic, wrapper repair behavior, and terminal-selection contracts. They need first-class profiling scenarios instead of being inferred from the enter path.

Design:
Extend the profiling surface carefully instead of creating a second profiler. Add explicit scenario coverage for desktop launch and managed new-window launch so reports can attribute time to prelaunch work, visible bootstrap paths, terminal-wrapper repair, and the transition into the actual Yazelix session. Keep the same structured report schema and avoid speculative instrumentation that does not correspond to real ownership boundaries.

Acceptance Criteria:
1. The profiling surface can produce structured reports for desktop or managed new-window startup, not only current-terminal entry. 2. The measured phases include any prelaunch work that users actually wait on in those flows. 3. Reports stay comparable with the existing current-terminal profiling schema. 4. Documentation explains which scenarios are covered and how to invoke them.
notes:
opus 4.6 review:

CRITICAL BUG — profiler invocation pattern is wrong for both new scenarios.

run_desktop_profile_command runs:
  ^$nu_bin desktop.nu "desktop launch"

run_launch_profile_command runs:
  ^$nu_bin launch.nu "yzx" "launch"

But desktop.nu has export def "yzx desktop launch" (no def main), and launch.nu has export def "yzx launch" (no def main). Running these as direct scripts with arguments will NOT dispatch to the exported commands. Nushell only dispatches to def main or export def main for script execution.

The production path in yzx_cli.sh uses a fundamentally different pattern:
  nu -c "use desktop.nu *; yzx desktop launch"

The test code also uses this pattern:
  ^nu -c $"use \"($desktop_script)\" *; yzx desktop launch" | complete

The profiler commands would load the scripts, define all functions, and exit without executing any profiled code. The report would have no steps or fail on report loading.

Fix: change both profiler commands to use the nu -c "use ... *; command" invocation pattern matching the production dispatcher and tests.

OTHER ISSUES:

2. Dead code in run_desktop_profile_command — the variable start_script is defined as the path to core/start_yazelix.nu but never used. Copy-pasted from run_dev_profile_harness which does use it.

3. Inconsistent env key lists — launch.nu propagate_profile_env propagates 6 keys (missing YAZELIX_SHELLHOOK_SKIP_WELCOME). launch_yazelix.nu launch_terminal_candidates propagates 7 keys (includes YAZELIX_SHELLHOOK_SKIP_WELCOME). Not a functional bug right now since the var propagates via process env inheritance, but an inconsistency that could bite if the architecture changes to explicit env blocks. Should either add the missing key to launch.nu or extract both lists into a shared constant.

4. No tests added — the implementation brief says to add focused tests defending scenario selection and the presence of desktop/launch-specific profile steps in structured output. None were added.

5. No documentation changes — acceptance criterion 4 requires documenting which scenarios are covered and how to invoke them. No docs were updated.

WHAT IS GOOD:

- The instrumentation approach is correct — profile_startup_step wrapping at ownership boundaries users actually wait on, not speculative internals.
- Step names are well-chosen: resolve_runtime_dir, build_launch_env, fast_path_handoff, parse_config, terminal_handoff, compute_config_state, validate_working_dir, resolve_terminals, generate_terminal_configs, reroll_ghostty_cursor, launch_terminal. These map cleanly to real wait phases.
- Profile env propagation through launch_terminal_candidates in launch_yazelix.nu is correctly implemented with the explicit key list.
- The desktop_fast_path vs launch component distinction in launch_yazelix.nu correctly attributes steps to the right scenario.
- Scenario naming (desktop_launch, managed_launch) is clear and compatible with the existing schema.
- The --desktop, --launch, --terminal, --verbose flag additions to yzx dev profile are natural extensions of the existing interface.
- Correct IN_YAZELIX_SHELL guard on both new profiler commands.
- Normal non-profile launch paths are behaviorally unchanged — profile_startup_step is a no-op when profiling env is absent.

VERDICT:

Do not ship as-is. The invocation bug means both new scenarios produce no useful data. Fix the invocation to use nu -c "use ... *; command" pattern, remove the dead start_script variable, align the env key lists, add tests, and update docs.
2026-04-18 implementation fix note:

Reviewed the first implementation and found two blockers: `yzx dev profile --desktop`/`--launch` invoked leaf modules as plain script files, so Nushell loaded them and exited without running `yzx desktop launch` or `yzx launch`; and detached terminal profiling rendered the report before the spawned startup path had necessarily written `inner.zellij_handoff_ready`.

Fix shape:
- `yzx dev profile --desktop` and `--launch` now invoke leaf commands via `nu -c 'use <module> *; yzx ...'`, matching the stable CLI wrapper command model.
- Desktop, launch, and launch_yazelix now share startup-profile env propagation, including the shellhook skip flag, so child terminal startup writes into the same JSONL report.
- The profile harness waits boundedly for `inner.zellij_handoff_ready` before summarizing detached desktop/managed launch reports.
- `terminal_launcher.nu` records `terminal_launcher.detached_launch_probe` around the spawn/probe wait and early-death capture seam.
- Docs now list `--desktop`, `--launch`, and `--terminal` profiling scenarios.

Defended by focused maintainer tests for desktop/launch profiler invocation and handoff waiting, terminal-launcher probe instrumentation, full maintainer lane, workspace canonical lane, syntax validation, traceability validation, upgrade-contract validation, nu-lint, and `git diff --check`.
2026-04-18 spec note: added live spec docs/specs/startup_profile_scenarios.md and listed it in docs/specs/spec_inventory.md so the desktop/managed-launch profile scenario contract is durable for the Rust rewrite baseline.

Open a new terminal outside Yazelix and run: yzx dev profile --desktop /  from  no longer requires a writable repo checkout. Added a maintainer regression covering the installed-runtime-outside-repo path and rebuilt the local Nix profile runtime for manual retest.
2026-04-18 runtime fallback fix note (corrected):
- `yzx dev profile` now resolves its source root from either the active repo checkout or the installed Yazelix runtime.
- This specifically fixes running `yzx dev profile --desktop` or `yzx dev profile --launch --terminal ghostty` from `~` with an installed local runtime.
- Added a maintainer regression for the installed-runtime-outside-repo path.
- Rebuilt the local Nix profile runtime after the fix for manual user retest.
close_reason: Completed: startup profiling now works from repo checkouts and installed local runtimes, desktop and managed launch scenarios invoke the real leaf commands, detached launch waits for inner startup completion before summarizing, detached terminal probe timing is recorded, and docs/spec/tests cover the scenario contract.

title: Explain and reduce duplicate shellhook entry during cold startup
description:
The structured cold-start report from yazelix-3n1 shows two distinct shellhook process invocations during shell entry. That may be architecturally justified, but today it is ambiguous and likely contributes avoidable cold-start cost.

Design:
Treat this as a startup-ownership and process-boundary investigation first. Determine why two separate environment.nu shellhook processes run during the cold path, which one is strictly required, and whether one can be deleted or narrowed. Prefer simplifying the flow over adding profiler-only complexity. If both invocations are truly required, document the contract and keep the profiler context clear enough to distinguish them.

Acceptance Criteria:
1. The cold startup path has an explicit explanation for each shellhook process invocation. 2. Unnecessary duplicate shellhook entry is removed if possible; otherwise the surviving duplication is deliberately justified. 3. The startup profile output remains clear about which shellhook process is being timed. 4. Any cleanup does not regress visible rebuild behavior, setup correctness, or noninteractive launch flows.
notes: Completed slice: refreshed startup no longer re-enters `devenv shell` after rebuild. `start_yazelix.nu` now rebuilds once, activates the fresh profile directly, and skips replaying `environment.nu` on that refreshed path. A new default-lane regression defends that ownership boundary. The remaining cold duplicate shellhook contexts were split into follow-up `yazelix-rqz4`, because they survive after this cleanup and appear to come from a deeper cold-path or upstream `devenv` interaction rather than the old Yazelix re-entry path.
close_reason: Completed refreshed-startup cleanup; remaining cold duplicate tracking moved to yazelix-rqz4

title: Optimize Zellij config generation on warm startup
description:
The new structured startup profiler shows that warm current-terminal startup is dominated by Zellij config generation in start_yazelix_inner.nu, typically around 2.9s to 3.3s on the maintainer machine. That makes this the highest-value warm-start performance follow-up after yazelix-3n1.

Design:
Keep this delete-first. First identify which parts of merged Zellij config generation are unnecessarily redone on every warm startup and which artifacts can be reused safely without weakening config correctness. Prefer deleting redundant regeneration or broad invalidation before micro-optimizing string processing. Preserve fail-fast behavior and managed-config correctness.

Acceptance Criteria:
1. The current bottleneck inside inner.generate_zellij_config is broken down enough to show which substeps dominate. 2. Warm startup avoids clearly redundant Zellij config work when inputs have not changed. 3. The change does not make generated Zellij state stale across real config/runtime changes. 4. A focused regression or profiling-oriented check defends the intended reuse or invalidation boundary.

title: Re-home package builder helper Nix files out of the repo root
description: Move root-level package-builder helper files that are implementation details rather than public entrypoints into packaging/, while keeping the top-level flake and the top-level package front doors stable.
design: Treat the helper builders as packaging internals. The public front doors can remain at the root: flake.nix, yazelix_package.nix, and yazelix_runtime_package.nix. The internal builder helpers such as locked_devenv_package.nix, mk_runtime_tree.nix, and mk_yazelix_package.nix do not need top-level discoverability and make the root harder to scan. Re-home them under packaging/ and update imports, validators, and packaging docs accordingly. Prefer a narrow move with no behavior change.
acceptance_criteria: 1. The internal package-builder helper Nix files no longer live at the repo root. 2. The top-level flake and package front doors still evaluate through the new locations. 3. Existing validators or packaging references that mention the old paths are updated. 4. The move reduces root-level clutter without changing the public install/package entrypoints. 5. Verification includes at least one package/validator check, not just path edits.
notes:
Completed as a narrow packaging-internal re-home.

Moved out of the repo root:
- locked_devenv_package.nix -> packaging/locked_devenv_package.nix
- mk_runtime_tree.nix -> packaging/mk_runtime_tree.nix
- mk_yazelix_package.nix -> packaging/mk_yazelix_package.nix

Kept at the repo root as public front doors:
- flake.nix
- yazelix_package.nix
- yazelix_runtime_package.nix

Updated callers and contract checks:
- flake imports
- top-level package front doors
- packaging/nixpkgs/yazelix_package.nix
- validate_installed_runtime_contract.nu

Verification:
- nix flake show --json >/dev/null
- nu nushell/scripts/dev/validate_installed_runtime_contract.nu
close_reason: Completed: package-builder helper Nix files now live under packaging/ while the public top-level flake and package front doors stay stable.

title: Organize the repository root and clarify root-level ownership
description: Audit the repository root and make root-level ownership legible. The goal is not cosmetic churn; it is to decide which files truly belong at the repo root, which should move under docs/ or a more specific subsystem directory, and which root artifacts need explicit documentation because they are part of the product or maintainer contract.
design: Treat the repo root as a high-signal surface. Root-level files should exist only when they are one of: repository entrypoints, packaging/build metadata, maintainer workflow files, user-facing discovery docs, or source-of-truth config/templates that genuinely belong at the top level. Everything else should either move under a more specific directory or be documented as intentionally root-level. Prefer delete-first reasoning over aesthetic reshuffling: remove stale or redundant root artifacts, group related files when a clearer home exists, and avoid broad moves that break discoverability without a real ownership win.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Inventory the current root-level files and classify them into intentional root ownership, should-move, or should-delete/document categories. 2. At least one real root-level ambiguity, redundancy, or stale artifact is either removed or re-homed. 3. The remaining root-level surfaces have a clearer ownership story consistent with current Yazelix architecture and maintainer workflow. 4. Any user-facing or maintainer-facing moves are reflected in docs or workflow references. 5. Leave behind a short summary of what root artifacts are intentionally kept and why.
notes:
Completed with a conservative first slice focused on root discoverability rather than broad reshuffling.

Removed:
- deleted the unreferenced docs_explore/ root directory and its stale exploratory Helix keybinding reference because current maintained guidance already lives under docs/keybindings.md and docs/helix_keybindings.md.

Intentionally kept at root:
- entrypoint/user-facing docs: README.md, CHANGELOG.md, AGENTS.md, LICENSE, CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md
- build and packaging entrypoints: flake.nix, devenv.nix, devenv.yaml, flake.lock, devenv.lock, locked_devenv_package.nix, mk_runtime_tree.nix, mk_yazelix_package.nix, yazelix_package.nix, yazelix_runtime_package.nix
- maintainer workflow/config: .pre-commit-config.yaml, .taplo.toml, .nu-lint.toml, .github/, .beads/
- source-of-truth runtime templates: yazelix_default.toml, yazelix_packs_default.toml

Documentation follow-up:
- added a short root-ownership rule to docs/contributing.md
- repaired nearby stale docs/specs path references so maintainer workflow docs no longer point at a local config path
close_reason: Completed a conservative root-ownership cleanup slice: removed the stale docs_explore/ root subtree and documented what intentionally belongs at the repo root.

title: Detached Yazelix sessions can accumulate and keep consuming CPU after windows close
design: Investigate why Yazelix/Zellij sessions can remain alive after their terminal windows are closed and continue consuming CPU while detached. Use the observed failure mode as the anchor: many long-lived zellij --server processes under /run/user/1000/zellij/contract_version_1 with zero attached clients, each burning nontrivial CPU. Determine whether the bug is in Yazelix launch/restart/session naming policy, desktop/current-terminal ownership, Zellij client detach behavior, or plugin/session lifecycle. Prefer a real lifecycle fix over cleanup-on-start band-aids.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Reproduce or explain how detached Yazelix sessions survive window closure and keep consuming CPU. 2. Identify whether the root cause is Yazelix-managed session lifecycle, Zellij behavior, or both. 3. If a Yazelix fix is made, closing a window or otherwise ending the last client for a non-persistent Yazelix session should not leave a hot detached server behind. 4. The fix must not regress intentional persistent-session behavior. 5. Verification should include a concrete check on session count or attached-client state, not just a code-path unit test.
notes:
Cross-window/session-semantics anchor:
- In the default non-persistent contract, closing the last client for a Yazelix window should not leave a hot detached Zellij server behind.
- In persistent-session mode, detached-server survival may be intentional depending on the named-session contract.
- Evaluate fixes against `yazelix-1zti` and `yazelix-vxrb` so cleanup logic does not erase intentional persistence.

Root cause found:
- Yazelix enables `session_serialization true`, strips user `on_force_close` from the merged Zellij base config, and previously failed to inject its own replacement.
- That left default non-persistent sessions inheriting Zellij's detach-on-terminal-close behavior, which matches the observed zero-client detached servers.

Fix direction:
- Generate `on_force_close "quit"` for default non-persistent sessions.
- Generate `on_force_close "detach"` for persistent sessions.

Verification:
- generated-config regression for both session modes
- concrete runtime probe: with a PTY-backed `zellij attach -c <session>`, `on_force_close "quit"` yielded `before=1 after=0`, while `on_force_close "detach"` yielded `before=1 after=1`
close_reason: Fixed: non-persistent sessions now generate on_force_close=quit while persistent sessions keep detach semantics, eliminating the observed default-mode zero-client session leak.

title: Define yzx command categories and palette eligibility
design: Write a small spec that classifies the public yzx command surface into practical UX categories for the command palette. The goal is not to restate backend-coupling buckets; it is to define which commands are palette-eligible by default, which are explicitly excluded, and how categories drive grouping and labels. Prefer broad inclusion of user-facing commands, with explicit exclusions for pane-level and tab-level commands and any commands whose interaction model is not suitable for palette dispatch.
acceptance_criteria: 1. The spec defines concrete command categories for the public yzx surface with palette-eligibility rules. 2. The spec makes explicit that pane-scoped and tab-scoped commands are not normal palette items. 3. The spec explains how categories differ from backend-coupling buckets and how the two models relate. 4. The spec names the current command families or concrete commands that belong in each category. 5. The spec includes traceability back to yazelix-qrnq and validation through the normal spec validator.
close_reason: Specified yzx command categories and palette eligibility in docs/specs/yzx_command_palette_categories.md and validated specs.

title: Split yzx menu from config/edit/open surfaces and thin the command palette
description: The current yzx menu module carries too many unrelated command families. It should be thinned so the command palette UI and popup behavior are separated from config/edit/open/reset/migrate implementations.
design: Keep yzx menu as the palette and popup wrapper only, but stop treating palette eligibility as a tiny hand-filtered subset. Prefer a small explicit command-category model that allows most user-facing yzx commands into the palette by default while excluding commands whose semantics are inherently pane-scoped, tab-scoped, or otherwise unsuitable for palette dispatch. Move config/edit/open/reset/migrate implementations out of menu.nu into dedicated leaf modules. Prefer one declarative metadata source for grouping, labels, and palette eligibility instead of mining help output and then layering ad hoc filters.
acceptance_criteria: 1. yzx menu remains the palette surface, but config/edit/open/reset/migrate implementations no longer live in nushell/scripts/yzx/menu.nu. 2. Palette eligibility is driven by explicit command categories or metadata rather than a fragile manual name filter. 3. Most user-facing yzx commands are eligible by default, while pane-scoped and tab-scoped commands are explicitly excluded or handled outside the palette contract. 4. Popup and normal menu flows keep working without regressing the lightweight dispatcher path. 5. Focused regressions defend the moved command surfaces or palette dispatch seam without weak help-output tests. 6. Net result is a meaningfully smaller and less mixed menu.nu.
close_reason: Split config/edit/open ownership out of menu.nu, added explicit palette categories and eligibility, and kept menu as a thinner palette wrapper.
